name: The SOURCE
description:
The SOURCE is designed to provide resources and support for registered student organizations on the campus of the University of Alabama. Currently there are more than 600 registered student organizations at the Capstone.

The SOURCE is also the place for students to learn how to get involved with registered student organizations at UA. 

Our team is dedicated to three core principles, our ABCs: we advocate for effective policies, open dialogue, and genuine engagement on behalf of student organizations; we build a strong community of student organizations by developing dynamic leaders, providing practical and accessible resources and  facilitating widespread and relevant communication; finally, we connect students to involvement opportunities and foster collaboration among student organizations.
summary: The SOURCE is designed to provide resources and support for registered student organizations on the campus of the University of Alabama. Currently there are more than 600 registered student organizations at the Capstone.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
summary: BGSA was created for the enhancement of the graduate student experience for Black students and minorities at the University of Alabama.

name: African Students Association
description: The African Students Association (ASA) at The University of Alabama is a student body formed with the following objectives: fostering fraternal relationships through friendly and mutual support among African students; bettering relationships and understanding among fellow African students; promoting greater understanding of African traditions; fostering unity among students regardless of race, ethnicity, or religious orientation; encouraging and supporting student involvement on the UA campus; welcoming and embracing all incoming African students and students interested in African affairs; and last but not the least, to work closely with other organizations, staff, and faculty to promote scholarship, fellowship, and service through diversity and internationalism.
summary: African, Fraternal, African Traditions, African Culture, African Diaspora

name: Afro American Gospel Choir
description: We are a special interest organization on the campus of The University of Alabama in Tuscaloosa, Alabama. Our members all share a common enjoyment for ministry and serving God through song and dance. Our organization is known world wide for its creative ministry and for the past four years we have participated in and won first place in the large choir division at the National Baptist Student Union Retreat. We travel throughout the southeast representing The University of Alabama as a recruitment resource and also representing our diverse communities as a form of outreach
summary: Choir, Gospel, Service, Music, Dance

name: Aces High Booster Club
description: The Aces High Booster Club’s mission is to support the Cadets of Air Force ROTC Detachment 010 in their endeavor to become Second Lieutenants in the United States Air Force. Aces High Booster Club will perform a variety of functions including, but not limited to: procurement of FAC funds, reserving university spaces, and reaching out to the UA community. It is important to note that Aces High Booster Club is in no way affiliated with The United States Air Force, Air Force ROTC, or Detachment 010. It is simply a support organization in accordance with Holm Center Instruction 51-601.
summary: A organization dedicated to improving and bettering the lives of military cadets on UA's campus.

name: Alabama Academic Quiz Bowl Team
description: The Alabama Academic Quiz Bowl Team is an organization dedicated to the play of Quiz Bowl. Quiz bowl is an academic quiz competition where teams of four students test their knowledge against other schools. Wednesday/Thursday practices and 4-6 Saturday tournaments per semester held at other Universities.
summary: Quiz bowl is an academic quiz competition where teams of four students test their knowledge against other schools. Wednesday/Thursday practices and 4-6 Saturday tournaments per semester held at other Universities.

name: Secular Student Alliance
description: The Secular Student Alliance empowers secular students to proudly express their identity, build welcoming communities, promote secular values, and set a course for lifelong activism. We work to promote Secular values through on campus connections, activism and overall have a welcoming community on University of Alabamas campus. Secular Student alliance is welcoming to all students and staff, regardless of religious, gender, race or sexual identity.
summary: The Secular Student Alliance is an organization that works to create a society in which the ideals of scientific rationality, secularism, and human-based ethics flourish at the University of Alabama.

name: Alabama Badminton Club
description: Join our vibrant community of badminton enthusiasts! Whether you're a beginner or an experienced player, our club offers a friendly and competitive environment to improve your skills, stay active, and connect with fellow players. We organize regular practice sessions and club events to make badminton both fun and engaging. Come be a part of our growing club and enjoy the game with us!

name: Alabama Club Baseball
description: The Club baseball team is an official student organization sanctioned by both the University and University Recreation. It is an excellent opportunity for college students that did not make a college varsity team to extend their baseball careers. More importantly, it offers an opportunity for the members to be a part of an organization that is active in the community with service and involvement with a purpose. These baseball club members are true student-athletes, with academics and community coming first, and baseball coming second. The University of Alabama Club Baseball Team is also sanctioned by the National Club Baseball Association (clubbaseball.org). This club is competitive and has the opportunity each year to earn a spot in the National Club Baseball World Series. If you want to learn more about the team, visit our website, www.bamaclubbaseball.weebly.com.
summary: Baseball, athletics, club sports, club, sport, sports, ncba, collegiate club baseball, national

Additionally, we hold fundraisers and workshops for the benefit of the engineering program.

name: Best Buddies UA
description: Welcome! Best Buddies is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.The Best Buddies chapter at the University of Alabama matches people with intellectual and developmental disabilities with college students and creates one-to-one friendships between them. In the past, individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities have not had the opportunity to have friends outside of their own, often isolated, environment. By becoming a College Buddy, volunteers offer a Buddy the chance to explore life in a new way.* Over 100 active and associate college students*Over 75 active persons with intellectual or developmental disabilities* Established at the University of Alabama in 1993* One of only two campus chapters in Alabama
summary: Best Buddies is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships and integrated employment for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals. 

The purpose of Beta Alpha Psi is to engage with members, industry, and educational institutions associated with Beta Alpha Psi. 

Beta Alpha Psi will shape the financial and business information professions by developing members into ethical, professional, and confident leaders. 

The mission of Beta Alpha Psi is to inspire and support excellence by:

  • encouraging the study and practice of accountancy, finance, business analytics, or digital technology;
  • providing opportunities for service, professional development, and interaction among members and financial professionals; and
  • fostering lifelong ethical, social, and public responsibilities.
summary: The International Honor Organization for Financial Information Students and Professionals.

name: Beta Beta Beta
description: The Tri-Beta Honor Society was formed in 1922 by Frank G. Brooks and dedicated to the appreciation and understanding of the biological sciences. It was founded on the ideals of promoting scholarship in biological sciences, in promoting the dissemination of biological knowledge, and to encourage research. There are now over 430 chapters across the United States and Puerto Rico and over 175,000 people have been inducted into lifetime membership. Chartered in 1966, the Kappa Beta chapter of Tri-Beta has been present on the campus of The University of Alabama (Tuscaloosa) for roughly 40 years.
summary: The National Biological Honor Society founded on the ideals of promoting scholarship in biological sciences, in promoting the dissemination of biological knowledge and to encourage research.

name: Beta Theta Pi
summary: The Delta Theta Chapter of Beta Theta Pi.

name: Black Student Union
description: The Black Student Union seeks to establish and innovate relationship opportunities, and experiences for minority students at the University of Alabama. We also serve as a liaison between the minority student body and administration in efforts to uphold the values stated in the Capstone Creed. We serve our members by providing community service opportunities, mentoring relationships within BSU, and providing outlets for expression and discussion. We strive to address the issues that affect our community on campus, locally, and internationally. Our goal is to provide a safe and welcoming environment for all students who wish to be a part of it.
summary: Black, student, union, activism, achievement, alliance, minority, organization, student body, community service, administration, relationships, mentor

name: Black Warrior Review
description: Founded in 1974, Black Warrior Review is an internationally renowned literary journal run by students in the MFA program for Creative Writing at UA. BWR is also the oldest continuously-run literary journal produced by graduate students in the United States. Our mission, in a nutshell, is to seek out and publish experimental writing & innovative writers that may not otherwise find a home for their work.

  • We support lifelong learning as a means to gain understanding and wisdom.
  • We promote the value of fraternal membership and commitment to higher education.
  • We help prepare members to contribute to the world's work.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha

The Chapter is located in the Southeastern Region (http://www.akasoutheastern.org/). The national AKA website is located at http://www.aka1908.com/.
summary: The Theta Sigma Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated

name: Blount Student Organization
description: The Blount Student Organization, a student collaborative, works to foster student relationships and build a strong community within the Blount Scholars Programs through coordinated events and outreach. This organization works in the best interest of all Blount students by fostering inclusivity, providing support and opportunities, and promoting and maintaining the academic integrity of the Blount program.
summary: The Blount Student Organization, a student collaborative, works to foster student relationships and build a strong community within the Blount Scholars Program through coordinated events and outreach within the Blount student body.

name: Student Veteran Association
description: The Student Veterans Association at The University of Alabama provides a strong support network for student veterans, dependents, and survivors, fostering camaraderie and ensuring access to resources that promote academic, social, and professional success. The SVA offers a community that builds on the bonds formed through military service, advocating for the needs of veterans on campus and beyond. Through structured leadership, weekly meetings, and key initiatives like Operation Iron Ruck (OIR)—which raises awareness for veteran well-being and suicide prevention—the organization remains dedicated to service and advocacy. Open to all students who support its mission, the SVA upholds a non-discrimination policy and operates with a military-inspired structure that reinforces professionalism, teamwork, and leadership development.
summary: The SVA at UA supports student veterans and military dependents through camaraderie, advocacy, and professional/academic growth. Open to all students, it hosts Operation Iron Ruck and follows a military-inspired structure with executive board governance.

name: Canterbury Episcopal Student Forum
summary: Religious, Episcopal, Christian

name: American Advertising Federation
description: UA's chapter of the American Advertising Federation is devoted to offering advantages in the advertising industry to members of all majors. We host professional development meetings, networking events and agency tours throughout the year. Additionally, members enjoy access to industry competitions/conferences, exclusive internship and job opportunities and more. We have previously hosted team members from companies like Edelman, BBDO and NBCUniversal. We hope to see you at our next meeting!
summary: Interested in learning more about advertising and marketing? Join the American Advertising Federation chapter at UA for professional development, networking, competition/conference and scholarship opportunities for students of all majors.

name: Capstone Agency
description: Capstone Agency is an award winning, student-run integrated marketing communications firm serving clients both across the country and within the community. Home to more than 70 associates, Capstone Agency provides students with real world client experience and tools to build their professional portfolios. Students research, plan and execute marketing campaigns based on the client's needs. Capstone Agency empowers students to develop professional, client-ready work in a learning environment that pushes creative boundaries, encourages collaboration and refines leadership skills.
summary: Integrated marketing communications firm empowering students to take on professional opportunities.

name: Capstone Association of Black Journalists
description: The Capstone Association of Black Journalists is the collegiate chapter of the National Association of Black Journalists. Established in 19__, CABJ has been a student organization aimed to address a variety of journalistic topics while also preparing all aspiring student journalists to obtain careers post-graduation. Students who are interested in joining CABJ do not have to be minorities. Anyone who has a love for journalism with a taste of diversity is welcome to join!

Additionally this organization strives to educate the community and unite environmental leaders in order to stimulate dialog and cooperation to create a more ecologically sustainable community. Through service projects, volunteer work, and activism we strive to make our campus more environmentally friendly each day.

name: Alabama Finance Association
description:
The Alabama Finance Association (AFA) is a professional development organization for students at The University of Alabama who are interested in learning more about a career in finance. The AFA hosts professional development events throughout each semester that bring a diverse array of speakers to campus who speak about their experience in finance. These events are a great way for students to network with their peers and business professionals.

 

SIGN UP HERE
summary: The Alabama Finance Association (AFA) promotes interaction of the academic and business worlds by hosting professional development events that bring a diverse array of speakers and recruiters to campus from across the country.

name: Alabama Information Management Society
description: The mission of AIMS is to give students a better understanding of the growing number of opportunities in the IT industry. AIMS meets Tuesdays during the Fall and Spring semesters, and meetings involve dinner and presentations from many of the MIS program's corporate partners. Each year, representatives from over 30 world-class companies come to campus to talk about industry trends, their companies, and their work, and to recruit for internships and full-time positions.
summary: The Alabama Information Management Society (AIMS) is a student run organization that holds weekly meetings where corporate executives and recruiters come to campus and talk to MIS students about internship and career opportunities.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
description:
Gamma Iota Sigma is a professional international insurance fraternity that promotes, encourages, and sustains student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial sciences as professions. It is open to all University of Alabama students who are interested in exploring a career in financial services or who want to become wiser consumers of financial services products. The fraternity is designed to bring members into contact with leaders in the insurance world and give them the opportunity to network. It is also open to students of ALL majors. 

Among the many benefits of membership are:

* Outstanding dinner events with guest speakers

* Seminars and conferences featuring industry leaders

* Assistance with internships and job placements

* Networking with industry professionals

* Resume enhancement

* Leadership opportunities

* Eligibility for scholarships

* Career Information

Upcoming dates for our events can be found at https://efls.culverhouse.ua.edu/rmias/news-and-events/

Follow our Instagram at @alabama_gis!
summary: A professional organization at the University of Alabama that aims to provide an outlet for professional development and networking to students of any major interested in the fields of insurance, risk management, actuarial science and financial services.

name: Alabama International Relations Club + Model UN Travel Team
description:
Join the Alabama International Relations Club + Model UN Travel Team (AIRC)!

We’re here to connect UA students with the world—literally. AIRC is all about breaking down political, cultural, and economic borders to spark global awareness and create the next generation of international leaders. Everyone’s welcome—undergrad, grad, or international students!

What We’re About:

  1. Exploring global issues, languages, and cultures.
  2. Prepping members for leadership in our connected world.
  3. Making an impact locally and globally through service and outreach.
  4. Building an awesome, supportive community.

What We Offer:

  • Weekly Meetings: Join us Thursday nights at 6:30 p.m. for engaging talks on global events, guest speakers (think Peace Corps, Study Abroad, and more!), and all things international.
  • Model UN Travel: Debate, solve global issues, and travel to cities across the U.S. (and beyond!) for Model UN competitions. Open to all dues-paying members.
  • TILE: Teach languages and share cultures with kids in Tuscaloosa schools.
  • MUNOP: Mentor high school students in Model UN skills, helping them compete and grow.
  • ALMUN & AIRMUNC: Run high school and collegiate Model UN conferences, gain leadership experience, and showcase your talents in organizing major events.
  • Community Fun: Intramural sports, trivia nights, cultural events, and holiday celebrations make this more than a club—it’s a family.
  • Advocacy & Outreach: Respond to global crises, raise awareness, and make a difference through fundraising and campus events.

Ready to explore, learn, and lead? Come join the global conversation with AIRC! ��
summary: AIRC engages UA students in global affairs by providing opportunities for professional development and community outreach through advocacy, debates, Model UN Competitions, language & cultural education, and by hosting 2 MUN  conferences (AIRMUNC & ALMUN)

name: Alabama Kayak Club
description: The Alabama Kayak Club is a UA sport club that specializes in whitewater instruction, recreation, and racing. No experience is necessary and we accommodate any whitewater paddling skill level. The only requirement is members must be comfortable swimming in moving water.
summary: UA's Whitewater Kayaking Club

name: Alabama Panhellenic Association
description: The Panhellenic Council is the largest women's organization on campus. It is the governing body of the eighteen National Panhellenic Conference sororities and one affiliate member sorority. The Panhellenic Council promotes excellence in academics, community and campus involvement, and sisterhood. We are committed to the vision of creating young women of excellence.
summary: Greek Life, Sorority

name: College of Communication and Information Sciences Ambassadors
description: C&IS Ambassadors provide student leadership, mentoring and guidance for the College of Communication & Information Sciences. Recruitment efforts for the college. events
summary: The C&IS Ambassadors is a leadership organization of approximately 35 students who serve as the official ambassadors for the College of Communication and Information Sciences.

name: Alabama Table Tennis Association
description: The University of Alabama Table Tennis Club started in 2005 with the help of our faculty adviser, Torin Alter. We compete in tournaments sanctioned by the National College Table Tennis Association and play recreationally here at The University of Alabama. We practice multiple times a week at The University of Alabama Recreation Center. No matter the skill level, anyone who is interested in playing table tennis is more than welcome!
summary: Table Tennis Club for all levels and people at UA (students, faculty, etc.). We have a traveling A and B team for inter-collegiate competitions, as well as some intramural league play. We have practices four times a week.

name: Alabama Triathlon
description:
Anyone who used to run, swim, or bike in highschool, or is looking for a group of people to get into fitness with will find a place here. Our club includes triathletes from every experience level, be it a first-time triathlete, or an Ironman finisher.

As a team, we train, race, volunteer, and attend social events together. While class schedules often make it hard for students to train together, Alabama Triathletes has non-mandatory team workouts seven days a week. Our officers are also willing to work with you personally to develop a training plan that allows you to work towards your goals with a schedule that works for you. We try to cater to different levels of fitness by breaking down workouts into groups by fitness level. Team members compete in USAT collegiate races all the way to the national level. As a team we also have team dinners and volunteer in the Tuscaloosa area.

If you have any interest in meeting us and training with us, join our GroupMe or reach out to any of our officers directly at the information listed below.
summary: Alabama Triathletes is a University of Alabama Club Team dedicated to the sport of triathlon. Our members compete in a number of races as a USA Triathlon SEC collegiate club.

name: Alabama Water Ski Team
description: The University of Alabama's Water Ski Team has been existent for over 30 years. Skiers from all around the country enroll in the University to not only expand their knowledge academically, but to be apart of the University's Water Ski Team. The team has skiers of all levels, from beginners to world champions. We are primarily focused on 3 event skiing (Slalom, Trick, and Jump). Every year, our skiers enhance skills and are opened to many opportunities through Alabama's Water Ski Team. With the motivation, drive, and enthusiasm that the members of our ski team possess, we are competing on a nationally ranked level and improving every year. We compete at NCWSA Division 1 Nationals each year.
summary: UA's competitive and recreational waterski team. Our goal is to promote the sport of waterskiing at The University of Alabama and give members an opportunity to compete and learn the sport of waterskiing.

name: Alabama Women's Club Volleyball
description:
The University of Alabama Women’s Club Volleyball team was established in 2006 and is continuously growing as a program. UA’s Club Volleyball team is a great opportunity for women who want to continue their volleyball career into college, without the major time commitment of a varsity athletic’s program. We practice three times a week, have two teams, and travel all over the SEC competing in tournaments.

All other information can be found on our student run website linked below:

https://alabamawclubvb.wixsite.com/uavb
summary: volleyball, club, women

name: Capstone Association of Nursing Students
description: The National Student Nurse Association (NSNA) is the pre-professional nursing student organization. The state constituent of the NSNA is the Alabama Association of Nursing Students (AANS). The local affiliate at the Capstone College of Nursing (CCN) is named the Capstone Association of Nursing Students (ANS). The national, state, and local pre-professional associations create unity among nursing students by stimulating understanding and interest in promoting professional nursing as a vital societal force. Participation in the ANS is a way for CCN students to begin to learn the functions, strategies, and results of organized efforts in addressing problems and issues germane to the profession. The ANS serves as a channel of communication among the CCN, the state, and national associations. Membership information for ANS is shared directly with students in nursing classes and notices are posted on student bulletin boards. Additional information including membership dues can be obtained from the ANS's faculty advisor. Personal benefits and accomplishments derived through membership in the ANS can be a valuable asset to students. Members also have the opportunity to voice opinion on nursing legislation. Attendance at area meetings and state and national conventions is encouraged. Announcements of ANS meetings are posted on student bulletin boards in the school of nursing and in various buildings around campus. Students are strongly encouraged to become participating members of the ANS as membership provides a means of preparing students to be contributing members of the nursing profession.
summary: nursing, preprofessional, student nurse, nurses, capstone college,

name: Capstone College of Nursing Ambassadors
description:
The Capstone College of Nursing Ambassadors are the official student representatives for the Capstone College of Nursing. They represent our top students and are true “ambassadors” for the CCN, providing service to the CCN, promoting pride in the CCN, and communicating information about the CCN to the public.

Primary activities include:

  • Providing tours of the CCN, including the Clinical Practice Lab and Simulation Center, for prospective students and their families
  • Coordinating homecoming activities
  • Assisting with academic advising activities
  • Participating in university-wide events
  • Hosting nursing students from CCN’s sister school in Chiba, Japan each spring
  • Hosting Alumni gatherings
summary: capstone, college, nursing, ambassadors, CCN

name: Financial Planning Association of UA
description: UA Financial Planning Association (UA FPA) is a vibrant community for students passionate about financial planning, investing, asset, and wealth management. Open to all majors, UA FPA connects members with industry experts, interactive workshops, and exclusive networking opportunities. Through events like panels, LinkedIn and resume workshops, and guest speakers from top firms, members gain valuable career insights and connections. UA FPA also promotes community involvement through volunteering, while fostering personal and professional growth. Join UA FPA to expand your network, develop career skills, and connect with peers and professionals in the industry!
summary: We provide members with networking opportunities. Any major is welcome to join but we particularly benefit students intending to work in financial planning, financial counseling, and the financial services industry.

name: Alpha Kappa Lambda
summary: Alpha Kappa Lambda is a social fraternity located here on campus. Our mission is to create men of character who live by our five ideals.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
Since being brought to campus in 1924, the Alpha Rho chapter of Alpha Kappa Psi has consistently been a top chapter within the national fraternity, winning the 2019 Chapter of Distinction award. With 150 active brothers and thousands of alumni around the country, this chapter prides itself on representing the values of Alpha Kappa Psi on the University of Alabama's campus: brotherhood, unity, integrity, knowledge, and service.

 

Alpha Kappa Psi, Alpha Rho is open to all majors. We enrich our brotherhood with a variety of professional and service events such as Students Teaching Students and Arboretum Service Days. Our members aim to excel in the professional world as well as in the classroom.

 

Outside of our professional development efforts, our members are creating life-long friendships. Every semester, we host a variety of socials, formals, and retreats. Whether we are in Hewson Hall conducting business or in Bryant-Denny Stadium cheering on the Crimson Tide, we always enjoy hanging out together. Head on over to our website, akpsibama.org, or our various social media channels to learn more about our organization!
summary: Welcome to the oldest and largest co-ed professional business fraternity in the world! Open to all majors across campus, we are the premier developer of principled business leaders. Head on over to our website, akpsibama.org, to learn more!

name: Capstone Minority Information Systems Society
description: The mission of this chapter shall be built upon the goal of increasing diversity in information systems and increasing the overall academic and social well-being of all our members through our mentoring and social programs. Diversity brings different people together, with different ideas, and increases the capacity of knowledge. As we grow and move forward, we will acknowledge this as a key area of opportunity and work hard to make this a reality.
summary: Minority, Information System, MIS, Professional Development, CS, Business, Technology, Recruiting

name: Capstone Music Therapy Association
description:
The Capstone Music Therapy Association (CMTA) is an organization that seeks to foster the growth and fellowship of its members and advocate for the music therapy profession through opportunities in the music therapy program, the School of Music, and the Tuscaloosa and Alabama community. As a member of CMTA, you will learn more about the exciting field of music therapy and have opportunities to serve hands-on in the community alongside other music therapy students.

 

What is music therapy? Music therapy is the clinical and evidence-based use of music interventions to accomplish individualized goals within a therapeutic relationship by a credentialed professional who has completed an approved music therapy program. Music therapy interventions can be designed to promote wellness, manage stress, alleviate pain, express feeling, enhance memory, improve communication, and promote physical rehabilitation. CMTA is an organization dedicated to improving comradeship amongst the members, as well as serving our campus and community through the use of music therapy.
summary: The purpose of the Capstone Music Therapy Assoc. is to promote professional development in the field of music therapy and to provide opportunities for interaction between music therapists of all levels. We also strive to serve our campus and community.

name: The Carl A. Elliott Society
description: The Carl A. Elliott Society is a service-based honor society dedicated to social justice efforts and improving the quality of education across the state of Alabama. This honorary demonstrates its namesake's dedication to justice, Carl Atwood Elliott, a former University of Alabama SGA President who later became an Alabama congressman in the U.S. House of Representatives. He based his political principles on assistance to the needy, racial tolerance, and better educational opportunities for all. Two very notable acts Elliott co-authored include The Library Services Act of 1956 and the National Defense Education Act of 1958. The Carl A. Elliott Society recognizes students who have made significant leadership contributions to the campus, community, state, and nation in areas of social and cultural progress and who portray a spirit of the ideals held by Congressman Elliott. Through leadership and collaboration, the Elliott Society works together to host events, such as Kickstart College, to serve our community and improve educational opportunities for all. Members collaborate in Carl A. Elliott Society events to create a meaningful impact on campus and in the community.
summary: The Carl A. Elliott Society is a service-based honorary dedicated to social justice efforts and improving the quality of education across the state of Alabama.

name: Chi Alpha Campus Ministry
description:
“We are therefore Christ’s ambassadors, as though God were making his appeal through us. We implore you on Christ’s behalf: be reconciled to God.” 2 Corinthians 5:20


The story of the Bible is a narrative that spans the entire existence of mankind. What’s the message of this story? Reconciliation. Mankind chooses independence over God, God sets in motion a plan to bring his children back to himself, we reach the climax with Jesus who makes the reunion possible. And then the plot twist comes. How does he choose to spread this message of reconciliation? Through US.


We are Christ’s Ambassadors or Christou apostoloi in Greek, which we abbreviate with the first letters Chi Alpha (XA). We are a movement of students who are continually being reconciled to Christ and who are on mission to reconcile others. We’re not just another ministry, we’re more of a family. And we are convinced that there is nothing more fulfilling, purposeful, or fun than pursuing God together. So come join the family!
summary: A group of students seeking growth as a Christian community.

name: Chi Epsilon
description: Chi Epsilon is a national civil engineering honor society founded in 1922. Invitation for membership is limited to students who rank in the top one-third of their class scholastically and who exhibit the four primary requirements of a successful engineer: Scholarship, Character, Practicality, and Sociability.
summary: chi epsilon, civil engineering, honor societies, engineering

name: Chi Omega
description: Chi Omega is one of the top sororities at UA and prides itself on academic achievement, campus involvement, and philanthropy. Our 300+ members are leaders on campus and are involved with many organizations and honor societies. In addition to our campus involvement, we volunteer with the Make-A-Wish Foundation and the Boys and Girls Club of West Alabama.
summary: Nu Beta Chapter

name: Chi Phi
description: Our Chapter was founded in 1920 at the University of Alabama.
summary: This is the Tau Chapter of the Chi Phi Fraternity.

name: Chinese Culture Club
description: Advance the knowledge and appreciation of Chinese culture and traditions among UA students through social and cultural engagement.
summary: We intend to advance the knowledge and appreciation of Chinese culture and traditions among UA students. Our club is open to students of all backgrounds interested in Chinese culture and meeting new people.

name: Circle K International
description: UA Circle K is a service leadership organization within the Kiwanis Family with three tenets: service, leadership, and fellowship. Those in Circle K have endless opportunities to make a difference on our campus and in the community through impactful and sustainable service that makes a difference each and every day. Additionally, through our unique leadership programs and positions, we provide hands-on leadership experience and development for all of our members.
summary: Since 1957, UA Circle K has been prominent on campus, encouraging service, leadership, and fellowship through initiatives promoting literacy, supporting health programs, working with animal care, and much more. CKI is an exciting way to make a difference

name: Club Geography
description: The goal of Club Geography is to promote interest in the history and development of geography at The University of Alabama.
summary: Club Geography, Geography Club, Club GY, Geography, Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Environmental Action, Student Associations of Geographers at Alabama, SAGA

name: Crimson Comedy Collective
description:
As members of the Crimson Comedy Collective, we believe that logical and creative thinking are mutually beneficial. We offer students a creative outlet, specifically in stage productions produced each semester. The entire club meets once every other week, and our Improv comedy group meets weekly. Club involvement is at the member's discretion, allowing people of all majors to participate. Many students become involved by seeing us at Get On Board Days and by seeing our productions. All majors and all theater experience levels are welcome to join! 

Join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/96182840/0inbQsl6
summary: Here at the Crimson Comedy Collective, we give students a creative outlet in the exciting performing of improv and standup comedy. All majors and theatre experience levels are welcome. Come join the fun!

name: The Collegiate 100
description: The mission statement and purpose of the Collegiate 100 is "to improve the quality of life within our communities and enhance educational and economic opportunities for all African American students at the University of Alabama."
summary:
The mission of the Collegiate 100 of The University of Alabama is to support the sponsoring Chapter of 100 Black Men of
America in its efforts to nurture and enhance the growth, development and opportunities for young African
American students.

name: Women Of Excellence
description: Women of Excellence is an organization of progressive women.Each member embraces the value of nurturing young women as they look toward brighter futures. Our purpose: To build and empower African- American women through greater access to education, political strength, volunteer opportunities and civic responsibility.
summary: WOE is an organization geared towards the empowerment of women and our community.

name: Collegiate Recovery and Intervention Services
description:
Collegiate Recovery and Intervention Services (CRIS) provides a comprehensive continuum of care for students with substance use concerns. Services range from prevention, awareness, and exploration of consequences for high risk behaviors to a supportive, safe, and engaging environment that supports students seeking or contemplating recovery.

The professional staff operate from a developmental and holistic wellness approach.  Services range from alcohol and other drugs (AOD) counseling, including family counseling in our Counseling and Intervention Services area to a vibrant Collegiate Recovery Community known as The University of Alabama Recovery Model.
summary: Collegiate Recovery and Intervention Services (CRIS) provides a comprehensive continuum of care for students with substance use concerns.

name: Center for Service and Leadership
description:
The Center for Service and Leadership (CSL) strives to enhance the student experience through active engagement, building upon the foundation that service is transformational for both the individual and the community. We cultivate a culture that produces committed and engaged citizens that understand their leadership potential in creating a better society.

The CSL is home to Al's Pals, Beyond Bama, Beat Auburn Beat Hunger, Big Days of Service, UA Habitat, UA Miracle, Serving Bama community action teams. These teams provide students the ability to enhance their leadership skills while becoming active, global citizens through experimental learning opportunities. Our Motto, taken from Dr. Seuss' book, The Lorax, " Unless someone like you cares a whole awful lot, nothing is going to get better. It’s not."
summary: The Center for Service and Leadership strives to enhance the student experience through active engagement, building upon the foundation that service is transformational for both the individual and the community.

name: Coordinating Council for Honor Societies
description: The Coordinating Council for Honor Societies (CCHS) was founded at The University of Alabama in the spring of 1987 with the goals of increasing campus awareness of honor societies and supporting the initiations and events of member groups. CCHS strives to stimulate cooperative programs and projects and encourage communication among member groups. CCHS also assists in the planning of Honors Week and sponsors the Honor Society Preview events.
summary: coordinating, council, honor, societies

name: Delta Zeta
description: Guided by our organizational purpose, we inspire, promote and nurture: enduring friendships, personal development, leadership excellence, generous service, and innovative growth.
summary: To be a premier women's organization by creating an enriching environment which is essential for all and empowering our members for life.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta, Alabama Alpha Chapter
description:
On April 28, 1926, fifteen premedical students at the University of Alabama met with Dr. Jack Montgomery, premedical advisor and professor of organic chemistry, to formalize the organization of a new premedical honor society. From these modest beginnings, Alpha Epsilon Delta has become the world's largest Honor Society exclusively serving premedical education, with a membership exceeding 144,000 in 186 chapters. 

AED provides pre-health students with valuable information and assistance in their quest for a career in health care. Weekly meetings provide excellent information from leading professionals in the medical field. In addition, our peer network assists in many aspects of a pre-health degree such as: tutoring, teacher recommendations, MCAT tips, course scheduling, and social events! Please stop by and see what AED has to offer you. 

Induction requirements include:

  • earning 15 points thorugh attending meetings and as otherwise announced
  • at least 3 semesters of college coursework
  • at least a 3.2 overall GPA and at least a 3.2 science and math GPA
  • completing at least 20 hours of community service.  

Our induction ceremony is in the spring semester.
summary: We are the Health Preprofessional Honor Society on the University of Alabama campus. We are a resource for pre-health students, offering mentoring services, shadowing tips, and networking. Induction requirements are listed in the full description.

name: Mock Trial Association
description: The purpose of this organization, the University of Alabama Mock Trial Association, shall be to unite students with a common interest in law, debate, legal issues, and the Courts. We aim to help members improve their debate skills, public speaking ability, and knowledge of the law by participating in intercollegiate mock trial competitions across the nation. Through our intercollegiate competitive activities and programs, the University of Alabama Mock Trial Association aims to provide meaningful learning experiences to those students interested in legal issues and careers by helping our members and participants refine their longer term career goals (whether or not these should eventually encompass formal legal work) by providing some of the earliest and most realistic exposure to the world of trial law. We do this through hands-on exposure to litigation, trial advocacy, and analytical casework. Lastly, we create an incredibly fun and team based environment.
summary: Competitive, nationally ranked mock trial team that helps to improve public speaking skills and knowledge of the law, through a team-based environment.

name: Criminal Justice Student Association
description: The Mission of the Criminal Justice Student Association is to promote a genuine interest in the criminal justice system outside of class, rigorous scholarship and life-long learning; to aid in transition into CJSA’s honor society counterpart, Alpha Phi Sigma; to keep it’s members up to date on criminal-justice-related career and internship opportunities.
summary: CJSA creates a fun environment that promotes a genuine interest in the criminal justice system outside of class; job and internship opportunities, and academic excellence which aids in transition into CJSA’s honor society counterpart, Alpha Phi Sigma!

name: Crimson Chaos
description:
As the Official Student Section for University of Alabama Athletics, our mission is to create a home advantage for the Crimson Tide at all of our sporting events. We do this by distracting and annoying the opposing team as much as possible through taunts, signs, and general tomfoolery.

 

In addition, we provide unique meeting experiences with stadium/facility tours, coaches meet and greets, and more! Sign up today!!
summary: athletics, students, basketball, sports, football, baseball, soccer, tennis, support

name: University of Alabama Cricket Club
description:
We warmly welcome all students to become part of our Cricket Club and practice with us. With over 30 active members, our club offers a great opportunity to connect with fellow students, stay active, and enjoy the spirit of teamwork.

Cricket is more than just a sport—it encourages cultural exchange, builds friendships, and promotes a healthy and active lifestyle. Whether you are an experienced player or just getting started, we would love to have you on board!

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to us at ahalim1@crimson.ua.edu.

Cheers,
University of Alabama Cricket Club
summary: University of Alabama Cricket Club

name: University of Alabama Club Tennis
description:
We are a traveling tennis club with players of all levels that competes against other universities and through the USTA Tennis on Campus circuit. Club tennis also offers intramural practices where we play games, and do various drills. The intramural level provides an opportunity for players of any level who enjoy tennis to participate in a like-minded group with the intent of providing a fun source of physical activity during the week. 

 

For updates on our intramural practices, join our Groupme:

https://groupme.com/join_group/101474769/YK1eXrJo

 

For updates on our club/travel team, join our GroupMe:

https://groupme.com/join_group/101474801/LdqHpNEA
summary: The University of Alabama Crimson Club Tennis

name: The University of Alabama Bass Fishing Team
shortName: UA Alabama Bass Anglers aka "The Team"
description:
University of Alabama Bass Fishing is a group of full-time students and UA faculty members that enjoy bass fishing, while also competing in different bass fishing tournaments. The team participates in different events which include Strike King Bassmaster College Series, MLF Abu Garcia College Series, Bass Pro Shops ACA Collegiate Series, B.A.S.S. Nation Alabama College Series. We are able to travel to and compete at some of the best bass fishing lakes in the world against multiple colleges and universities. Our main focus is competing and that takes determined hard-working anglers to have enough discipline and good time management skills to maintain the balance between school and fishing. We are not a recreation-only type team if that is what you are looking for I'm sorry this organization isn't that.

We have a group of anglers that will travel to compete and others that aren't to that level yet. This group will need more time to learn in order to benefit the organization and help it grow. That group will be the local ones until it is decided by the officers that they are ready to be on the travel team.
summary: University of Alabama Bass Fishing is a group of current students and faculty that enjoy bass fishing and competing at collegiate-level events.

name: UA Chapter of The American Choral Directors Association
description:
The student chapter of ACDA promotes all choral activities at the University of Alabama. It is comprised of predominantly of Choral Music Education majors and Choral Conducting graduate students, but is open to all students who love choral music. The chapter assists with local ACDA events hosted at the University of Alabama and attends conferences at the National and Southern Divisional levels.

The group is open to all students!
summary: UA Chapter of The American Choral Directors Association

name: American Institute of Aeronautics & Astronautics
description:
All meeting dates, locations, and guest information will go through our GroupMe.

This is an organization for those who share a common interest in aerospace. Meetings include speakers from government agencies and other companies related to the aerospace industry. We focus on networking with industry professionals, aerospace professors, and between fellow classmates!
summary: Aviation, Aerospace, Planes, Aircraft, Airplanes, Engineering, Astronautics, Aeronautics, Rocketry, Professional Organization

name: Culverhouse Ambassadors
description: As official student public relations representatives, ambassadors, tour guides, and student recruitment agents for the Culverhouse College of Business, it is our purpose that we provide services, promote pride, and communicate an increasing understanding of the College of Business and the University of Alabama. It is our responsibility to represent the College in a credible and positive manner with a sense of dedication and responsibility to the students, faculty, administration, alumni, and the community.
summary: Ambassadors are the official student public relations representatives, ambassadors, tour guides, and student recruitment agents for the Culverhouse College of Business.

name: Culverhouse Investment Management Group
description:
Visit uacimg.com for more information, or reach out to cimg.cba@gmail.com. 

 

CIMG is a student-run investment fund managed by select students at The University of Alabama with oversight from the Director and Associate Director of the C.T. and Kelley Fitzpatrick Center for Value Investing, John Heins and Mark Meulenberg. CIMG uses a value-based, equity-only investing strategy to actively manage a portfolio worth over $2,500,000. Members learn to apply quantitative and qualitative analysis techniques to value companies and make intelligent investment decisions. The group prepares young professionals for careers in investment banking, equity research, and numerous other attractive post-graduate fields. Current CIMG members and CIMG alumni have received internship opportunities and full-time job offers at a variety of top-tier financial and business-strategy organizations such as Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Evercore, Point72 Asset Management, Jane Street, Boston Consulting Group, Bain & Company, and many others. However, an interest in a career in finance or business is not necessary to join CIMG, and students of all majors, backgrounds, and interests are encouraged to apply.
summary: The Culverhouse Investment Management Group (CIMG) is a team of select undergraduate students who manage an equity investment portfolio with a value investing philosophy.

name: Dance Alabama!
description: Dance Alabama!'s goal is to facilitate a student-focused experience where dancers are given a platform to choreograph, create, and learn in a professional environment. We pride ourselves increasing a diverse and engaging setting for all members and the community through our outreach, opportunities, and events.
summary: Dance Alabama! is a student organization that encompasses major and non-major dancers and is the primary dance organization to host student-led classes, professional development workshops, philanthropic/social events, and so much more! #SICKMOVES

name: Delta Chi
description: Delta Chi (ΔΧ) (del-ta kai) or D-Chi is an international Greek letter college social fraternity formed on October 13, 1890, at Cornell University, initially as a professional fraternity for law students. On April 29, 1922, Delta Chi became a general membership social fraternity, eliminating the requirement for men to be studying law and opening membership to all areas of study. Delta Chi became one of the first international fraternities to abolish “hell week”, when it did so on April 22, 1929. Delta Chi is a charter member of the North-American Interfraternity Conference (NIC). The Fraternity is headquartered at 314 Church Street in Iowa City, Iowa 52244. As of Spring 2011, Delta Chi has initiated over 107,000 members.
summary: Delta Chi Fraternity

name: Delta Delta Delta
summary: Delta Delta Delta is a sorority bonded by friendship and encouragement.  We are motivated by our philanthropy St. Jude and make it one of the most important aspects of building our sorority character.

name: Delta Kappa Epsilon
summary: Founded nationally in 1844 and at The University of Alabama in 1847 as the first fraternity on campus.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity, and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce, and the civic and commercial welfare of the community.
summary: A professional business fraternity dedicated to building connections and developing students into the business leaders of tomorrow.

name: Delta Tau Delta - Delta Eta Chapter
description: Founded nationally in 1858, the Delta Eta Chapter of Delta Tau Delta was established at The University of Alabama in 1925.
summary: Founded nationally in 1858, the Delta Eta Chapter of Delta Tau Delta was established at The University of Alabama in 1925.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering Honor Society

name: Eta Sigma Gamma
description:
The mission of Eta Sigma Gamma is promotion of the discipline by elevating the standards, ideals, competence and ethics of professionally prepared men and women in health education.

The goals of the honorary include:

  • Supporting the planning, implementation and evaluation of health education programs and resources
  • Stimulating and disseminating scientific research;
  • Motivating and providing health education services
  • Recognizing academic achievement
  • Supporting health education advocacy initiatives
  • Promoting professional standards and ethics
  • Promoting networking activities among health educators and related professionals
summary: We are a Nationally Chartered Health Education and Health Promotion Honorary Society.

name: Student Fashion Association
description:
Calling all aspiring fashion designers, fashion buyers, marketers, models, photographers, graphic designers, stylists, fashion bloggers, make-up & hair artists, event planners, personal shoppers & more! 

MISSION STATEMENT

SFA is dedicated to serving its members by providing opportunities to network, acquire knowledge, and enhance skills in order to compete in the fashion industry.

WHAT IS SFA? 

The Student Fashion Association is a professional organization open to students who are interested in the promotion and development of the fashion industry. Activities include; trips to major market centers, photo shoots, fashion shows, interaction with professionals to promote an in-depth understanding of the fashion corporate world and career opportunities in the textile and apparel industry. 

WHO CAN JOIN?

SFA is open to all currently enrolled University of Alabama students!

WHAT WE OFFER

  • Internship searches and portfolio builds
  • Opportunities to network with small business owners, student interns, and successful industry alums.
  • Exposure to fashion show production
  • Community involvement with local organizations
  • Collaboration among fellow students
  • Workshops
  • Guest speakers from professionals in the industry
  • Photoshoots and modeling experience

For more information, email alabamasfa@gmail.com
summary: The Student Fashion Association is dedicated to serving its members by providing opportunities to network, acquire knowledge, and enhance skills in order to compete in the fashion industry.

name: Financial Affairs Committee
description: Please visit fac.sl.ua.edu to review the FAC guidelines, training, dates & deadlines, forms, etc.
summary: financial, affairs, committee

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: The American Institute of Chemical Engineers is an international organization whose mission is to promote excellence in Chemical Engineering, further and enhance the development of knowledge, and promote the profession's standards and ethics. The University of Alabama's student chapter of AIChE works to serve the needs of our members and other interested students through personal and professional development, connecting students with industry and academic professionals, and using social programs to bring each other together outside of the classroom.
summary: Chemical Engineering pre-professional organization

name: First College Ministry
description: First College Ministry seeks to lead students into a growing relationship with Christ. We are part of First Baptist Church.
summary: Christian, campus ministry, Baptist, church, college ministry, small group Bible studies, community, fun, community service, mission trips, Bible, connect, intramural sports, retreats, tailgate party, gameday activities

name: The Anderson Society
description:
The Anderson Society is a leadership honorary that recognizes students who have made significant contributions to The University of Alabama. In order to be considered for active membership, candidates must fill out an application in the spring of their junior year. Applicants must have a 3.3 overall GPA, outstanding leadership qualities, and be active on campus and in the community.

Twenty-six active members are selected. Another category of membership is honorary membership. Honorary membership consists of those students, graduate and undergraduate, that are selected for membership but who will not be returning to The University in the fall due to graduation. Faculty members who are chosen are also considered to have honorary membership. Seven honorary members are chosen. Applicants who are selected for both types of memberships are tapped on The Mound during Honors Day in the spring. The Anderson Society participates in fundraising and philanthropic activities. The Anderson Society is also responsible for maintaining the Anderson Society Room in The University of Alabama Student Center.
summary: The Anderson Society is a leadership honorary that recognizes students who have made significant contributions to The University of Alabama.

name: Anthropology Club
description: We are a club open to undergraduate and graduate students that are interested in learning and furthering their education in anthropology. Our club prides our self in providing excursions, talks, and off-campus visits to foster education and community. You do not have to have an anthropology major or minor to join!
summary: We are a club open to undergraduate and graduate students that are interested in anthropology. Our club prides itself on providing excursions, talks, and off-campus visits to foster education and community. All majors and minors welcome!

name: Barefield College of Arts and Sciences Ambassadors
description: The Barefield College of Arts and Sciences Ambassadors is a student leadership organization comprised of 30 of the best and brightest BCAS students. Ambassadors have the opportunity to share their skills and talents by working directly with the Office of the Dean, engaging with prospective students and interacting with donors and alumni. Ambassadors must go through an application, interview and selection process managed by the Dean's Office.
summary: The Official Ambassadors of the J. Frank Barefield Jr. College of Arts and Sciences

name: ASCE -- American Society of Civil Engineers
description: The objective of this Chapter shall be to encourage the development of a professional consciousness, to afford an opportunity for civil engineering students to become acquainted and to practice working together effectively, to promote a spirit of congeniality among them, and to provide friendly contact with the engineering professions.
summary: A professional student organization dedicated to the promotion of the civil engineering profession

name: Associated Builders and Contractors
description:
The University of Alabama Student Chapter of the Associated Builders and Contractors was established in November of 2004. The student chapter is sponsored by the Alabama Chapter of the national organization.

The purpose of the student chapter is to increase student knowledge of the construction industry, promote fellowship and professionalism, and provide service to the College of Engineering, the University of Alabama, and the community.

Associated Builders and Contractors Professional Chapters' chief purpose is the creation of a work climate where independent and union firms can work together in harmony.

Important functions of the student chapter include sponsoring guest speakers at each meeting, hosting job site tours, bridging the gap between students and industry leaders, and helping to prepare students for their future careers.
summary: Associated Builders and Contractors is a student organization, in relation with the State of Alabama Chapter of Associated Builders and Contractors, that helps bridge the gap between students and leaders in the construction industry.

name: Association of Chinese Students and Scholars
description:
As the most important organization for all Chinese students and scholars in the University of Alabama, our main purpose is to provide all Chinese students and scholars in Tuscaloosa the necessary help.

Our organization holds many activities and events, mainly including New Student Orientation, New Members Enrollment, Mid-Autumn Festival, Basketball Tournaments and Spring Festival Celebration, etc. Through our efforts, we wish we can help all Chinese students and scholars feel like living in a big family; provide them better learning environment; build a better relationship between the U.S and China.
summary: An organization serves convenience on studying and living for most Chinese students and scholars, and introduces Chinese tradition culture to all other students.

name: Athletic Training Students' Association
description: The purpose of this association is to promote awareness of the profession of athletic training and to connect, collaborate, and engage with athletic training students and professionals.
summary: Athletic Training

name: Bama Anime and Manga Association
description: BAMA Anime and Manga Association embraces Japanese animations and comic books, including material that emulates the style. We meet every Thursday from 6:00 to 10:00 PM in Lloyd 38, and open our doors for everyone interested in learning about or discussing said topics. We also hold events for our members as opportunities for socialization--our interest is in being a close group of friends rather than a formal organization. We also have a discord, email the president for an invite.
summary: BAMA watches anime and discuses manga during weekly meetings and holds social events.

name: UA Miracle
description:
The University of Alabama Miracle Network Dance Marathon, UA Miracle, is the largest student-run philanthropic organization on campus. We host numerous events throughout the year to raise both awareness and funds for the patients at Children's of Alabama in Birmingham. It is an honor to come together for something much bigger than all of us and we are so proud to have raised over $2,700,000 for Children's!

All of our efforts lead up to our main event in February, BAMAthon: a 10+ hour event! We hear stories from our kids treated at Children's, participate in various games with the theme hours, see special guests, and fundraise to help these children receive the best care and treatment possible as they fight these battles. We are simply students with a passion to MAKE A MIRACLE!
summary: UA Miracle is a philanthropic, student-run organization benefiting Children's of Alabama, our local Children's Miracle Network Hospital. Our mission is to raise money and awareness for the families treated at Children's, by fundraising and hosting events

name: AlabamaHockeyACHA
description: The Alabama Hockey Club was founded in 2005 by the University of Alabama students. The team is now in its twelfth season and plays all of its home games in Pelham, AL at the Pelham Civic Complex. The organization is managed by the University of Alabama students. The program elevated its status to ACHA Division I in 2015 and now competes at the highest level of ACHA college hockey. Alabama is the only SEC school to have a program competing at this level.
summary: The University of Alabama ACHA Division 1 Club Hockey Team.

name: University of Alabama Ranger Challenge
description: The Ranger Company with the Crimson Tide Battalion (UA Army ROTC) is an organization of Alabama student cadets representing the University of Alabama in an annual U.S. Army Ranger Challenge competition against 39 other universities. We train six days a week for the competitions we travel for, which can be nationwide, with a culminating competition at the United States Military Academy at West Point which features teams from around the world.
summary: An organization that aids students in becoming better leaders by applying mentally and physically demanding courses that require teamwork and technical skills.

name: Bama SoS Brigade
description:
The Bama SoS Brigade specializes in cosplay, manga, and genre appreciation, this organization is meant to spread the culture of the anime otaku (Japanese animation fans) by creating community-involving programs.

The Bama SoS Brigade helps provide the core staff to Kami-Con, an anime convention in Birmingham, AL.
summary: Student Organization providing a social outlet for fans of anime, manga, and Japanese culture (otaku).

name: Alabama Wrestling Club
summary: A competitive and inclusive wrestling team whose goal is to promote the sport of wrestling at The University of Alabama and give members an opportunity to compete and continue wrestling careers at a high level as a part of the NCWA.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description: We are a sorority of 503 women on the campus of The University of Alabama. We are actively involved in other organizations on campus, pride ourselves in having top grades, and participate in various philanthropy projects. We seek new membership at the beginning of each Fall semester.
summary: sorority, philanthropy, recruitment

name: Alpha Delta Pi Sorority
description: Alpha Delta Pi was founded on May 15, 1851 at Wesleyan Female College in Macon, GA. Alpha Delta Pi holds the distinction of being the first secret society in the world for college women. Our motto is, "We Live for Each Other," but we live not only for each other, but for the community and campus as a whole. Our philanthropy is the Ronald McDonald House and we love being involved with our time, money and spirit. This sisterhood is founded on tradition, loyalty and love- three aspects that bring us together.
summary: Sorority, Sisterhood, Social, Community, Greek Life, Ronald McDonald House

name: Alpha Lambda Delta
description: Alpha Lambda Delta is an Honor Society for first year men and women who earn at least a 3.5 GPA while carrying a minimum of 12 credit hours in their first semester at the University of Alabama.
summary: Alpha Lambda Delta

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
description: Alpha Omicron Pi is an international women's fraternity that forges lifelong friendships, inspires ambition and promotes character, dignity, scholarship and college loyalty.
summary: alpha, omicron, pi

name: Alpha Phi
description:
Alpha Phi was founded at Syracuse University by ten of the first nineteen women admitted to the newly opened university. The Original Ten, the founders of Alpha Phi, were women of courageous vision and pioneering hearts. Pursuing their studies in a largely male-dominated university, these women yearned for a circle of friends who could sympathize with each other's troubles and support each others' ambitions. On September 18, 1872, these ten women initiated themselves into Alpha Phi. For more information about Alpha Phi, we invite you to visit our website.

Alpha Phis have enjoyed a long tradition of excellence on the University of Alabama campus. Beta Mu was originally founded at UA in 1932
summary: Sorority, Alpha Phi

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: Our mission is to provide support and assistance to the often disjointed University minority population. Poised to enter our 50th year on the campus, The KA Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha has enjoyed a proud history, including but not limited to, 2-time National Chapter of the Year, 5-time Regional Chapter of the Year, and 22-time Alabama District Chapter of the Year. Kappa Alpha prides its self in accomplishing and succeeding in every endeavor of pursuing the aims of this Fraternity: Manly Deeds, Scholarship, and Love For All Mankind, we can be found accomplishing these goals through our national programs: Go to High school, Go to College; Project Alpha; also on the chapter level with, 3M tutoring program, Salvation Army, and Junior Achievement, among the few.
summary: The Kappa Alpha Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated

name: Alpha Psi Omega
description: Alpha Psi Omega is a nationally recognized honor society for students studying theatre. Here at the University of Alabama, we are the Gamma Gamma Cast and belong to Region Four. Being a part of Alpha Psi Omega is more than just finding people who have a common interest in theatre, it is a group of people who love and support all aspects of the performing and technical arts. The members of Alpha Psi Omega are continuously working to discover innovative methods of performance and craft, study and understand classic and modern theatre, and support the work of our peers. One event that Alpha Psi Omega hosts sporadically throughout the semester is Guerrilla Theatre. Guerrilla Theatre is a variety show and creative outlet for theatrical artists, dancers, poets, writers, and any performer of any kind. The Gamma Gamma Cast is proud to be a part of the University of Alabama and encourages you to come see what we are about.
summary: theatre, dance, guerrilla

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: Our vision is to be the co-curricular organization of choice for discerning undergraduate men, through the provision of an enriching brotherhood experience and a full range of character and leadership development opportunities that are: Relevant, Replicable, and Recognizable.  Our purpose is "To Better the Man, through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood founded upon the values of character... Silence, Charity, Purity, Honor, Patriotism."  These values live in the heart of each Brother in this Fraternity.  It is through these common values that our brotherhood, is not only felt from within the Fraternity, but is easily noticed by all non-members.
summary: Alpha Sigma Phi is a collegiate men’s fraternity committed to the development of character, leadership, and brotherhood. Founded in 1845, our purpose is "To Better the Man" through upholding the values of Silence, Charity, Purity, Honor, and P

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description:
Founded on September 11, 1865, Alpha Tau Omega is America's leadership development fraternity. With more than 250 chapters nationwide and over 200,000 members, ATO is one of the largest fraternities in the United States. Follow our Chapter as we continue to lead and pursue excellence in all of our endeavors.

We honor the past, live in the present and work toward the future.
summary: Alpha Tau Omega - Beta Delta Chapter Est. 1885

name: Al's Pals Mentorship Program
description: Al's Pals offers UA students an opportunity to serve as mentors and tutors for elementary children in Tuscaloosa City and County schools providing academic assistance, enrichment and recreation activities for them.
summary: enrichment, volunteer, service, recreation, tutors, mentoring, children

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
description: SAACS is a student run organization focused on community outreach, professional development, and science education. Our focus is the improvement of chemistry and general science knowledge with all those involved with our organization.
summary: ACS, American Chemical Society, chemistry, science, community service, chemistry department, chemical, biology, biochemistry, research, organization, outreach, education

name: American Marketing Association UA Chapter
description: Open to all majors, AMA provides students with a direct connection to the world of marketing through professional speakers, workshops, activities, and leadership opportunities.
summary: Open to all majors, AMA provides students with a direct connection to the world of marketing through professional speakers, workshops, activities, and leadership opportunities.

name: ASCE - Concrete Canoe Team
description:
ASCE Concrete Canoe is a student competition team comprised of mostly civil engineers, but any and all interested students are welcome, regardless of major. Our primary objective is to design and construct a canoe every year to compete in the ASCE Southeast Regional Conference. This process incorporates all parts of the typical process in an engineer's career, namely: product design, testing, product and technical presentations, practice in a sales pitch, and teamwork.

The team meets all of these parts by first creating the design of the hull, followed with construction of a mold. The boat is then cast with a concrete mix that is designed by our team. After finishing, the boat is displayed along with a visual display, and has several papers and an oral presentation. The final part of the competition is several races. Students can be involved in any steps they wish, with no prior experience required.
summary: Student team working to design, construct, and race a concrete canoe. Affiliated with ASCE.

name: Formula SAE
description: Formula SAE is a competition in which students design and build an open wheel vehicle to compete against teams from around the world.
summary: Formula, SAE, Race, Racecar, Engineering, Automotive

name: French Club
description: The French Club meets regularly to engage in fun activities related to the French language and Francophone civilizations and cultures. French is not required to attend events, but speaking French is not prohibited. Events include movie nights and tutoring.
summary: A club for students interested in Francophone cultures and the French language.

name: Future Alumni Association
description: Future Alumni Association is a social networking organization. It is an open-membership organization for all enrolled students at The University of Alabama. FAA gives students the opportunity to make friends, get free stuff and have fun. It is the largest student organization on campus with more than 1500 members. It is sponsored by The University of Alabama Alumni Association. It is $25 per year to join the Future Alumni Association. University of Alabama students are the future alumni of this great institution. Students are an important part in the future development of the Alabama Alumni Association. The Future Alumni Association is to build a strong relationship and bridge the gap between Alabama alumni and University of Alabama students.
summary: The Future Alumni Association is a student organization dedicated to fostering lifelong connections between students and alumni. FAA serves as a bridge between current University of Alabama students and the National Alumni Association.

name: Future Black Law Student Association
description: The Future Black Law Student Association (FBLSA) strives to provide efficient resources to African American students at the University of Alabama, as well as encouragement, mentorship, and guidance from legal professionals to prepare these students for their journey to law school.
summary: Future Black Law Student Association's aim is to assist students interested in a profession in law with networking opportunities, learning resources, and guidance to help them prepare for law school admissions.

name: Gamma Phi Beta
description: On November 11, 1874, the Gamma Phi Beta sorority was founded by four courageous women at Syracuse University. A little over a hundred years later, a group of women came together on Alabama’s campus, motivated by the same guiding principles of our founders. Together, they established the Epsilon Lambda chapter in 1989, and it has been a part of Alabama’s Greek life and campus community ever since.
summary: We will build confident women of character who celebrate sisterhood and make a difference in the world around us. Our mission is to consistently inspire the highest type of womanhood.

name: Gamma Sigma Epsilon
description: Gamma Sigma Epsilon is a national honorary society for students studying chemistry. The society was founded in 1919 at Davidson College in Georgia. There are currently over 40 chapters of Gamma Sigma Epsilon in 18 states.The UA chapter of Gamma Sigma Epsilon (Delta Beta) was founded in 1924 and is the longest continuously active chapter in the country. It has a long history of recognizing the outstanding students and faculty of the Department of Chemistry. Membership in Gamma Sigma Epsilon is available to any chemistry or chemical engineering major or minor who has completed at least 14 hours of chemistry courses at UA, has a 3.3 GPA in chemistry, and a 3.3 GPA overall. Induction of new members occurs during the spring semester. For more information, contact Dr. Kevin Shaughnessy, Gamma Sigma Epsilon faculty advisor.
summary: chemistry, academics, honor society

name: UA Geology Club
description: We are an undergraduate-graduate organization of like-minded outdoor enthusiasts, of which the majority of members also share an academic background in geological sciences. We hold social events, community service events, and professional development events.
summary: A student organization open to anyone who enjoys Geology, and most things outdoors. We are an outdoor based club with goals of taking trips to educationally friendly areas.

name: German Club
description:
The German Club at the University of Alabama is an affiliation of U.S. students interested in German culture and language, as well as exchange students from German speaking countries.
