Through a series of engagement initiatives, as well as efforts to support targeted student populations, Student Involvement seeks to make a large campus feel smaller.

Through involvement, students are connected with leadership opportunities that allow them to work toward their own specific goals.  As students embark on the journey of higher education, this office seek to provide them a variety of landmarks along their path, which are designed to ensure that they have a well-rounded academic experience.
summary: The Office of Student Involvement provides opportunites for each UA student to achieve personal excellence by encouraging open exploration and engagement with the campus learning environment and by fostering individual growth and development.

name: Blackburn Institute
description:
• The Blackburn Institute was created by the Division of Student Life of The University of Alabama and operates as a department under its oversight. Named in honor of Dr. John L. Blackburn, the Institute is his legacy and a testament to his lifetime commitment to student learning and leadership development, civic engagement, and the state of Alabama. The staff members, including the director and other support staff, of the Blackburn Institute are employed by the university, responsible for the institute’s daily operations, and accountable to the university. These individuals coordinate the curriculum, events, and programs for the benefit of the three constituent groups of the Blackburn Institute:

    1. The Advisory Board of the Blackburn Institute, which, acting through its officers and committees, advises the institute’s director on all issues regarding the institute.
    2. Alumni of the Blackburn Institute, called Blackburn Fellows, which are represented by the Fellows Involvement Network (FIN). The FIN promotes active participation in Blackburn Institute events and advances the mission of the institute by serving as a mechanism to connect fellows to each other, students, and the Advisory Board.
    3. Students of the Blackburn Institute are selected through a competitive application process each spring semester and participate in a one-year curriculum that introduces them to the state of Alabama, the institute’s values, the Blackburn community, and the skills necessary to create positive change. Students continue their involvement in this role until completion of their degree requirements and graduate with recognition as Blackburn Fellows.
summary: The Blackburn Institute is a network of engaged student and community leaders focused on a better Alabama and nation.

name: English Graduate Organization
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide support and opportunities (including travel and research funding) for members in the English graduate program as well as provide a social atmosphere to allow graduate students to form professional relationships with one another and those within their discipline. The English Graduate Organization was conceived in the spirit of bridging the gaps between the English department’s individual programs, providing support professionally and academically for its members, and supporting the department as a whole.
summary: This organization provides support and opportunities (including travel and research funding) for members in the English graduate program.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
description: Upsilon Pi Epsilon is the first and only, existing international honor society in the Computing and Information Disciplines. It has received endorsements from the two largest computer organizations in the world, the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and the IEEE Computer Society (IEEE-CS).  Qualified students are given a one-time only invitation to join UPE.
summary: An honor society for outstanding undergraduate and graduate students in Computer Science.

name: Italian Club
description: The UA Italian Club is dedicated to celebrating and promoting the rich traditions, values, and contributions of Italian and Italian-American communities. Our mission is to create a welcoming space where students can explore the beauty of Italian culture through its language, history, art, and customs. We honor the profound impact of Italian-Americans on the State of Alabama while fostering appreciation and awareness through cultural events, guest speakers, film nights, and more. Whether you have Italian roots or simply a passion for the culture, all are welcome to join us in building community and keeping Italian culture alive on campus.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to engage actively in Italian culture and to honor the impact Italian-Americans have had the State of Alabama and the South.

name: Collegiate National Association for Music Education
description: cNAfME aims to ensure every student has access to quality music instruction by advocating for music education and providing resources for educators, administrators, and parents. The organization also focuses on expanding equitable access to music education, fostering inclusivity, and empowering generations to create, perform, and respond to music.
summary: The mission of the National Association for Music Education is to advance music education by encouraging the study and making of music by all.

name: No Strings Attached
description: No Strings Attached is UA's first co-ed a cappella group. Started by members of the all male group Subject to Change, No Strings Attached performs at all sorts of functions and events, and performs a winter and spring concert for the general public. NSA sings a variety of music, from current pop to classic rock, all the way from country to spiritual. Check our Instagram for upcoming appearances and audition dates!
summary: The University of Alabama's first co-ed a cappella group

name: Sigma Tau Gamma
description: Sigma Tau Gamma was founded June 28th, 1920 by veterans of the First World War. They had seen the horrors of war, and wanted a place where they could share the common bond of peace. Here at the University of Alabama, we strive to continue the principles penned by the original seventeen members. We expect our members to exhibit qualities of manhood, brotherhood, and citizenship. Our purpose is to make a positive impact on the Capstone and the surrounding community.
summary: Sigma Tau Gamma is a social fraternity striving to instill in its members the values of manhood, brotherhood, and citizenship. We do not tolerate any forms of hazing; our members are family. We strive to make a positive impact on the Capstone.

name: Alpha Delta Phi
description: Alpha Delta Phi is a fraternity that aims to develop each brother morally, socially, and intellectually with a true philosophical spirit within an environment of fraternal brotherhood.
summary: Alpha Delta Phi is a fraternity that aims to develop each brother morally, socially, and intellectually with a true philosophical spirit within an environment of fraternal brotherhood.

name: Alpha Phi Sigma
description: The Alpha Rho Chapter of Alpha Phi Sigma National Criminal Justice Honor Society is devoted to promoting academic excellence and leadership through campus programs and community outreach. One of the primary goals of Alpha Phi Sigma is to get more undergraduate students thinking seriously about their post-college plans and understanding what they need to do to prepare for their careers in criminal justice or related fields. Meetings are held monthly to plan events and discuss issues and concerns about academic careers.
summary: The goal of Alpha Phi Sigma is to recognize and promote high academic achievement among actively participating members and to create a competitive atmosphere for further advancement of criminal justice students.

name: American Concrete Institute
summary: A non-profit technical and educational society. ACI is a forum for the discussion of all matters related to concrete and the development of solutions to problems.

name: Run UA
description:
REGISTER ON IMLEAGUES!

Whether you have never run a 5k before, or you are training for your next marathon, we have a spot for you at Run UA! Our goal is to help University of Alabama undergraduate students, graduate students, and faculty achieve their running goals. We give members the opportunity to make lasting friendships, find running partners of similar skill, and support each other along the way.

New to running? Our club is the perfect is the perfect place to start! All members have the option to attend weekly runs, social events, and races; however, no events are mandatory. The purpose of this club is not to create elite athletes, but to help each member achieve their own individual goals.

Run UA offers multiple weekly runs on campus, each with a route planned in advanced. All routes are posted on the Strava app so that you will always know the way — no runner left behind! Every run will have at least one club officer present who will know the routes and be available to answer any questions. We are here to support you in your running journey!

No running journey would be complete though without events to train for and to show off your hard work.  We participate in races at a variety of distances — 5k, 10k, half marathon, and marathon — giving members at all running levels the chance to compete. Many of these races are outside of Tuscaloosa, so of course we offer transportation.

The most exciting opportunities that we give our members are our "City" and "Beach" races. Each semester, we travel as a club to a location that is hosting a race event. Every runner is able to choose which distance they will compete in, ranging from a 5k to a full marathon. Past destinations have been Nashville, New Orleans, Gulf Shores, and Panama City Beach! Be sure to check our socials for the announcement of this semester's destination.

Interested in joining? We are excited to have you! Your next steps are to register on IMLeagues and pay the $40 dues. Upon completion, you will be added to the official 2025-2026 Run UA GroupMe that we use as our primary communication channel. Your membership will be valid for both the Fall and Spring semester and include this year's club t-shirt.

Please check us out on Instagram, Facebook, or STRAVA and feel free to contact us for more information!

 

REGISTER ON IMLEAGUES!
summary: Official Running Club at The University of Alabama. NIRCA Affiliated. Whether you're a novice or experienced runner, Run UA is an amazing opportunity to build long lasting friendships and achieve your running goals!

name: NMDP On Campus
description:
Join the social club that helps save lives! We are one of top recruitment NMDP (previously known as Be the Match) chapters in the nation and were selected as UA's Outstanding Service Organization for the 2022-2023 school year.

We partner with our NMDP national representative to plan and execute the responsibilities of the on-campus student organization:

  • We serve as a liaison between the school administration and NMDP.
  • Oversee and approve all school-related NMDP activities.
  • Educate students and the school community about the NMDP's mission to save lives through marrow transplantation.
  • As campus policy allows, receive training to plan and conduct fundraising efforts to help cover the costs of growing the NMDP Registry®.
summary: NMDP on Campus has one primary goal - saving lives! We are a student-run organization that is on the move to expand the reach of our life-saving mission by delivering cures for blood cancers through registration drives and fundraising.

name: The UA Club Golf Team
description: The UA Club Golf Team's mission is to provide non-varsity golfers the opportunity to continue their playing career in a competitive and friendly atmosphere, while maintaining focus on academics and emphasizing community outreach through golf and other initiatives.
summary: The UA Club Golf Team's mission is to provide non-varsity golfers the opportunity to continue their playing career in a competitive and friendly atmosphere...(click read more)

name: Beta Upsilon Chi
summary: Beta Upsilon Chi is a national Christian fraternity that exists for the purpose of establishing brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ.

name: Radio Television Digital News Association Capstone Chapter
summary: RTDNA is dedicated to setting standards for newsgathering and reporting. Although news techniques and technologies are constantly changing, RTDNA's commitment to encouraging excellence in the electronic journalism industry remains the same.

name: HOSA - Future Health Professionals
description: The University of Alabama HOSA- future health professionals, welcomes all students passionate about health-related fields. The organization is dedicated to empowering future healthcare professionals through a variety of engaging programs, including hands-on experiences, competitive events, and networking opportunities with industry leaders. Members gain invaluable skills in health education, advocacy, and leadership, all while fostering a supportive community. Whether you’re interested in medicine, nursing, public health, or any other healthcare profession, UAHOSA offers the resources and connections to help you succeed. Join us to enhance your knowledge, develop your skills, and prepare for a fulfilling career in healthcare!
summary: HOSA is a national student-lead organization that provides opportunities for leadership development of all prospective health care students.

name: Alabama Civil Rights & Civil Liberties Law Review
description: The ACRCL is a legal journal of the University of Alabama.  Its board of editors is composed solely of law students from the School of Law.  As Volume 1 of the Journal was only published in 2011, the ACRCL is the newest legal journal at the law school, but is quickly growing in membership and popularity due to its focus on social issues that continually affect not only Alabama, but also have influence on a national and global level. In addition to its printed publication, the Journal also regularly updates an online civil rights blog with current legal issues arising in the area of civil rights and civil liberties.
summary: A legal journal of the University of Alabama School of Law which focuses on subjects and topics that affect civil rights and civil liberties

name: The College Xperience
description:
The College Xperience program is designed to empower all students by providing the tools, resources, and mentorship necessary to navigate and excel in higher education. Its purpose is to ensure that students not only access college but also thrive throughout their academic journey, equipping them with the skills and opportunities needed for personal, academic, and professional success.

Through internships with Fortune 500 companies, networking events, financial literacy workshops, trips and academic support, the program aims to:

  1. Bridge the gap between high school and higher education.
  2. Foster a community of support that encourages students to graduate on time.
  3. Provide pathways to postgraduate degrees or direct entry into competitive job markets.
  4. Inspire students to aspire to leadership roles within their fields.

The ultimate goal of the College Xperience is to transform potential into achievement, ensuring that students from all communities can redefine their futures and contribute meaningfully to society.
summary: The College Xperience empowers all students to excel in college and beyond by providing mentorship, resources, and career opportunities. It bridges gaps, fosters academic success, and prepares students for leadership and impactful careers.

name: The Well Tuscaloosa
description:
We want The Well at Calvary Tuscaloosa to connect you to the inter-generational church and a community of other college students! Join us Sundays at 9:45 or 11am for our main worship service and Wednesday at 8:00pm for The Well College Service!

We also have Life Groups that are the heart of The Well as we believe the Christian life is better together! Life groups meet on Monday and Tuesday Night (unless noted otherwise) at 8pm for both freshman and upperclassmen. 

The Well wants to be a force for good both in the church and out in our community. Learn about our Ministry Teams to find your place to serve with us!
summary:
The Well is the college ministry of Calvary Tuscaloosa located on the campus of the University of Alabama.
We offer several events to attend, including The Well worship service on Wednesday nights at 8 pm, one of the largest worship gatherings at UA!

name: Pre-Pharmacy Society
summary: The pre-pharmacy society's goal is to bring together students who are planning on applying to pharmacy school. We have admission representatives from various schools, pharmacist, and other speakers at our meeting to better inform students about pharmacy.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description:
Alpha Epsilon Pi was founded to provide opportunities for the Jewish college man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience. We have maintained the integrity of our purpose by strengthening our ties to the Jewish community and serving as a link between high school and career.

Our heritage stems from one source: young Jewish men banding together in allegiance. The role of Alpha Epsilon Pi has expanded since its inception in 1913. Initially, the Jewish fraternity served as a brotherhood of young men who came from similar religious backgrounds and who had experienced the same prejudices against their religious beliefs. Alpha Epsilon Pi soon broadened its role to include serving as the living quarters for some of its members. The fraternity became a home away from home, providing the same stabilizing and guiding values that students previously gained from their families. Armed with these values, Alpha Epsilon Pi faced changing conditions on the college campus and survived. Today, Jewish students search out Alpha Epsilon Pi because it is a Jewish Fraternity. In the fraternity’s 98-year history, over 93,000 men have worn the badge of Alpha Epsilon Pi and each year, over 2,500 undergraduates perform the Ritual of Initiation, which remains the same ritual adopted decades ago.

Perhaps of greater importance, Alpha Epsilon Pi develops leadership for the future of the American Jewish community. Tomorrow’s Jewish leaders are in our chapters today. These are the young men who must be counted upon to support Jewish causes and to prepare to be one of tomorrow’s Jewish leaders, so that they may aid themselves, their family, their community, and their people. Those students who enter the mainstream of non-Jewish life on the campus are far more likely to assimilate and to forsake their heritage. Working together with the Foundation for Jewish Campus Life/International Hillel, Alpha Epsilon Pi can play a vital role in helping reverse the growing trend among our young people to abandon Judaism at this critical time.

Throughout our history, the fraternity setting has served as a “learning laboratory”, a testing ground for young men who later become leaders in business, education, government, religion, and science. A goal of our fraternity is to help each student to develop character, to learn responsibility, and to develop a proper set of values through living together in brotherhood. Alpha Epsilon Pi prepares young men for their role in life as responsible citizens.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi is a Jewish fraternity, though non-discriminatory and open to all who are willing to espouse its purpose and values.

name: Bryan K. Fair Chapter of the Black Law Students Association
description: BLSA is an organization designed to support and promote diversity within the law school and the legal profession.
summary: BLSA is an organization designed to support and promote diversity within the law school and legal profession.

name: Professional Women in Communication and Business
description: Professional Women in Communication & Business (PWCB) is an organization devoted to developing successful leaders on campus and in the workplace. PWCB encourages networking, helps to better develop professional etiquette and promotes women empowerment! We meet every other Tuesday night to help each other to be the best we can be. Hope to see you soon!
summary: PWCB is an organization devoted to developing successful leaders on campus and in the workplace. PWCB helps to promote professional development and gives members a chance to network with other like-minded, motivated individuals.

name: Crimson Clay
description:
Crimson Clay brings together students who share a common enthusiasm for working with clay. Whether focused on functional or sculpture clay art, each member works toward a greater understanding of the material and processes. Every semester, members seek to benefit the larger group and broader community by building relationships with local partners. This is accomplished, in part, through the exhibition of student artwork as well as fund-raising events, including Empty Bowls, the Druid City Arts Festival and the Kentuck Festival of the Arts. As a result, students receive funding assistance from the club to attend conferences or ceramic-based research activities throughout the United States.

-Fall 2025 Studio 101: TBA

-Dues: $50.00 per semester due at the first meeting

-Location: BOM 5 room 114, 600 Margaret Drive

-Meeting times: Sundays 10 - 6
summary: We are an organization focused around bringing students with an interest in the inner workings of the field of ceramics together. Each member works towards the benefit of the group as a whole through individual practice with clay materials.

name: Slavic Cultures Club
description:
The Slavic Cultures Club is designed to provide the students of the University of Alabama with a forum to share the languages, histories, and cultures of Eastern, Central, and Southern Europe, and of Central Asia. The club hosts regular meetings to explore and promote these cultures through film viewings, traditional cuisines, games, sports, guest lecturers, and more; and to develop language skills by connecting native/heritage speakers with language learners of every level.

*Note: Students enrolled in the Russian program and in Ukrainian classes within the Department of Modern Languages and Classics are encouraged to join SCC to enrich and reinforce what they learn in class. However, it is not necessary to be enrolled in a Slavic-related class, nor to have any previous language skills, in order to belong to SCC and to participate in club activities. So if you are interested in Slavic cultures, join us!
summary: Promoting interest in Slavic, Eastern European, and Central Asian cultures and languages at the University of Alabama.

name: The Distance Student Dietetic Association
description: The Distance Student Dietetic Association will provide nutrition information and promote sound nutrition practices among communities and peers. It will help distance nutrition students become familiar with the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics and other related professional organizations. The organization will facilitate nutrition distance students meeting other nutrition distance students to foster camaraderie, develop leadership skills and support each other through virtual study groups and projects. The Distance SDA will also promote community volunteer opportunities.
summary: Organization for University of Alabama distance nutrition students interested in networking with other distance students, faculty and professionals in the nutrition field.

name: Alabama Ballroom Dance Team
description: The Alabama Ballroom Dance Team is a university-based Club Sport dedicated to providing a supportive community to students that promotes physical health and awareness; positive emotional expression; personal and professional confidence; and the direction, motivation, and achievement of personal goals through dance. With technical training by Tuscaloosa's most prominent competitive coaches, the team competes in both American and International styles of dance, attending up to eight competitions per year.
summary: The Alabama Ballroom Dance Team is an organization that trains in both American and International styles of ballroom dance throughout the year to prepare for ballroom competitions across the country.

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon
description: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a social and professional sorority for promoting women in engineering and technical science fields. We promote ideals of friendship, leadership, and professionalism.
summary: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a social and professional sorority for promoting women in engineering and technical science fields. We promote ideals of friendship, leadership, and professionalism.

name: Avanti Alumni Network
description: The Avanti Alumni Network is meant to connect former University of Alabama Avantis and build a strong network of alumni to help with professional networking, team recruitment and maintaining teammate relationships! After all, you're an Avanti for LIFE!
summary: The Avanti Alumni Network is meant to connect former University of Alabama Avantis and build a strong network of alumni to help with professional networking, team recruitment and maintaining teammate relationships! After all, you're an Avanti for LIFE!

name: Rising Tide Film Festival (formerly Black Warrior)
description: The Rising Tide Film Festival, formerly known as the Black Warrior Film Festival, was founded in 2013. The Rising Tide Film Festival is an independent, student-led organization that strives to provide an outlet for students to showcase their films to a large audience, as well as to create a film festival environment where student filmmakers can meet and learn from industry professionals, network with other filmmakers and celebrate film. Along with this Rising Tide hosts events throughout the year to create a place for film lovers to connect.
summary: The Rising Tide Film Festival strives to provide an outlet for students to showcase their films, as well as to create a film festival environment for The University of Alabama. We showcase weekly student film screenings and monthly social events.

name: Philosophy Club
description:
The club generally meets bi-weekly (once every two weeks) in order to discuss a pre-selected topic/theme. Topics include broad and specific philosophical issues as well as the practical application of philosophy. After the main topic is discussed, any other topic may be brought up for additional discussion. Drinks and food are provided at each meeting.


Meetings are usually held in ten Hoor 352a on Thursdays from 7 pm to 8:30 pm. Meetings are sometime held on different days in relation to Philosophy Today talks (philosophy lectures).

As a whole the club is geared towards the cultivation of interdisciplinary views in relation to philosophy. We encourage all students from all majors to come question, debate, and learn with us!

Information about Philosophy Today talks can be found here:  http://philosophy.ua.edu/about/lecture-series/
summary: Philosophy Club is a group that strives to promote unique discussion of philosophical issues among the student body by engaging with both veterans of the field and newcomers alike.

name: Pre-Physical Therapy Society
description: Pre-Physical Therapy Society provides students with essential information, knowledge, and assistance towards reaching their goals in pursuing a career in physical therapy. Monthly meetings are held during which speakers who are professionals in the field of physical therapy come and share valuable insight and information to help prepare members for the PTCAS application process. These speakers also teach students about the facts and opportunities a doctorate degree in physical therapy has to offer them. Pre-PT Society strives to be active in the community, social media, and with other organizations on the University of Alabama’s campus.
summary: Pre-Physical Therapy Society provides students with essential information, knowledge, and assistance towards reaching their goals in pursuing a career in physical therapy.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
summary: A group of young men dedicating to championing a lifelong commitment to brotherhood, learning, ethical leadership and exemplary character.

name: Special Olympics College at UA
summary: The purpose of Special Olympics College at UA is to engage college students to enhance the lives of people with intellectual disabilities through sports, friendship, advocacy, and other activities.

name: The Undergraduate Social Work Organization
description: USWO is a pre-professional organization that provides an opportunity for social work majors and minors to  participate in meetings, activities, and programs related to the School of Social Work and community service and outreach. We work to provide additional resources for those students pursuing a degree or future career in the field of social work. We engage with faculty of the School of Social Work for guidance as well as members of the community to perform and carry out the values of the School of Social Work.
summary: USWO is a pre-professional organization that provides an opportunity for social work majors and minors to participate in meetings, activities, and programs related to the School of Social Work and community service and outreach.

name: The Alabama Clay Shooting Team
description: To provide students at the University of Alabama with the opportunity to compete against other universities around the country in shotgun events. To stimulate public awareness of shotgun sports as a college sport by participating in local, regional, and national college-level shooting tournaments.  To improve our skills as marksmen through fellowship and exchange of shotgun knowledge.  To function in accordance with the principles and purposes of the National Rifle Association and the National Skeet Shooting Association as well as our other governing bodies.
summary: This is a competitive clay shooting team for the University of Alabama. We compete in collegiate shotgun sports competitions throughout the U.S.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life
description:
The Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life, a department within the Division of Student Life, is dedicated to the growth and success of the Greek community and to the individual development of fraternity and sorority members. Specifically, the Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life advises and provides guidance to the fraternities and sororities associated with the four Greek governing councils: the Alabama Panhellenic Association (APA), the Alabama National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC), the Alabama Interfraternity Council (IFC), and the Alabama United Greek Council (UGC).

The Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life strives to offer chapters on-campus support services. Staff can serve as a liaison between faculty, alumni advisors, inter/national offices, and parents, as well as work to develop values-based leadership development programming. The office also offers training on chapter operations, diversity, risk management, health and safety, and membership recruitment. As Greek life aims to produce well-rounded students, staff can also assist in monitoring the academic performance of fraternity and sorority members.
summary: The Office of Fraternity and Sorority Life, a department within the Division of Student Life, is dedicated to the growth and success of the Greek community and to the individual development of fraternity and sorority members.

name: Corps of Cadets
summary: The purpose of the Corps of Cadets is to provide an environment that aids students in becoming better leaders by applying mentally and physically demanding courses that require teamwork and technical skills.

name: Tuska UAV Design Team
description: Tuska UAV Design Team is a student-led team at the University of Alabama. Our independent club designs multirotor and fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) for disaster relief, photogrammetry, and other applications. As a team we walk through the entire design process from concept to modeling to fabrication and finally testing our custom planes and rotorcraft. Our team includes several majors in engineering and beyond, representing a range of backgrounds with different levels of experience. Tuska UAV seeks to encourage learning about drone technology through hands-on experience in a creative environment.
summary: We are an interdisciplinary team at the University of Alabama developing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV's)  for disaster relief, photogrammetry, and other experimental applications.

name: Alabama Rocketry Association
description:
The Alabama Rocketry Association (ARA) is established to develop impactful engineering experiences by giving members the opportunity to build world-class student-developed rockets through participating in three national rocketry competitions and one experimental team - Friends of Amateur Rocketry Dollar-Per-Foot Challenge (Liquid Propellant Rocketry), Spaceport America Cup (Solid Propellant Rocketry), and NASA Student Launch (Solid Propellant Rocketry), and the XPRT (Experimental Rocketry Team), as well as providing experiences and assisting individuals within our local community in getting L1, and L2 NAR high power certifications!

​ARA is one of the largest engineering teams and the only rocketry team on-campus, servicing over 150 active undergraduate and graduate students from the College of Engineering, Arts & Science, and Business. ARA is open to students from any major or discipline, making it accessible to everyone on campus passionate about rocketry. Through ARA you will be able to excel and meet new people while working on projects that are unique and innovative in the scientific community.

Outreach and educating the next generation of STEM Leaders is also a big part of ARA. We achieve our outreach goals through the Tuscaloosa Rocketry Challenge where we teach middle schoolers the basics of rocketry and help organize a water bottle rocket competition, as well as local and state-wide science fairs and the Science Olympiad. 

Stop by Hardaway 150B to see our lab and get involved today!
summary: The Alabama Rocketry Association (ARA) is the University of Alabama's rocket team dedicated to providing world-class collegiate engineering experiences for students by competing in national and international rocketry competitions and representing UA!

name: University Programs
description:
University Programs creates engaging events where all students feel welcome, find support, and build community.

University Programs is committed to serving all students through on-campus programming for students, by students. UP is a Student Life department with a dedicated team of over 30 students who plan, promote, and execute over 150 events per year.

Here, you can learn about our event series, how to best stay informed about events on campus, and how you can reach out to us if you want to get involved. For the most up-to-date information, follow us Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook. We look forward to seeing you at an event soon!
summary: University Programs fosters community and maximizes student development through campus-wide events implemented by UA students.

name: Capstone Church College Ministry
description:
The purpose of this association is to grow the body of Christ and to equip college students to live out the gospel in every part of their life with the intention and purpose of growing the kingdom of God on the campus of the university.

 

https://www.capstonechurch.org/ministries/college

 

-Christ-Centered
-Community Driven
-Compassionately Minded
 
Join us at 7PM on Tuesdays in the chapel!
Follow us on Instagram for information about other events. 
Wednesday nights at 8pm college service
summary:
Working to grow the Community of Christ on the campus of UA. 
We are Christ centered, community driven, and compassionately minded.

name: Transfer Law Students Association
description: The purpose of the TLSA is to welcome transfer, international, and visiting law students to The University of Alabama School of Law and support them in their transition to the law school. The TLSA will serve as a network for information, friendship, and mentoring.
summary: The purpose of the TLSA is to welcome transfer, visiting and international law students to the UA School of Law and support them in their transition to the law school. The TLSA will serve as a network for information, friendship, and mentoring.

name: Discovery Buddies
description: Discovery Buddies is a mentoring program which strives to spark children's interest in science and provide them with role models who they can look up to and try to emulate. Each child is paired with a University of Alabama student mentor. They work together on a weekly basis to perform fun science experiements and demonstations. These demonstrations cover basic scientific concepts, promote hands on learning, and serve as a supplement to the material in the children’s current science classes. The long term goal of this program is to motivate the elementary students to pursue careers in higher education and not just science while also developing a curiosity for the world around them.
summary: Discovery Buddies is a mentoring program which sparks children's interest in science and their own curiosity about the world. Elementary and University of Alabama students work together to perform fun science experiments in a weekly after school program.

name: Crossroad College Ministry
description: Enter by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and there are many who go in by it. Because straight is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few here be that find it. Matt. 7:13-14
summary: The purpose of Crossroad College Ministry is to lift the name of Jesus Christ, while encouraging and showing students how to use the Bible to deal with the everyday issues they face in college.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: The Pre-Dental Society calls all University of Alabama students interested in a dental career to come together and learn from each others' progress. The group meets weekly and arranges numerous guest speakers to visit the group each semester. The students are given the opportunity to become involved with shadowing opportunities, as well as touring UAB School of Dentistry. The friendships created through the Pre-Dental Society are the beginning steps to walking your journey into dental school!
summary: The Pre-Dental Society is a student lead organization for students interested in the dental career to gather information and further their knowledge.

name: Alabama Student Rural Health Association
description: The purpose of the ASRHA is to educate students and Alabamians regarding rural health issues in the state by addressing pertinent health issues through activities, workshops, volunteering, and information distribution through multiple media outlets. In addition, these activities will support the mission of the Alabama Rural Health Association which represents the geographically diverse regions of Alabama and a variety of for profit and non-profit entities.
summary: To educate students and Alabamians regarding rural health issues in the state by addressing pertinent health issues through activities, workshops, volunteering, and information distribution.

name: Spanish Club: Los Estudiantes de Alabama sin Fronteras
description: Spanish Club: Los Estudiantes de Alabama sin Fronteras is a Spanish language focused organization whose mission is to promote student engagement with Spanish language and culture on campus, as well as involvement within the Tuscaloosa Latino community in order to break down cultural and language barriers. We host a weekly "Charlas" or Spanish Conversation Hour, that allows students to practice their Spanish and learn about different cultures. Throughout the semester we host other events, ranging from movie nights to multicultural trivia. Students of all Spanish-speaking abilities are welcome to participate.
summary: Spanish Club: Los Estudiantes de Alabama sin Fronteras is a cultural and community service organization that strives to promote student involvement in Spanish language and culture, as well as involvement in the Tuscaloosa Latino community.

name: Scholars for Community Outreach, Partnership, and Engagement
description: Scholars for Community Outreach, Partnership and Engagement (SCOPE) is an initiative of the Center for Community-Based Partnerships to provide a framework for connecting students who have an interest in and commitment to community engaged scholarship.  SCOPE membership is open to currently enrolled University of Alabama undergraduate and graduate students from all disciplines.
summary: The purpose of SCOPE is to enhance and support the capacity of University of Alabama students to collaborate with faculty and community partners in achieving mutually beneficial outcomes that address challenges facing communities.

name: Omega Chi Epsilon, Tau Chapter
description:
Omega Chi Epsilon is the National Honor Society for Chemical Engineering. The Society promotes high scholarship, encourages original investigation in chemical engineering and recognizes the valuable traits of character, integrity and leadership. It serves both undergraduate and graduate students and fosters meaningful student-faculty dialogue.

The name is based upon our motto: Ode Chrototos Eggegramai which means In this Society, professionalism is engraved in our minds. The letters OXE, or their Greek equivalent of WCE, represent Order of Chemical Engineers.

The key represents the objectives of Omega Chi Epsilon. The four arms of the Maltese cross represent the first four objectives:

1. RECOGNITION to recognize excellence in chemical engineering
2. INVESTIGATION to promote original investigation and innovation in chemical engineering
3. SERVICE to provide service to the chemical engineering department or school and its student body
4. COMRADESHIP to promote comradeship among chemical engineers

These four are bound intimately together by the fifth,

5. PROFESSIONALISM to promote honesty, integrity and social responsibility: the hallmarks of professional ethics

represented by the circular maroon crest in the shape of a globe, reminding us of the world-wide scope of chemical engineering. The gold Greek letters, Omega, Chi, Epsilon, are inscribed on a white band encircling the globe. The crossed retorts, the integral sign, and the bolt of lightning represent the three main tools of the chemical engineer: chemistry, mathematics, and physics.

WCE is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies. The Society also has many common goals with the American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE); we support the AIChE with funds for student awards. We further share many goals with the American Society for Engineering Education. WCE has over 60 active university chapters and total membership of nearly 20,000 men and women. While the chapters are primarily composed of Junior and Senior students, it is also open to graduate students and faculty. In recent years, 2-4 new chapters have been chartered each year.
summary: Omega Chi Epsilon is the National Honor Society for Chemical Engineering.

name: Alabama Boxing Club
description: The Univeristy of Alabama Boxing Club is a group students who are interested in learning, practicing, and perfecting the art of boxing. All skills levels are accepted whether that be multiple years or none at all. The club practices three times each week to get in shape, practice old skills, and learn new ones.
summary: A group of students from all experience levels who are interested in learning, practicing, and perfecting the art of boxing through competition and fitness.

name: OutLaw
description: OUTLaw is an organization of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, asexual and ally members of the University of Alabama Law School community. Our goals are to provide a community and social network for LGBTQIA-identified people within the Law School, to provide opportunities and connections for member of the Law School community who are interested in LGBTQIA+ rights, to bring the Law School community’s attention to issues of special concern to the LGBT community, and to serve as a bridge between Alabama students, law school alumni, and the legal profession at large.
summary: OutLaw is an organization of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, asexual, and ally members of the University of Alabama Law School community.

name: Veterans in Business
summary: Veterans in Business provides veterans and service members a business professional community that provides resources, mentorship, networking opportunities, and professional development while at The University of Alabama.

name: Alabama Club Field Hockey
description: The University of Alabama Club Field Hockey Team.
summary: The University of Alabama Club Field Hockey Team

name: Alta Marea Winterguard
description: Alta Marea Winterguard is a competitive group that combines dance with the skill of spinning color guard equipment. Alta Marea competes in the SAPA Circuit and Winter Guard International. Alta Marea is an Independent Open winterguard and travels around the Southeast to compete with other winterguards from around the country.
summary: Alta Marea Winterguard is a competitive group that combines dance with the skill of spinning color guard equipment. Alta Marea competes in the SCGC Circuit and Winter Guard International.

name: Outdoor Adventures Club
description:
To become a member of the OAC, please contact us at alabamaoac@gmail.com to learn more about our club and updated information about what we do. After paying membership dues, we will add you as a member and you are welcome to come on any of our trips.
 

The Outdoor Adventures Club is a student organization at the University of Alabama. Our purpose is to bring together interested members of the University and surrounding community who enjoy being in the outdoors and spending time with like-minded individuals.  We help these people come together and provide a place to plan out trips, meet new people, learn new skills, and try new types of activities! We do our best to facilitate all types of trips that our members want to take from hiking and backpacking to rafting and kayaking.
summary: This club provides students the opportunity to explore Alabama and the surrounding areas with an emphasis on spending time outdoors and with great people.

name: Crimson Smash Club
description:
Crimson Smash Club is your premier source for competitive Super Smash Bros. at the University of Alabama! Whether you're a seasoned pro or want to learn a unique and fast-paced fighting game, join us for weekly gaming sessions and monthly tournaments.

We focus on competitive Melee and Smash Ultimate, but we appreciate all kinds of Smash. Feel free to come out and see us, even if you only play casually!
summary: Crimson Smash Club is your premier source for competitive Super Smash Bros. at the University of Alabama! Whether you're a seasoned pro or want to learn a unique and fast-paced fighting game, join us for weekly gaming sessions and monthly tournaments.

name: National Society of Leadership & Success
description:
The Society is the nation's largest leadership honor society.  Students are selected by their college for membership based on either academic standing or leadership potential.  Candidacy is a nationally recognized achievement of honorable distinction.  With 506 chapters, the Society currently has 590,592 members nationwide. 

In addition to honorable distinction, the Society provides a step-by-step program for members to build their leadership skills through participation at their campus or online.  Upon completion of the program, members receive their leadership certificate and take their place among the top student leaders at their campus and across the country.  Members are able to list their affiliation on all statements of personal accomplishment, including their resume.  

Membership is for life and provides access to benefits including scholarships and awards, exclusive on-campus events, employer recruitment through an online job bank, and discounts on computers, textbooks, grad school prep courses, insurance and much more.
summary: "We Build Leaders Who Make a Better World". The National Society of Leadership & Success is an organization that helps people discover and achieve their goals. Starting with a handful of chapters, NSLS is now the largest leadership society in the nation!

name: Kappa Kappa Psi: National Honorary Band Fraternity
description:
Founded on the campus of Oklahoma State University on November 27, 1919, Kappa Kappa Psi is a guiding light with which band students can grow, learn, and develop themselves and their band programs. Kappa Kappa Psi is an organization of opportunity and quality, brimming with historic, well-developed leadership and membership programming that students use to become great leaders, better band members and better adapted young adults prepared for what lies ahead of them.

Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of his/her band. Our goals are to provide the band not only with organized and concentrated service activities, but to give our membership valid and wholesome experiences in organization, leadership and social contacts. The honorary nature of membership is based on our premise that “it is an honor to be selected to serve” this band, its department of music, its sponsoring institution, and the cause of band music in the nation’s colleges and universities.

The educational value to the student of participation in a smaller group’s activities cannot be overemphasized. Experience in planning, organizing and carrying through projects to benefit the larger band organization are necessary if a well-rounded and responsible student leader is to be trained. The fraternal group, as embodied by our active chapters, provides this needed training group for the student by establishing both worthy goals and worthwhile guideposts for a meaningful college experience.

Membership in Kappa Kappa Psi thus becomes an educational and service activity as well as social one. And it is this kind of activity that enriches the band student’s life and sharpens an appreciation for both the best in Music and what it takes to achieve it. It is to this end that our National Fraternity is dedicated, and we cordially invite your participation in this effort.
summary: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of his/her band.

name: Graduate Social Work Organization
summary: The purpose of this graduate organization is advocate, promote, and research social injustice issues that affect individuals in the surrounding communities that are oppressed and vulnerable to help meet the needs of the individuals in the community.

name: Alabama Rock Climbing Club
description: The University of Alabama's passionate group of rock climbers! From indoor routes to outdoor adventures, our members can get a fantastic introduction to the sport! We participate in bouldering, sport climbing, and pursuing opportunities to give back to the growing local climbing community. We'll travel to various indoor gyms for fun or for competition! Our outdoor trips can range from climbing day trips, overnight camping, or trail restoration!
summary: Climbers of all backgrounds and skill levels are welcome to join in order to network with fellow climbers to go on outdoor trips, compete in competitions, and to give back to the local climbing community.

name: Alabama SUP Club
description:
Welcome to The Alabama SUP Club! Stand Up Paddleboarding, or SUP for short, is one of the fastest growing sports in the nation. Stand Up Paddleboarding is popular for many reasons: it's easy to learn, connects you with the outdoors, can be done on any body of water, and is a full-body workout. Stretch your body and mind with SUP yoga, hit the water running with SUP race training, take a journey through nature with a SUP tour, ride a wave, catch a fish, sit and relax, and fellowship with people.

SUP is not just a hobby - it is a lifestyle. 

We are a group of students who are passionate about SUP. Join the movement today! Anyone is welcome at our meetings and boards are provided; get connected by following us on social media or by joining our GroupMe.

See you on the water!
summary: The University of Alabama SUP Club is an organization of people united by a love for the water and Stand Up Paddleboarding. We supply the boards!

name: First Year Experience
description: First Year Experience (FYE) is committed to supporting academic and social transition to The University of Alabama. We define first-year student as any undergraduate student attending the University for the first time. FYE seeks to help students understand expectations associated with being a member of the Capstone and educate first-year students about various support services on campus. FYE provides support, resources, and programs for freshman and transfer students as they build the foundation of their Capstone experience.
summary: First Year Experience (FYE) is an area of Student Life committed to supporting academic and social transition to The University of Alabama. We define first-year student as any undergraduate student attending UA and transfer students.

name: Alabama Chem-E Car Team
description: The Alabama Chem-E Car Team designs, builds, and tests a miniature car that starts and stops solely on chemical reactions. Members compete in a regional competition each spring in hopes of qualifying for the national competition each fall.
summary: The members of this club must design, build, and test a miniature car that starts and stops solely on chemical reactions.  The car competes in a regional competition each spring, in hopes of qualifying for the national competition each fall.

name: Bama ASL Club
description: To join our email list, email us bamaaslclub@gmail.comfor information on our organization meetings, events, volunteering, and other opportunities to study and support American Sign Language and Deaf culture.
summary: Bama ASL Club is an organization focused on Deaf culture! Its mission is to connect the hearing and Deaf communities through language and cultural education, community service, and partnerships with other groups.

name: Language, Literature, and Linguistics Graduate Student Organization
description: The Organization is for Graduate students of the University of Alabama who are interested in organizing conferences related to language, linguistics, literature, and cultures.
summary: Graduate students of the University of Alabama interested in organizing conferences related to language, linguistics, literature, and culture

name: Rho Lambda
description:
The Greek letters Rho Lambda stand for Panhellenic Leadership. Those who are leaders light a torch in the darkness for others to follow, and the flame is the symbol of Rho Lambda. Membership is available to any sorority woman (Panhellenic, UGC, NPHC) as she meets the membership selection criteria.

The purpose of Rho Lambda is to honor those women within the sorority community who have exhibited the highest qualities of leadership and service to their Greek community and sorority. They are women who have furthered the ideals and principles of the Greek community throughout their years of sorority affiliation.
summary: Rho Lambda is the National Sorority Leadership Recognition Society. This society honors women within the sorority community who have exhibited the highest qualities of leadership and service to their Greek community and their sorority.

name: Turning Point USA
description:
Turning Point USA, Inc. is organized and operated exclusively for educational and charitable purposes in accordance with Section 501(c)(3), to empower informed civic and cultural engagement grounded in American exceptionalism and a positive spirit of action. Turning Point USA guides citizens through development of knowledge, skills, values, and motivation, so they can meaningfully engage in their communities to restore traditional American values like patriotism, respect for life, liberty, family, and fiscal responsibility.

TPUSA activists strive to:

  • Educate students about the importance of freedom, free markets, and limited government. With innovative marketing techniques and strategic outreach methods, TPUSA has expanded our presence to include 3,500 high school and college campuses nationwide.

  • Identify student activists across the country who believe in limited government and individual liberty.

  • Empower young activists to get involved in the fight for free markets and limited government. Through building strong campus networks, organizing conferences, and equipping activists with the knowledge and strategies needed to combat the left, TPUSA empowers young people to make a difference within their community.

  • Organize young activists in chapters and networks across the country. TPUSA believes there is strength in organization, and our activists work tirelessly to build grassroots networks on every high school and college campus in America.

  • Mobilize students who believe in free markets. TPUSA mobilizes its networks to get involved in activism, issue advocacy, public policy education, and grassroots organization.

  • Register students to vote. TPUSA has helped thousands of college students apply for voter registration and access absentee ballots.
summary: The fastest-growing and most innovative youth movement in the country. Free Markets, Limited government, Fiscal Responsibility. Join Us Today!

name: Greek Programming Board
description: Greek Programming Board is a unified body composed of membership from all four Greek governing councils (APA, IFC, NPHC, and UGC) whose main goal is to create dynamic programming contributing to four key pillars of development for Greek members: education, leadership, service, and social engagement. The Greek Programming Board aims to cultivate the professional, academic, and social development of all Greek members on campus, while inspiring students to unite to serve the local community - all through creating unique programming and opportunities both on and off campus.  The UA Greek Programming Board also advocates for all Greek members, fosters relationships among Greek students from all four councils, and develops pride in the Greek system as a whole.
summary: UA Greek Programming Board will unite the Greek community at UA by upholding the 4 pillars of education, leadership, service, and social engagement through creating unique programming while promoting unity and diversity among all four governing councils.

name: The University of Alabama EcoCAR Team
description: The University of Alabama is competing in the EcoCAR EV Challenge, a premier collegiate automotive engineering project sponsored by The. U.S. Department of Energy, General Motors, MathWorks, and managed by Argonne National Laboratory. Over the four years of the competition (2022-2026) the UA EcoCAR Team will work to redesign a Cadillac LYRIQ to meet the changing scope of the automotive engineering industry toward vehicle electrification. See a tour of our lab here!
summary: The University of Alabama is competing in the EcoCAR EV Challenge, a competition organized by Argonne National Lab and sponsored by The U.S. Department of Energy, MathWorks, and General Motors to redesign and optimize an electric production vehicle.

name: Ducks Unlimited Chapter at The University of Alabama
description: The Ducks Unlimited Chapter at The University of Alabama is one of the elite collegiate chapters of ducks unlimited ranked with all other collegiate Ducks Unlimited Chapters. We hope to help the national organization of Ducks Unlimited to preserve ducks and wetlands for generations forward. The national organization was founded in 1937 and leads the world in wetland and duck habitat management.
summary: The Crimson Tide Chapter of Ducks Unlimited is one of the elite collegiate chapters of ducks unlimited ranking with in the top ten of all Ducks Unlimited Chapters. We hope to help the national organization of ducks unlimited to preserve ducks for others.

name: The Classics Academy Club
description: During our meetings, celebrations, and scheduled talks by various speakers, we engage the community of Classicists and lovers of the arts. This club is open to all who are interested! Not just students who have taken a class through the Modern Languages and Classics departments.
summary: The Greco-Roman Social Club is a social organization that promotes interest in the language, literature, art, philosophers, history, and archaeology of the Classical world and is open to all majors.

name: Biology Graduate Student Association
description: BGSA serves to represent the graduate students in the UA Department of Biological Sciences. We work to foster community, support one another through graduate school, and to bring graduate student issues to the attention of the faculty when needed.
summary: BGSA serves to represent the graduate students in the UA Department of Biological Sciences. We work to foster community, support one another through graduate school, and to bring graduate student issues to the attention of the faculty when needed.

name: Not On My Campus
description: We are connected by our passion for The University of Alabama and our desire to make a difference in our community and throughout the world. We have experienced either directly or indirectly, the devastating effects of sexual assault on our campus and realize it is our responsibility to step up and take responsibility for our student culture.
summary: Not On My Campus is a student-led movement working to end the silence surrounding sexual assault and to create a safe environment on campus.

name: UA Greek Ambassadors
description:
The Greek Ambassadors are a group of students selected to serve as official ambassadors for the Greek Community at The University of Alabama. Representing all 4 councils at UA, Greek Ambassadors provide prospective students with insight into the Greek Community.

This unique program enables potential students at the University to meet with a sorority or fraternity member, hear about their experience in the Greek Community, and ask any questions.

Greek Ambassador tours can only be scheduled as part of an official campus visit. If you are interested in meeting with a Greek Ambassador, please notify your Admissions Recruiter. For questions and/or concerns about the Greek Ambassador program, please contact our office at 205-348-2693 or ofsl@ua.edu.
summary: The Greek Ambassadors are the official ambassadors for the Greek Community at The University of Alabama. Representing all 4 councils at UA, Greek Ambassadors provide prospective students with insight into the Greek Community.

name: Crimson Association of Neuroscience
description:
The purpose of the Crimson Association of Neuroscience is to promote and propel the growth of neuroscience at The University of Alabama. This organization aims to connect students with neuroscience-oriented opportunities, including the introduction into faculty research, awareness for neuroscience courses and seminars, and offer access to service and outreach events. By forming a community where students from diverse majors and departments can explore and express interest in neuroscience, the organization will contribute to the overall propagation and recognition of neuroscience as a leading discipline at UA. Here are our objections:

To promote access to neuroscience and behavioral science research opportunities at UA, encouraging student involvement in academic research to contribute to the advancement of the field.
To raise awareness of neuroscience course and seminar offerings, ensuring students are well-informed about new and existing courses that can further their understanding of neurobiology and behavioral science.
To provide service opportunities to neuroscience that allow students to practically apply their knowledge and interest in real-world contexts.
To share and discuss neuroscience research and news, keeping students engaged and information with the latest developments in the field.
To secure support for student attendance at neuroscience conventions, where they can network with expert professionals and learn about cutting-edge research.
summary: UA’s Crimson Association of Neuroscience links students to research, courses, outreach, and conferences, growing campus neuroscience through lab access, event info, service projects, news sharing, and support for convention travel.

name: Crimson Stage
description: Our mission is to produce student led works in a setting that encourages the development of individual artistic pursuits in the theatre and dance students of The University of Alabama. Our vision is to expand the production knowledge of UA Theatre & Dance students.
summary: Crimson Stage is a student production organization that allows UA students to expand their theatrical skillset by producing student works.

name: Chabad Jewish Student Group
description: The primary purpose of Chabad Jewish Student Group is to provide living Judaism to the students at UA within the framework of traditional Judaism. Our objectives include but are not limited to; educational, ritual, and social activities, in addition to counseling and spiritual guidance to individual students. Chabad is a place where students socialize in a comfortable homelike setting with great friends, superb food and stimulating discussions. This organization is non-profit and noncommercial in nature.
summary: Our focus is to provide living Judaism to the students at UA within the framework of traditional Judaism. The group also sponsors social events within the University.

name: Hispanic-Latino Association
description: Main focus is to unite students, graduate students, and faculty/staff of Hispanic/Latino ethnic background and those who have interest in Hispanic/Latino culture through activities that invoke cultural, social, educational, and political awareness, while creating a representative voice for the Hispanic/Latino campus community by identifying the issues of students on campus and addressing these needs to administration, so that our students have an inclusive, positive, and engaging experience
summary: We provide familia-- a home away from home-- for students, faculty, and staff to promote awareness and representation on campus

name: Intervarsity Christian Fellowship
description:
In response to God's love, grace, and truth: 

The Purpose of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship is to establish and advance at The University of Alabama, wittnessing communites of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord; growing in love for God, His Word, His people of every ethnicity and culture, and His purposes in the world. 

We are longing to build a multi-ethnic, multi-denominational Kingdom movement of students and faculty who are being transformed by Jesus, joining Him in His work in renewing the campus and being developed by God to be instruments of change in the world. 

We do our best to create a welcoming environment for people from any background to join us as we look at scripture, allowing Jesus to speak for Himself, asking our deepest questions and giving ourselves to the application of His word. We do this through what we call Missional Small Groups which are designed to reach a specific corner of campus, with the hope of multiplying into every corner of campus.
summary:
Students & Faculty Transformed - The Campus Renewed - World Changers Developed
That's what we long to see as we seek Jesus through student-led, discussion based Bible Studies.
Come as we seek to create a space to ask our questions and find answers!

name: Students United with America's Toothfairy
description: The purpose of Students United with America’s Toothfairy is to educate and introduce the importance of oral health to children in undeserved communities while they are at a young and influential point in their lives. This will be achieved through fun and interactive science presentations performed in hopes that the children may be more conscience of their oral health in the present as well in the future. This organization is also aimed to create community among aspiring dentists so that we may encourage and help each other in our endeavors.
summary: Students United with America's Toothfairy is an organization aimed to educate and introduce the importance of oral health to children in undeserved communities through fun and interactive science presentations.

name: Alberta Baptist Church College Ministry
summary: We exist to help students know God, find community, and live on mission. We are a part of Alberta Baptist Church.

name: The University of Alabama Student Center
description:
The Ferg is the heart of campus. We host different events and programs for students, student organizations, University departments, faculty and staff. We also partner with student groups and University departments to organize events for the student body. In addition, we have spaces available for reservation by student organizations and university departments.

Our Mission

The Ferguson Student Center serves to enhance the student and UA community experience by providing physical space and services that build community, develop leaders, support programs, and promote student learning, development, and inclusion.

Our Vision

  • We will provide a variety of programs and services that create an inclusive and welcoming environment where interaction and understanding among individuals from diverse backgrounds occur.
  • We will uphold the facility for current and future generations of college students by practicing continuous high standards of maintenance, sustainability, refurbishment, and renovation.
  • We will be the center of campus student activities and provide excellent service to the students we serve as well as the members of the university community through programs, facilities, and other amenities.
  • We will provide opportunities for student leadership development through student employment, boards, committees, and volunteerism.
  • We will complement the University mission through intentionally designing, creating and providing environments which promote out-of-class learning experiences.
  • We will recruit, support, develop, and retain excellent staff who are committed to customer service and to the visions and goals of the Ferguson Student Center, the Division of Student Life and The University of Alabama.

Role of the College Union

The college union advances a sense of community, unifying the institution by embracing the diversity of students, faculty, staff, alumni, and guests. We bolster the educational mission of the institution and the development of students as lifelong learners by delivering an array of cultural, educational, social, and recreational programs, services, and facilities.

By any form or name, we serve as the heart of the campus community and create a welcoming environment by:

  • Operating as a student-centered organization that engages in shared decision making and holistic development through employment and involvement.
  • Advocating for inclusivity and equity, fostering respect, and affirming the identities of all individuals.
  • Educating students in leadership and social responsibility and offering firsthand experiences in global citizenship.
  • Providing gathering spaces to encourage formal and informal community interactions that build meaningful relationships.
  • Traditionally considered the living room, the college union enhances the student experience and cultivates an enduring connection to the institution.

Copied from Association of College Unions International (ACUI.org). Adopted by the Association’s general membership in 1996, this statement is based on the Role of the College Union statement, 1956.
summary: The Ferguson Student Center, affectionately known as “the Ferg”, is the heart of campus. From grabbing coffee or a meal to providing space to hang out and relax with friends, you can find a place for most anything at the Ferg.

name: CHAARG
description:
CHAARG [Changing Health, Actions, + Attitudes to Recreate Girls] aims to ignite a passion in college women for health + fitness. Too many college girls rely solely on the elliptical for their exercise. CHAARG liberates girls from the elliptical + shows them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun! Since every girl has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our CHAARG girls to "find their fit" no matter their fitness level! 

 

Once a girl joins CHAARG, she is welcomed into a supportive + empowering community of 10,000+ girls! When you workout, you feel good — but, when you surround yourself with people to support you, you feel even better. CHAARG combines good friends + a good workout to keep every girl feeling her happiest + healthiest self. 

 

GRAB YOUR MEMBERSHIP HERE.
summary: We aim to ignite a passion in college women for health + fitness by partnering with local studios and gyms once a week + hosting social events for our members to build relationships while *finding their fit*!

name: Vote Everywhere
description:
Vote Everywhere is an initiative of The Andrew Goodman Foundation (AGF). The AGF is a national organization that empowers young people to initiate and sustain creative, effective social actions that advance civic engagement and develop cooperative coalitions in local communities.

The AGF is named for Andrew Goodman, and its work honors his legacy. At the height of the Civil Rights Movement, Andrew (Andy) Goodman took one of his summers off from school to join Freedom Summer ’64 and register African-Americans to vote in the South. On Andy’s first day in Mississippi, he and two other young civil rights workers, James Chaney and Michael Schwerner, were murdered by the Ku Klux Klan. He was 20 years old. The story of these three young men struck a public chord that galvanized support for the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Now a historic figure and role model to many, Andy Goodman was first a student and a friend to those seeking equality.

Vote Everywhere continues in the tradition of Andy Goodman by training college students to register, educate, and mobilize voters. Vote Everywhere believes in the power and importance of civic engagement, and it recognizes the important historical role that young people have played in moving our society toward justice at and beyond the ballot box. We operate 40 college campuses across 19 states, and we have registered more than 8,800 new voters in the last semester alone.


For more information on the Andrew Goodman Foundation, you can visit: https://andrewgoodman.org/who-we-are/

For more information about the nature and impact of Vote Everywhere, you can visit: https://andrewgoodman.org/vote-everywhere/
summary: Vote Everywhere is a nonpartisan, experiential program that recruits, trains and supports emerging leaders in America’s universities. Our students strengthen democracy by leading long-term voter engagement, public policy and social justice initiatives.

name: Society of Women In Medicine
description: This organization seeks to encourage, empower, educate, support, and prepare women who wish to enter the medical field, though we welcome any pre-health students to attend. We feel that women have unique challenges and considerations when it comes to entering the medical field, and we wish to provide a space where these can be discussed with role models in the profession. We have meetings twice a month and we try to bring in speakers who can provide a unique perspective. We think that providing mentors who have completed what we wish to is the best way to educate others! We also facilitate the building of a community of women that is supportive and fun.
summary: Seeking to encourage, empower, educate, support and prepare woman who wish to enter the medical field, though any pre-medical or pre-health students are welcome to attend.

name: Nepalese Student Association
description:
NSA is a cultural-based, non-profit organization at the University of Alabama. Our goal is to create a strong sense of community and belongingness to all Nepalese students and their families at UA. NSA strives to host events celebrating Nepalese heritage, culture, and traditions for all students to enjoy and learn from. The mission of the organization is to foster a cultural and educational exchange platform at the University of Alabama.

Nepalese as well as Non-Nepalese students, faculty/staff, and the greater UA community are welcome to attend and benefit from our events.
summary: NSA is a cultural-based, non-profit organization established for the welfare of Nepalese students at the University of Alabama.

name: Vertical Flight Society - UA Chapter
description: VFS aims to foster and advance research, innovation, and professional development in vertical flight technology. This is accomplished by student-led projects and educational initiatives focused on rotorcraft, eVTOL, and related aerodynamic applications.
summary: VFS aims to foster and advance research, innovation, and professional development in vertical flight technology. This is accomplished by student-led projects and educational initiatives focused on rotorcraft, eVTOL, and related aerodynamic applications.

name: Society for Human Resource Management Student Chapter
description:
The purpose of the SHRM Student Chapter is to provide University of Alabama students with the opportunity to gain knowledge and insight into the effective management of human capital in the field of Human Resource Management through affiliation with the Tuscaloosa HR Professionals and the SHRM organization.  In the pursuit of this general purpose, the SHRM Student Chapter will work to:

  1. Promote the exchange of work-related experiences of established business professionals with the theoretical academic perspectives of student and faculty members through open forums and information exchanges;
  2. Encourage the acquisition, growth and continuation of our respective careers in human resource management through expanded knowledge, camaraderie, respect and mutual support among chapter members and through our affiliation with SHRM and our sponsoring chapter;
  3. Provide a pool of human resource management leaders for perpetuation of the Chapter and of SHRM;
  4. Function as an important vehicle for promoting the field of human resource management to students;
  5. Serve as a source of new members for SHRM;
  6. Act as part of the two-way channel of communications between SHRM and the individual members.
summary: We provide students with the opportunity to gain knowledge and insight into the effective management of human capital in the field of Human Resource Management through affiliation with the Tuscaloosa HR Professionals and the SHRM organization.

name: UA Young Americans for Freedom Chapter
