description: The UA Young American’s for Freedom chapter is an organization designed to provide conservative students the opportunity to both network within the conservative movement, as well as to serve as a platform of which to advance conservative principles.
summary: The UA Young American’s for Freedom chapter is an organization designed to provide conservative students the opportunity to both network within the conservative movement, as well as to serve as a platform of which to advance conservative principles.

name: UA Club Swimming
description: Club swimming was created to provide a positive atmosphere for those with a love of swimming to practice and compete together. Our team welcomes swimmers of all backgrounds, and currently boasts athletes who were previously summer swimmers, high school swimmers, year round swimmers, and triathletes. Practices for a variety of skill levels are provided. The team competes regularly in meets around the southeast, as well as Regional and National Championships each year.
summary: Club swimming was created to provide a positive atmosphere for those with a love of swimming to practice and compete together.

name: The Writers Guild
description:
Whether you write fiction, poetry, non-fiction essays, screenplays or anything else or in between, the Writers Guild is the place for you. While housed in the Department of English, students are welcome from all majors with an interest in reading literature and writing, be it poems, short stories, or fantasy and science fiction. The WG sponsors book discussions, supportive critique groups, public readings, film screenings, field trips, and other social and professional development opportunities. 

For those who want more practice and training, courses are available through the creative writing minor (declared in MyBama, start with EN 200). The Creative Writing Program sponsors master classes, book talks, workshops, and social events as well as hosts established and emerging writers through the Bankhead and Coal Royalty Visiting Writers Series.

See the full events calendar here.
summary: The University of Alabama Writers Guild cultivates an undergraduate creative writing community and provides fun and enriching opportunities for members to grow artistically while being part of a sustaining fellowship.

name: The Arnold Air Society
description:
The Arnold Air Society is specifically reserved for those who are enrolled in the Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps.

The following three objectives were adopted by AAS to enhance the Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps (AFROTC) and the United States Air Force Academy (USAFA),and to project the image of the United States Air Force on the university campus and in the surrounding community.

First Objective:

To create a more efficient relationship among Air Force officer candidates, in particular within the Air Force Reserve Officer Training Corps.

Function of the First Objective:

To provide opportunities for a greater interaction among collegiate level Air Force officer candidates and to encourage increased communication with Air Force officers and leaders in national defense, government, and industry.

Second Objective:

To aid in the development of effective Air Force officers.

Function of the Second Objective:

To provide more opportunities for Air Force officer candidates to exercise leadership, management, organizational, and public relations skills.

Third Objective:

To further the purpose, traditions, and concepts of the United States Air Force.

Function of the Third Objective:

To provide opportunities for officer candidates to participate in campus and community service activities, demonstrating the close relationship between civilian and military institutions.
summary: The Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power.  Arnold Air Society contributes to campuses, communities and its own corps in making its primary mission to improving ourselves.

name: Bama Tutors for Service
description: Bama Tutors for Service (BT4S) is a University of Alabama student-run volunteer tutoring organization that provides free tutoring at the Tuscaloosa Public Library to K-12 students. There is no minimum requirement of hours volunteered each semester to be a part of our organization -- we're happy to have you whenever you'd like to volunteer. Additionally, we have a carpool in place for students who do not have their vehicles on campus. Please email us at bamatutorsforservice@gmail.com if you are interested in becoming a tutor or if you have questions about our program!
summary: Bama Tutors for Service is an entirely student-run volunteer organization that provides free tutoring to local students. We tutor multiple times per week at the Tuscaloosa Public Library.

name: Black Faculty and Staff Association Ambassadors
description:
The Black Faculty and Staff Ambassadors serve as a vital link between The Black Faculty & Staff Association and students of color, particularly in the context of the Hallowed Grounds Tour. As part of our mission, BFSA Ambassadors primarily lead the Hallowed Grounds tour, an alternative campus tour designed to shed light onto the lives, experiences, and legacy of the many unsung enslaved men, women, and children who lived, worked, and even died at the University of Alabama. Moreover, we play a supporting role in promoting the success, empowerment, professional growth, and inclusion of students within the campus community.

To achieve these objectives, our organization is dedicated to promoting networking opportunities among individuals and student groups, facilitating dialogue on shared concerns, and advancing educational equity. We fulfill this mission by organizing and implementing a varying array of events and programs aimed at fostering unity and cultivating a culture of advancement and empowerment.
summary: The objective and purpose of Black Faculty and Staff AmbassadorsBFSAA) is primarily to enhance the relationship between the Black Faculty and Staff Association and the African-American student body.

name: Iranian Students Association at UA
description:
The Iranian Students Association at The University of Alabama is a non-religious, non-political student organization whose objectives are:

  • To promote knowledge of Iranian culture among students, faculty, and staff as well as various communities at The University of Alabama in Tuscaloosa and surrounding cities
  • To strengthen the spirit of friendship and cooperation among other cultural groups and student organizations at The University of Alabama
  • To provide support to the Iranian community at The University of Alabama.
summary: ISAUA aims to promote knowledge of Iranian culture among various communities at UA; to strengthen the spirit of friendship and cooperation among other cultural groups and student organizations at UA; and to provide support to the Iranian community at UA.

name: Capstone College of Nursing Research Society
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for undergraduate research, promote collaboration of faculty and students, and provide undergraduate exposure to research opportunities for their upcoming future. The CCN Research Society will serve as an official organization to allow the collaboration of students and faculty to conduct research together within surrounding communities.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for undergraduate research, promote collaboration of faculty and students, and provide undergraduate exposure to research opportunities for their upcoming future.

name: Intramural Officials Association
description: The purpose of this association is to provide intramural sport officials at the University of Alabama the opportunity to review sport rules, discuss proper mechanics, and create a community of officials.
summary: The purpose of this association is to provide intramural sport officials at the University of Alabama the opportunity to review sport rules, discuss proper mechanics, and create a community of officials.

name: The B+ Foundation at Alabama
description:
As part of The Andrew McDonough B+ Foundation, UA's B+ Foundation is dedicated to raising funds to provide financial assistance (via The B+ Family Assistance Program) and emotional support to families of children battling cancer nationwide. This mission is primarily accomplished through fundraising initiatives and The B+ Heroes Program.

The B+ Heroes Program pairs various campus organizations with local Tuscaloosa children battling cancer. The core aim of this program is to spend quality time with the kids, elevate their spirits by treating them with the admiration they deserve, and provide a brief respite from the challenges they face.

The Andrew McDonough B+ Foundation honors the life of Andrew McDonough. Andrew battled leukemia, septic shock, and complications of childhood cancer for 167 days before passing away on July 14, 2007, at the age of 14. Andrew’s B+ blood type became his family’s and friends’ motto throughout his fight against childhood cancer — to “Be Positive.”

The B+ Foundation is about “Kids Helping Kids Fight Cancer” and is the largest provider of financial assistance to families of kids with cancer nationwide. Additionally, The B+ Foundation funds cutting-edge childhood cancer research and advocates on behalf of kids with cancer.
summary: The Andrew McDonough B+ Foundation at The University of Alabama is dedicated to “Kids Helping Kids Fight Cancer." Our primary objective is to raise funds to provide both financial & emotional support to families with children fighting cancer

name: Kesem
description: Kesem is an organization that raises money to support children and their families as they battle a parent's cancer diagnosis. Kesem at the University of Alabama will also focus on building a large campus presence through leadership. We offer many leadership positions and provide support and tools for college students to develop their leadership abilities. Selection and affiliation with the Kesem summer camp is not a part of our on campus mission.
summary: Kesem is an organization that fundraises money to support children through and beyond their parent's cancer.

name: General Art Club
description:
The General Art Club is a student-run arts club at the University of Alabama. Expressing ourselves through art, both professionally and for fun, is the basis of our organization. We believe that everyone should have access to artistic communities and experiences, regardless of skill-level or college major. Our organization is home to aspiring professional art majors, engineering majors with artistic hobbies, and those simply wanting to learn the basics and have fun. We aim for at least one large project each semester, which may include public events, seasonal festivals, artist workshops and visitors, and exhibitions. Additionally, we have small events and activities to practice new techniques. Our main priority is to connect students to each other and the community through art. 

Our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/88940470/dEdFX2Og
summary: You've reached the official General Art Club at the University of Alabama. All majors and artistic skill-levels are welcome, and we'd love to have you!

name: Communication Studies Club
description:
The purpose of The Communication Studies Club is to promote interest in the communication studies discipline. In the pursuit of this general purpose, The Communication Studies Club will work to:

A. Provide resources and advice for members interested and/or already pursuing a communication studies degree;

B. Encouraging the Academic study of human communication (rhetorical, interpersonal, and organizational);

C. Host social events that provide opportunities for members to network with each other and professionals;

D. Help members gain experience through partnerships with other student organizations.
summary: An org. dedicated to promoting interest in communication studies. Doing so by providing resources to members, encouraging the study of communication, hosting social events, and helping members gain experience through partnership with other student orgs.

name: Asian American Student Association
description:
Mission: Our mission to be able to provide a social community and an environment for people of all backgrounds to celebrate Asian American culture at the University of Alabama. We aim to achieve this by having weekly coffee hours, monthly meetings, as well as hosting a bigger monthly event to honor Asian celebrations ranging from Lunar New Year to Diwali. 

Vision: Eventually as the program begins to expand, Asian American Student Association will become big enough to promote other multi-cultural organizations on campus. Through this continued vision, other multi-cultural organizations including AASA can work together to create a wonderful multi-cultural environment hosting events, cultural discussions and panels. This will be achieved by growing the AASA community and partnering with other multi-cultural organizations to host major events.

Major Programs/Initiatives: monthly meeting, monthly celebratory event, and possible volunteer options relating to the Asian American community
summary: A social organization where students can come together and have an open dialogue about Asian American cultures and experiences.

name: Alabama Gymnastics Club
description: Welcome to Alabama Gymnastics Club! We are a registered NAIGC club, and welcome any men or women who are looking to get back into the sport they love or try something new. We compete in NAIGC sanctioned meets around the country. We do not have a skill requirement, and we are open to accepting anyone who is interested in gymnastics! Please reach out to us through email for further inquiries and interest.
summary: Welcome to Alabama Gymnastics Club! We are a registered NAIGC club, and welcome anyone with gymnastics, cheer, or dance experience as well as anyone looking to try something new!

name: Beat Auburn Beat Hunger
description: It is the mission of Beat Auburn Beat Hunger to host an annual event during the seven weeks leading up to the Iron Bowl that provides both food and monetary donations to the West Alabama Food Bank.  The BABH food drive unites University of Alabama students, faculty, staff, alumni, fans,  and Tuscaloosa and Northport community members to help “fight” hunger and poverty in West Alabama. In competition with Auburn University and the Food Bank of East Alabama’s “Beat Bama Food Drive”, BABH aims to collect the most non-perishable food to help those who suffer from food insecurity.  This good-natured competition has made an impact that reaches far beyond the campuses where it began. Since its inception, Alabama and Auburn have combined to raise over 8 million pounds of food for Alabamians. All of the food collected during the food drive competition at each school will benefit their local food bank, either the West Alabama Food Bank or the Food Bank of East Alabama.
summary: Beat Auburn Beat Hunger is a food and monetary drive competition against Auburn University to see which school can collect the most pounds of food for their local food bank during the 7 weeks leading up to the Iron Bowl.

name: Management Consulting Academy
description:
​The UA Management Consulting Academy, founded in 2015 by University of Alabama students, was created for students interested in the management consulting field. The Academy’s purpose is to market and establish the Capstone as a premiere recruiting destination for top management consulting firms across the nation. Its intent is to adequately prepare top students for the unique, challenging interview process associated with the industry.

The UA Management Consulting Academy will assist students with the following:

  • Learning about the recruiting process and best practices of successful applicants
  • Exposing members to a comprehensive interview preparation curriculum including a focus on the case interview problem solving methods
  • Establishing relationships with industry professionals
  • Crafting effective resumes and cover letters tailored for the consulting industry
summary: MCA exists to help prepare students for applying to and succeeding in careers in the field of management consulting, through skill development, network-building, and other activities critical to the process.

name: Forest Stewards Guild: Student Chapter
description:
We carry out the Forest Stewards Guild unique mission of "putting the forest first." The Guild discusses ecological forestry and maintains the integrity of the forest and human communities that depend on it. This student chapter is an active club that goes hiking, camping, participates in public land clean up days, and provides opportunities for professional development in forest science careers.

The view of ecological forestry is managing forests for multiple uses, rather than exploiting land for wood products, wild game, etc. Our nations forests are not static, they are ever-changing, and we wish to promote healthy forests in the future by managing for resiliency to perturbations. In addition to advocating for management entries by mimicking natural disturbances, we wish to maintain the aesthetic values of our lands by participated in public land clean up days, organizing camping excursions, and discussing daily impacts on our forest treasures. We have contacts of employees from Talledega NF, Bankhead NF, USFS southern research station, Westervelt, and others. We will reach out to these connections to discuss professional development such as what jobs are out there, how to apply and how to be the best qualified applicant.
summary: The purpose of this guild is to promote healthy forests. We discuss ecological forest practices and exhibit exceptional forest stewardship by offering opportunities to work with state and federal forest agencies.

name: Alabama Undergraduate Research Association
description: The purpose of this association is to promote a community for students who are interested in, have conducted, or are currently involved in undergraduate research at the University of Alabama. Members will attend meetings and social events, and support the network of undergraduate researchers on campus. AURA is a balance of academics and fun, where we will come together to discuss research experience, interdisciplinary views, and future opportunities.
summary: The purpose of this association is to promote a community for students who are interested in, have conducted, or are currently involved in undergraduate research at the University of Alabama.

name: Association for Women in Sports Media
description: The Association for Women in Sports Media (AWSM) has the following objectives: to serve as a positive advocate for women in sports media through support services, networking, and national visibility; to mentor and assist young women entering sports media through scholarships, internships, and contact with experienced members; and to serve as a watchdog by promoting fair portrayal of female professionals in sports media, encouraging positive workplace environments, and supporting equal access to opportunities for all. (Adopted February 2005)
summary: Membership in registered student organizations is open to all University of Alabama students, regardless of federally protected class, except designated fraternal organizations exempt under Title IX.

name: Artificial Intelligence Club
description: Join us to explore the future of Artificial Intelligence! Our club is dedicated to fostering a community of AI enthusiasts, providing opportunities for learning, collaboration, and innovation. Whether you are a beginner or an expert, we have something for everyone. Participate in workshops, hackathons, and guest lectures to enhance your skills and network with like-minded individuals. Let's shape the future of AI together!
summary: Bringing contributions from all backgrounds to explore AI and inspire a community of understanding

name: Yonder Contemporary Dance Company
description:
Yonder Contemporary Dance Company is an internationally focused performing arts student organization. We perform a run of evening length works for an international audience on a biennial schedule. Planning, choreographing, rehearsing and promoting of this event is a year long process and requires collaboration between students from multiple colleges and schools within the University, such as Dance, Advertising and Public Relations, and Music. By doing so, we develop a network of creative collaborators.

As a means of preparing for the international performances, we perform our show locally as well as regionally. This is one of the ways that we build awareness of—and appreciation for—contemporary dance being choreographed and performed at The University of Alabama. After these preparatory showings we gather feedback from audience members as well.

Active membership requires the students to be enrolled at The University of Alabama during the Fall and Spring semesters prior to the Summer international performance. As members graduate, we will continually develop an alumni base that grows our network of professional connections.
summary: YCDC encourages and seeks out collaboration with artists, composers, creative thinkers and makers. Local and regional tours are performed in advance of, and in preparation for, a biennial creative research and international performance event.

name: Legal Research Club
description: The Legal Research Club is a group led by Alabama Pre-Law undergraduates from a variety of majors who desire to make resources regarding legal research and writing more accessible to Alabama undergraduates. Housed within our organization is the Capstone Journal of Law and Public Policy, supplemented by workshops, collaboration, and events. We invite any Pre-Law undergraduate (or anyone interested in legal research) from any major to be a part of LRC!
summary: The Legal Research Club is dedicated to helping Pre-Law students by connecting them with the resources they need to be successful in the areas of legal research and writing, especially through the publication of an Undergraduate Law Review.

name: Caribbean Students' Association
description: The mission of the Caribbean Students' Association is to create a family of individuals who share Caribbean heritage or simply a love for the Caribbean and its history and culture. Within this family, we seek to provide support to one another, create avenues for learning and academic success, and foster a welcoming atmosphere for all to socialize. Our major goal is to bring awareness of the Caribbean culture to the University of Alabama and to our community through hosting festivities on campus and dedicating service.
summary: The Caribbean Students' Association is dedicated to bringing awareness and appreciation of the Caribbean culture to the University of Alabama while assisting its members in achieving academic and social success.

name: Student-Athlete Advisory Committee
description: The Student-Athlete Advisory Committee (SAAC) is an organization that represents student-athletes on the campus of The University of Alabama.  Student-athletes have a voice in the NCAA through advisory committees at the campus and conference levels to support SAAC at the national level.  Each committee is made up of student-athletes assembled from all contributing sports to provide insight on the student-athlete experience and offer input on the rules, regulations, and policies that affect student-athletes' lives on campus.
summary: The Student-Athlete Advisory Committee (SAAC) serves as the voice of all student-athletes at The University of Alabama related to institutional, conference and national issues that impact the intercollegiate athletics experience.

name: Leftist Collective
description: Our mission is to build power for the working class and oppressed people in our community through political education and direct action. We are a space for socialists, communists, anarchists, and other anti-capitalists to learn, organize, and take action. We oppose capitalism, imperialism, racism, patriarchy, queerphobia, ableism and other forms of oppression. We also welcome those who are developing their political understanding and want to learn about leftist ideas.
summary: Leftist Collective is a broad spectrum leftist org advocating for anti-capitalism and material improvement in the lives of all working people. Our mission is education, community service, and political action.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Crimson Defense - Cyber Security Club
description:
Visit our website here: uacrimsondefense.github.io!

Crimson Defense Cyber Security Club is the premier cyber security student organization affiliated with the University of Alabama Department of Computer Science. We strive to create a community on campus for those who are interested in all things cyber security: from cyber crime to digital forensics to malware analysis and everything in between! We host General Interest meetings on Wednesday evenings, with Competition Team and Project Crimson meetings. Be sure to join our Discord for the latest meeting information.

 

Crimson Defense offers three different experiences to cater to club members of all ability/knowledge levels:

- General Meetings: Lectures, demos, and discussions on the latest cyber security topics! Topics range from introductions to malware analysis, cyber crime overviews, to talks from industry representatives. No technical experience required!

- Project Crimson: Our peer 1-on-1 cyber security skills mentoring sessions, perfect for beginners and advanced students alike!

- Competition Team: Join our competition team to compete in national cyber security competitions and put your skills to the test! Perfect for intermediate to advanced members!

 

DISCLAIMER: Using security techniques with malicious intent will result in your removal from Crimson Defense. If applicable, you will be reported to the appropriate authorities. Do not abuse your power, WE ARE NOT A HACKTIVIST GROUP.
summary: At Crimson Defense Cyber Security Club we strive to create a community on campus for those who are interested in all things cyber security: from cyber crime to digital forensics to malware analysis and everything in between!

name: Investment Banking Academy
description: The Investment Banking Academy, founded by two Alabama alumni working on Wall Street, was created for The University of Alabama students interested in pursuing careers in financial services. The Academy's purpose is to assist and prepare students for the industry and establish the Capstone as a premier recruiting destination for firms nationwide. Since inception, members have secured internships at leading investment banks, including Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, J.P. Morgan, Moelis, Lazard, Barclays, Bank of America, Citi, and Jefferies, as well as at prominent hedge funds and private equity firms.
summary: The Investment Banking Academy seeks to prepare elite students for internships in the financial services industry and to establish The University of Alabama as a premier recruiting destination for banks across the country.

name: Campus Outreach
description: Campus Outreach is an interdenominational Christian organization. We are committed to helping students know Christ and become  "Multiplying Christ-Like Leadesr" through life on life discipleship.   If you are unsure about Jesus, we want to be a place that you can investigate what you think about him.  If you are a Christian, we want to give you opportunities to grow deeper in your relationship with God.   We will have, Bible studies, socials, retreats, conferences and summer trips that you can be a part of so that you connect with people and think about your relationship with God.
summary: Campus Outreach is an interdenominational Christian organization.  We are committed to helping students know the person of Jesus christ and to become a "Multiplying Christ-like Leader."

name: Friends of MSF
description: Our organization will allow people to learn about, support, and advocate for the work of MSF-USA. One of our main goals is to raise campus awareness of global health issues. This will be done through a variety of events including speakers that will be provided by MSF national as well as local sources. These speakers will also help explain what they do within MSF and network with the members to show them what a career in global medicine looks like. The second goal of our organization is to raise funds for MSF-USA. This will be achieved through a variety of events. This will include everything from simple fundraiser nights on The Strip, to more complex events such as an annual 5k and community banquet events.
summary: Our organization will help create spaces in which people can learn about, support, and advocate for the work of MSF-USA, through a variety of events and actions including, without limitation, meetings, film-screenings, fundraising efforts, etc.

name: UREC Sport Club Officers
description: The mission of this group is to build a community within the Sport Club Officers at UA. This group page will be the main page for communications between club officers and the administration.
summary: The mission of this group is to build a community within the Sport Club Officers at UA. This group page will be the main page for communications between club officers and the administration.

name: Collegiate Entrepreneurs' Organization
description: The purpose of the Collegiate Entrepreneur's Organization is to organize students at UA to practice and promote both entrepreneurship and innovation through social and educational experiences. Our goal as an organization is to bring entrepreneurial minded students together and provide them with networking and learning opportunities, as well as provide a place for said students to hone their skills as an entrepreneur.
summary: The purpose of CEO is to organize students at UA to practice and promote both entrepreneurship and innovation through social and educational experiences.

name: Alabama Student Genetics Association
description:
ASGA hosts meetings to involve members in the discussion and education of genetics-related topics for the duration of an hour. Each meeting will provide opportunities for members to discuss their opinions and learn from others about ethical controversy, possible career paths, and technological advancements that are directly impacting the field of genetics.

Please join our group me if you are interested in becoming a member!!
summary: Our goal is to educate members and the campus community about genetics. We host informal events, foster a welcoming environment to encourage further learning, and strive to understand the world of genetics better.

name: The Vietnamese Student and Scholar Association
description: The Vietnamese Student and Scholar Association (VSSA) is open to every student at UA who is interested in learning more about Vietnam. We aim to create not only a supportive environment for UA students from Vietnam but also a welcoming community for everyone to get in touch with Vietnamese culture. VSSA will host several events throughout the school year for members to hang out, build friendships, and promote an intercultural experience.
summary: The Vietnamese Student and Scholar Association is a place for all student and staff of the University Alabama to come together and emerge in the Vietnamese culture, learn new thing and meet new people around the campus.

name: Anchor College Ministry
description: We are a group of people that want to make a difference in the lives of others. We want to provide an atmosphere that gives students a place to belong, a hope to believe in, and opportunities to contribute to the overall well-being of The University of Alabama campus by knowing the truth of Jesus and living out that truth daily. We believe this happens best through building relationships and fostering community. Walking through the journey of life together is what we believe we have been tasked to do by God through Jesus.
summary: We are a group of people that want to make a difference in the lives of others. We want to provide an atmosphere that gives students a place to belong and opportunities to contribute to the overall well-being of The University of Alabama.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Student Chapter
description:
1. The Chapter is organized and will be operated for educational and scientific purposes to promote the following:
    1. an increased knowledge of and greater interest in the mathematical sciences, including pure and applied mathematics, statistics, and their applications;
    2. a greater understanding of the contributions of women in the mathematical sciences; and
    3. mentoring and encouraging interested students as they prepare for careers in the mathematical sciences.
summary: The Chapter promotes an increased knowledge of and greater interest in the mathematical sciences by mentoring and encouraging women and girls as they prepare for careers in the field.

name: Data Analytics Club
summary: The Data Analytics Club intends to bring together students who aspire to become a data analysts or are pursuing a career related to analytics.

name: Sigma Kappa
description: Sigma Kappa's values are personal growth, friendship, service, and loyalty. These values are carried out on a chapter level through programming and chapter functions, and on an individual level by each member of the organization in their daily life. Sigma Kappa's philanthropic efforts support gerentology and Alzheimer's disease research through a national partnership with the Alzheimer's Association. The Sigma Kappa chapter at Alabama has found many ways to connect with the city of Tuscaloosa in this service project.
summary: The purpose of Sigma Kappa is to provide women lifelong opportunities and support for social, intellectual and spiritual development by bringing women together to positively impact our communities.

name: Saint Thomas More Society
description: The Society's endeavor is to facilitate discussions about topics concerning Christianity, especially the Catholic Church, and their convergence to the study of law. The purpose of this organization is to provide a structure within which Catholic law students and others in the Law School having an interest in the Catholic Church, its policies, or positions on various issues, can develop among themselves and the general UA community an awareness and appreciation of Catholic legal, social, political, historical and religious tradition.
summary: The Saint Thomas More Society is the Catholic student organization at The University of Alabama School of Law.

name: Tune In A Cappella
description: Tune In is co-ed A Cappella group made up of fun and talented singers here on campus. Tune In is student led and open to students of all grade levels and majors. We perform advanced arrangements from a variety of styles and genres, both old and new. These include pop, Disney ballads, old classics, Christmas carols, hymns, Hip-Hop, R&B, Gospel and Jazz. We have a variety of performance opportunities including concerts, on and off campus events, assisted living facilities, and more. Tune In is an auditioned group that rehearses 2 nights per week. We work hard, and we have fun. Stay tuned in!
summary: Tune In is a co-ed A Cappella group, comprised of talented singers. We strive to spread the joy of music through A Cappella, at an advanced level, throughout our campus and community. Auditions are conditionally held in the fall and spring of each year.

name: Solid Rock Student Ministry
summary: SRSM's aim is to use bible studies, prayer and Christian fellowship to promote and encourage spiritual growth in the lives of its student members.

name: Alabama Print & Book Group
description: We provide learning opportunities and social activities for people interested in printmaking, paper-making, and the book arts. Our group fosters appreciation for the arts through activities like workshops, guest lecturers, exhibitions, sales of work, and educational travel.
summary: We provide learning opportunities and social activities for people interested in printmaking, paper-making, and the book arts.

name: Manderson Specialized Masters Association
description: MSMA is a professional development and collaborative network for students enrolled in specialized masters programs in the Manderson Graduate School of Business. MSMA hosts fun social and professional events in the hopes of helping grad students connect with each other/professionals and continually expand their network!
summary: An organization dedicated to the empowerment of specialized masters students enrolled in the Manderson Graduate School of Business through forward-looking development and personal growth.

name: College of Education Graduate Student Organization
description:
The purpose of College of Education Graduate Student Organization (COE-GSO) is to represent the broad interests of graduate students in the College of Education at the University of Alabama. 

- In the pursuit of this general purpose, College of Education Graduate Student Organization will:

- Represent graduate students in the College of Education to faculty, staff, and administrators   

- Support and engage graduate students as they pursue coursework, research, and scholarly work 

- Host social events to foster collaboration and a sense of community among graduate students 

- Conduct professional development programs to help graduate students grow professionally and to prepare students for future careers 

- Foster professionalism and provide an opportunity for graduate students to network with faculty and staff in the College of Education and the University of Alabama as a whole 

- Serve the University of Alabama community by means of service and professional activities.
summary: The College of Education Graduate Student Organization (COE-GSO) is a student-run organization aimed at representing the broad interests of graduate students in the College of Education at the University of Alabama.

name: Crimson Historical Review
summary: The Crimson Historical Review is the University of Alabama’s first undergraduate history journal, and one of the few of its type in the country. The Editorial Board, Design Team, and Review Board are all comprised of UA undergraduate students.

name: Student Organ Donation Advocates
description: The UA chapter of SODA is an organization for students who want to make a difference by hosting events and leading organ donation campaigns. The efforts of these students save lives, build leadership skills, and create positive ripple effects throughout their communities.
summary: SODA is a group of students who want to make a difference by hosting events and leading organ donation chapters on their high school and college campuses. These students' efforts save lives and create positive ripple effects throughout their communities.

name: Nano Science and Engineering Association
description: This organization promotes nanoscience at UA by hosting inter-departmental discussions to promote University of Alabama collaboration and publicly promoting scientific activities to show the connection between science and life.
summary: This organization promotes nanoscience at UA by hosting inter-departmental discussions to promote University of Alabama collaboration and publicly promoting scientific activities to show the connection between science and life.

name: UASPACE
description:
CubeSats are small satellites that range in mass from less than a kilogram to approximately 40 kilograms. Historically launched as secondary payloads, the highly standardized structure and reliance on commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) components makes CubeSats cheaper to produce and launch compared to larger satellites. The low cost of development also makes placing satellites in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) more accessible to academic institutions. To successfully place a satellite in LEO will require a multi-disciplinary team that can address both the technical and financial aspects of satellite development, as well as communication, marketing, and public relations. As such, UASPACE will provide opportunities to a wide range of students from across the University of Alabama campus.

 

The primary goal of UASPACE is to launch the first University of Alabama satellite into space. The CubeSats for UASPACE will be launched through NASA’s ElaNa project. As part of this effort, UASPACE will be laying the foundation for future University of Alabama CubeSat launches performing various missions beneficial to the university. In addition to the construction and launching of CubeSats, UASPACE will also serve as a space advocacy group to the public, with a special emphasis on outreach to K-12 students in underperforming schools.
summary: The primary goal of UASPACE is the design, fabrication, testing, launch, orbital insertion, tracking, and successful operation of CubeSats. UASPACE also aims to work as a space advocacy group to the public, especially to K-12 students.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
summary: TKE’s mission is to aid men in their mental, moral and social development for life. Tau Kappa Epsilon contributes to the advancement of society through the personal growth of our members, and service to others. TKE builds Better Men for a Better World.

name: College of Human Environmental Science's Council for Engagement and Outreach
description: The purpose of this club is dedicated to ensuring all students in the College of Human Environmental Sciences (CHES) have access to information regarding opportunities provided by the College and by UA. As a group of student leaders, they work together to provide support and guidance for current students. The Council for Engagement and Outreach (CEO) is made up of various CHES students and exists to promote collaboration across CHES student organizations, current students, faculty, staff, and administration. This will be accomplished by developing and maintaining strong relationships with the CHES departments to identify and implement outreach opportunities and networking within the college. Furthermore, the organization will build strong relationships with faculty and staff and ensure that students can connect with their instructors outside of a classroom setting.
summary: The CHES Council for Engagement and Outreach is dedicated to promoting a relationship between students, faculty, staff, and administration within the College by providing opportunities for engagement and outreach.

name: MIS Ambassadors
summary: Group of students that serve as the official representatives for the UA MIS program. Ambassadors give tours to prospective MIS students and lead initiatives that promote student success and confidence within the program.

name: First Generation Law Students Association
description: FGLSA seeks to support University of Alabama Law School students who are the first in their families to pursue a law degree. Our goal is to assist members as they navigate the law school environment. Additionally, FGLSA advocates for engaging in activities that support law students through relevant professional, social, and academic opportunities that suit the needs of its members in the law school community.
summary: FGLSA supports first generation law students. Our goal is to assist members in navigating law school by advocating for support through relevant professional, social, and academic opportunities within and outside the law school community.

name: Paws of UA
summary: Paws of UA is an organization that volunteers with local animal shelters. We will help with any of the shelter's needs and have days out with the animals as well. Our main goal is to raise enough awareness so that every shelter animal is adopted.

name: Graduate Nutrition Organization
description: The Graduate Nutrition Organization (GNO) aims to facilitate a professional, collaborative network for graduate nutrition students at The University of Alabama. By hosting meetings and events, members will engage with each other through insightful discussion regarding modern issues in nutrition, whether in practice, research, or also in preparation for future endeavors. GNO strives to provide a variety of useful information while building a strong network among members.
summary: GNO aims to facilitate a professional, collaborative network for graduate nutrition students at The University of Alabama.

name: Education Abroad Alumni Association
description:
The Education Abroad Alumni Association provides opportunities for Education Abroad Alumni to expand on their abroad experience and promote an interest in education abroad on campus. Students can go abroad to study, do research, to intern, or volunteer. Once they come back from their time abroad EAAA is there to enhance their abroad experience. EAAA creates opportunities for students to socialize with other students through bi-monthly meetings, mixers, and other special events. EAAA also hosts workshops to work on resume building as well as verbalizing student’s experiences for job interviews.

EAAA participates in campus events such as the Study Abroad Fair, International Education Week, International Coffee Hour, Around the World at UA, as well as other events hosted by the Capstone International Center. Each semester students within EAAA are tasked with creating new and innovative events to promote education abroad at the University of Alabama. These events can range from information sessions, tabling, classroom visits, Show N’ Tells, Ask an Alumni events, and international food tastings. Students who participate in EAAA will gain a greater understanding of intercultural communication, diplomacy, cultural differences, globalization, cross-cultural awareness, new perspectives, intersecting identities, and global citizenship.
summary: The EAAA provides opportunities for members to continue to expand on their abroad experiences and promote an interest in education abroad on campus. This includes study abroad, intern abroad, research abroad, or volunteer abroad students.

name: IGNITE National - UA
description: IGNITE is a nonpartisan organization dedicated to empowering young women to become the next generation of political leaders. IGNITE is a movement of young women who are ready and eager to become the next generation of political leaders. IGNITE teaches young women how government works, why it matters, and how to become politically engaged.
summary: IGNITE is a nonpartisan organization dedicated to empowering young women to become the next generation of political leaders. IGNITE is a movement of young women who are ready and eager to become the next generation of political leaders.

name: Crimson Car Club
description:
Crimson Car Club was founded to unite car enthusiasts across the UA campus. We foster a vibrant community by organizing local meets, regional car gatherings, exhilarating drives, autocross events, visits to racetracks, and more – all at no cost to join.

But we're more than just a car club; we're a family. Beyond our shared passion for cars, Crimson Car Club provides a safe and welcoming space to create lasting memories and forge meaningful bonds. Starting college can be overwhelming, and we understand the importance of finding your place. Whether you're a seasoned driver or simply curious about cars, our club offers an inclusive environment to connect with others and unwind from the stresses of college life.

Driving is more than transportation; it's a reflection of personality and a means of escaping stress. If cars pique your interest or you're looking to make new friends, join us and discover the Crimson Car Club community.

Click this link for more information: https://linktr.ee/crimsoncarclub
summary: Uniting car enthusiasts across The University of Alabama since 2019.

name: Sigma Alpha Iota
description: Sigma Alpha Iota is an International Music Fraternity that includes composers, teachers, and performers of high merit. It's purpose is to form chapters of women college students and alumnae who have a sincere interest in music, to uphold the highest standards of music, to further the development of music in America and throughout the world, to give inspiration and encouragement to members, to organize the cultural life of Sigma Alpha Iota members as a contributing factor to their educational growth, to support the ideals and goals of the member's Alma Mater, and to adhere to the highest standards of citizenship in school, community, and fraternity life. Some of the national objectives include nurturing sisterhood, supporting women in music, and to increase knowledge and support of SAI Philanthropies, Inc.
summary: Sigma Alpha Iota is an International Music Fraternity that aims to encourage, nurture, and support women in the art of music.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Alabama Archery Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to instruct and practice archery, while also bringing together people interested in archery.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to instruct and practice archery, while also bringing together people interested in archery.

name: Black Business Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Black Business Student Association is to foster a sense of community for Black-identifying students in the Culverhouse College of Business at the University of Alabama.

name: Psychology Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the Psychology Graduate Student Association at The University of Alabama is to provide a strong voice for all psychology graduate students. With this voice, the PGSA aims to facilitate communication amongst graduate students, faculty, and staff. The PGSA also aspires to encourage inter student coordination and collaboration with the purpose of enhancing student life and promoting research, teaching, and service as students embark on their primary goal of successfully completing and graduating from the program.
summary: The Psychology Graduate Student Association is a group of elected doctoral students in the psychology department who work to promote graduate student interests and provide opportunities for socializing and professional development for graduate students.

name: Conservation Biology Society
description: The Conservation Biology Society is dedicated to discussing historic and current developments in conservation, biodiversity and policy.  Our organization's main focus is to spread awareness of conservation throughout all levels of the community. We work to disseminate information on conservation to the public and engage in outreach and educational events. We also have engage in letter writing campaigns to local government officials in hopes to make conservation a priority in Tuscaloosa's decision making. Overall, this is an organization dedicated to discussing and promoting biological conservation, making changes in our daily lives to benefit the environment and enaging our community in conservation by hosting and participating in litter clean-ups, invasive species, letter-writing, outreach events, biolblitzes, and other related activities.
summary: The CBS is dedicated to learning historic and current developments in conservation, biodiversity and policy to then disseminate this information to the public through outreach and educational events.

name: Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Association
description:
PAGSA an organization for physics and astronomy students at the University of Alabama. The benefits of PAGSA public events are intended for the general public, including the University of Alabama students. 

PAGSA will work to:

  • provide mentorship to first year graduate students and to provide outreach to prospective graduate students;
  • promote interdisciplinary talks and collaboration;
  • provide opportunities to graduate students to share research;
  • promote physics & astronomy amongst the general public;
  • promote communication between graduate students and faculty.
  • Organize outreach and extra-curricular activities/events for students and faculty.
summary: PAGSA is an organization for physics students at The University of Alabama. The benefits of PAGSA public events are intended for the general public, including The University of Alabama students.

name: Management Doctoral Student Organization
description: The Management Doctoral Students Organization (MDSO) is an initiative led by students within this program. Its primary mission is to serve as a central hub for doctoral students in the Management Department, facilitating the exchange of resources, fostering professional development, and enhancing engagement with the broader university community. The organization is dedicated to improving the accessibility of professional networks and resources, thereby enriching our members' academic and career trajectories. Through MDSO, doctoral students are offered a structured platform to collaborate, share academic insights, and leverage collective expertise to advance their scholarly and professional goals.
summary: The MDSO is a student-run organization for students in the PhD program in the Management Department at the University of Alabama.

name: League of Women Voters UA
description: The League of Women Voters is a non-partisan organization founded over 100 years ago to secure the right to vote for women. Today, our purpose is to ensure everyone has access to the vote. We welcome members of all races, genders, sexual orientations, and religions. We are actively committed to fostering diverse discourse and civic engagement. Our chapter will strive to empower women and defend democracy. Join us today. Some campaigns we will pursue include voter registration and rights restoration drives and education on issues such as election security, gerrymandering, and climate change.
summary: The League of Women Voters is a non-partisan organization founded over 100 years ago to secure the right to vote for women. Today, our purpose is to ensure everyone has access to the vote.

name: UA Chess Club
description:
We hold informal weekly meetings where people come to play casual games. You are welcome to bring your own board, but it is not needed. We also occasionally host tournaments, both casual and rated. 

https://web.groupme.com/join_group/61962616/uyZhYMH4
summary:
A club for anyone interested in playing or learning the game of chess.

Open to any skill level.

No previous experience/knowledge required.

GroupMe: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/61962616/uyZhYMH4

name: The University of Alabama Hunt Seat Equestrian Club
description: The purpose of this Sport Club is to provide fellowship, experience in an equine environment both for noncompetitive and competitive purposes, and a worthwhile contribution to campus life for all its members. We aim to foster competitive interaction between the University of Alabama (UA) and other universities in the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (IHSA) and bring service to our community and club.
summary: The Hunt Seat Equestrian club provides experience in an equine environment for both noncompetitive and competitive purposes. We aim to foster competitive interaction between UA and other universities in the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association (IHSA).

name: Mentor Undergraduate Peer Partnering
description:
Mentor UPP is a mentoring and networking program designed to cultivate relationships
between upperclassmen and underclassmen to increase involvement, retention, and
confidence in the newest members of the College of Engineering. Freshmen,
sophomores, and transfer students are matched in groups with one to two junior or
senior mentors who provide a support system along with offering assistance and
information to freshmen, sophomores, and transfer students as they adapt to campus
and college life at The University of Alabama. Mentees are encouraged to complete their
freshman and sophomore years in Mentor UPP, and then give back to future students as
peer mentors. There is also an option to have a professional mentor who is a UA COE alum.
summary:
Mentor UPP is a mentoring and networking program designed to cultivate relationships
between upperclassmen and underclassmen to increase involvement, retention, and confidence in the newest members of the College of Engineering.

name: Dare to Diagnose
description: We are an organization for pre-health students at UA centered around patient simulations. Our biggest event each year is a competition featuring two rounds, an online test intended to assess students' critical thinking and diagnostic skills and a simulated patient interaction round intended to help pre-health students practice bedside manner. Our mission is to improve undergraduates' understanding of the medical profession by challenging them to interweave the science of medicine with the art of empathy, something that is not normally emphasized in the traditional pre-health academic curriculum.
summary: We are an organization for pre-health students at UA whose goal is to challenge students to interweave the science of medicine with the art of empathy. Our biggest event each year is a competition focused around patient simulations.

name: Dance Alabama Film Festival
description: Dance Alabama Film Festival is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting collaboration in dance, music composition, and cinematography through the creation and exhibition of original screen dances. Working with the Faculty Advisor, the organization includes and serves graduate and undergraduate students. Dance Alabama Film Festival oversees the creation and presentation of films at an annual public screening, as well as fundraising, community outreach, and events to facilitate collaboration.
summary: Dance Alabama Film Festival is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting collaboration in dance, music composition, and cinematography through the creation and exhibition of original screendances.

name: Students Supporting Healthcare Workers
description: SSHW is a service-based organization for students interested in volunteering in the medical field and gaining hands-on experience. Our members support healthcare professionals and the community through meaningful service, skill-building opportunities, and outreach initiatives. Past projects have included volunteering in hospitals, clinics, and hospice care, as well as CPR and First Aid training for members. SSHW provides a space for students to develop practical healthcare skills, connect with like-minded peers, and make a tangible impact on the health and well-being of our community.
summary: SSHW is a service-focused organization that supports healthcare professionals through volunteering, skill-building, and community outreach. Members gain hands-on experience, professional growth, and meaningful ways to give back to the medical community.

name: The Physical Education Club
summary: The purpose of this organization is to help promote professional and personal growth as individuals within the fields of physical education, exercise science, athletic training, sport management, health promotion and wellness, and dance.

name: Young Water Professionals
summary: We are an interdisciplinary professional society for students of any major who are interested in a career in the field of water and/or wastewater.

name: For The Road
description: The idea behind For The Road is to form an organization based on a foundation of community reinvestment and support. We hope to collect donations that allow us to assemble and deliver care packages, personal items, and food to various establishments supporting disadvantaged individuals throughout the Tuscaloosa community. We strive to achieve these goals while instilling a sense of pride and service in each of our volunteers.
summary: For The Road Mission Statement

name: University of Alabama Western Equestrian Club
summary: The purpose of the Western Equestrian Club at the University of Alabama is to provide opportunities for collegiate athletes to practice and compete in Western Horsemanship.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action
description:
Our main goals are to work with members of the UA community to:

  • Reduce abortion stigma.
  • Rally activists for reproductive freedom.
  • Increase public awareness regarding reproductive health and rights. 
  • Inform members of our community about sexual health. 
  • Prioritize inclusivity, elections, and trans inclusion.
summary: Planned Parenthood Generation Action exists in order to mobilize young activists to advocate for reproductive freedom. Creating lasting change in our community, we work with Planned Parenthood to raise awareness regarding reproductive and sexual health.

name: Student's Tide Against Suicide
description: The purpose of Student’s Tide Against Suicide shall be to act as an advisory board for UA Counseling Center primarily regarding suicide prevention efforts and use the already existing Tide Against Suicide platform in order to adapt it to students and make it more focused on student involvement. It will consist of the same framework as the Counseling Center’s Tide Against Suicide which strives to prevent suicides at UA through visible, approachable, community-wide, and student-led efforts.
summary: The purpose of Student’s Tide Against Suicide shall be to act as an advisory board for UA Counseling Center primarily regarding suicide prevention efforts and use the already existing Tide Against Suicide.

name: Operation Smile Club of Alabama
description: Operation Smile has stations in over 60 countries and over 5,000 medical volunteers, and since its inception in 1982 has changed the lives of over 250,000 children around the world. Our club here at UA aims to raise money for the organization, raise awareness about the severity of being born with a cleft lip or cleft palate, and engage in service projects to benefit Operation Smile.
summary: As a branch of the largest surgical charity in the world, Operation Smile Club of Alabama focuses on fundraising for the organization as well as raising awareness about cleft lip and cleft palate deformities.

name: National Retail Federation Student Association
summary: The NRF Student Association provides talented students interested in retail and business careers unique educational and scholarship programs, networking opportunities and access to the latest industry news and research.

name: University of Alabama Disc Golf Club
description: The UADGC is a sport club that allows students of all skill levels the opportunity to learn and play disc golf both in a non-competitive and competitive environment. The club will meet once a week to play at one of the three local disc golf courses in Tuscaloosa. Additionally, a select group of players will compete in collegiate tournaments throughout the country.
summary: The UADGC is a sport club that allows students of all skill levels the opportunity to learn and play disc golf both in a non-competitive and competitive environment.

name: Tide Timing Clock Club
description: The Tide Timing Clock Club is a student organization that provides a platform for its members to gain experience and exposure to precision timing. This field deals with Positioning, Navigation, Timing, and Frequency (PNTF) standards. We hold weekly meetings, discussions, and occasional outreach.
summary: Tide Timing Clock Club (T² C²) is a platform for undergraduate and graduate students of STEM at UA. The club actively provides hands-on experience in Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT). Teaching GPS, timescale, & atomic clock development concepts.

name: STEMinists: Graduate Students in STEM
summary: STEMinists is an organization for STEM graduate students of all identities who are committed to inclusivity, social outreach, networking, mentoring, and building community at UA

name: Men Against Rape and Sexual Assault
description: MARS is a sexual-assault prevention and peer education group which seeks to educate campus community members about rape and its prevention. The mission of MARS is to be leaders who inform the campus of how to prevent rape and sexual assault, and how to help those who survive rape/sexual assault, or attempted rape/sexual assault. MARS is committed to engaging male leaders in the fight against interpersonal violence, in all forms, on the University of Alabama campus and beyond.
summary: MARS is an interpersonal violence prevention advocacy group seeking to engage male-identifying campus community members in the fight to prevent interpersonal and sexual violence on campus, and beyond.

name: STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA Ambassadors
shortName: STEM/CREATE Path to the MBA Ambassadors
summary: The STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA Ambassadors promote the STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA program to current students at UA as well as people and organizations outside of the university.

name: Public Interest Institute Student Board
description: The purposes of this organization are to promote service to the community by law students through engagement in community service and pro bono opportunities.
summary: The purposes of this association are to promote service to the community by law students.

name: Alabama Autism Advocates
description: Our club has two main goals. The first and foremost is to raise acceptance of Autism around campus and beyond. We strive to share education and experiences of Autism Spectrum Disorder. Our second priority is raising money for rural areas in Alabama to help them get the resources they need to better serve their local autistic population.
summary: Alabama Autism Advocates is a student-run organization that advocates and raises acceptance for Autism Spectrum Disorder at the University of Alabama. We also fundraise for rural communities in Alabama that need resources for their Autistic population.

name: Crimson Creatives
description:
Are you passionate about music, film, or any form of creative expression? Whether you're an artist, producer, writer, or just someone who loves talking about culture — our club is the space for you.

We’re a community of creatives, deep thinkers, and media appreciators who come together to share original work, spark meaningful conversations, and explore the constantly evolving world of Hip Hop and beyond. While our main focus is Hip Hop — from its roots to the modern underground — we also dive into movies, shows, and other genres that shape the culture.

We host fun and engaging events like album brackets, tier lists, open mic nights, and listening sessions where members can showcase their music, writing, or visual art. It’s a collaborative environment where everyone, regardless of major or background, is welcome to contribute.

Whether you're working on your next project, want feedback on your beats or lyrics, or just love breaking down classic albums and films — this is the place for you. Bring your passion, your perspective, and your playlist.

Let’s build a space where art meets conversation. Come vibe with us.
summary: We’re a community of creatives, deep thinkers, and media appreciators who come together to share original work, spark meaningful conversations, and explore the constantly evolving world of Music, Film, and Tv.

name: HopeU
summary: A passionate and hope-filled group of student activists committed to help end human slavery who believe in the power of working together to spread awareness at a local level and fight for the voiceless through education, awareness, outreach and coalition

name: The Christian Legal Society
description: Our purpose is to establish a service-oriented, intellectually-honest, inclusive community among Christians and other interested students, staff, and faculty members of the University of Alabama School of Law. CLS accomplishes this purpose through serving and encouraging people. We also foster fellowship among Christians pursuing legal studies and facilitate intellectual discussion of Christianity and its connection to the legal profession. This includes providing members with opportunities to cultivate personal and spiritual growth through communal prayer, fellowship, worship, and study.
summary: Our purpose is to establish a service-oriented, intellectually-honest, inclusive community among Christians and other interested students, staff, and faculty members of the University of Alabama School of Law.

name: Active Minds UA
description:
We aim to create mental health awareness and promote general well-being through fun interactive events. We also work to educate the student body about mental-health resources available to them both on and off campus. 

If you are interested in mental-health, getting involved on campus, promoting social inclusion, and being a part of a fun and inclusive community, then reach out and join us!
summary: Active Minds UA works to destigmatize mental health through student-led initiatives around campus.

name: Students Consulting For Nonprofit Organizations
description: • SCNO’s mission is to give back to the non-profit community by providing high-quality, impact-driven strategic consulting for nonprofits so that they can perform more efficiently and effectively. SCNO simultaneously aims to foster the growth of our student consultants through hands-on and collaborative learning, giving students real consulting experience to develop personal and professional skills.
summary: SCNO’s mission is to give back to the non-profit community by providing high-quality, impact-driven strategic consulting for nonprofits so that they can perform more efficiently and effectively.

name: HALLYUA
description: HALLYUA is a performance and social organization centered around Korean popular culture and music! We host multiple social events throughout the semester, inviting students to build community through their shared interests while promoting diversity and understanding of different cultures. We post covers and performances on our YouTube channel, perform at multiple events on and off campus, compete professionally in local and national competitions, and host an annual professional dance competition in the spring. Follow us on Instagram @hallyua_ to learn more about HALLYUA!
summary: HALLYUA is a performance, competitive, and social organization centered around Korean popular culture and music at the University of Alabama! We post dance covers on YouTube and host multiple social events on and around campus! Follow our IG: @hallyua_

name: Housing and Residential Communities
description:
Living on campus is the key to a great start at The University of Alabama. Residential living puts you at the heart of campus life, where you will interact with a diverse community of people.

With up to 8,600 residents on campus in 33 residence halls, we are dedicated to propelling residents to a successful transition to life on campus with the aid of our 236 resident advisors as well as our 19 graduate community directors.

It is our mission to support students’ development, learning, and engagement through intentionally designed environments, programs, and services.
summary: Living on campus is the key to a great start at The University of Alabama. Residential living puts you at the heart of campus life, where you will interact with a diverse community of people.

name: Off-Campus Resources
description:
When transitioning to living off campus, the Housing and Residential Communities’ Off-Campus Resources team acts as UA’s main channel for information about off-campus living in order to empower UA students to make informed decisions about their housing options.

This offices provides resources to help students succeed as members of the local community, understand and advocate for their own rights and responsibilities, and persevere in navigating challenging situations faced for the first time.
summary: This offices provides resources to help students succeed as members of the local community, understand and advocate for their own rights and responsibilities, and persevere in navigating challenging situations faced for the first time.

name: Counseling Center
description:
The Counseling Center helps students, undergraduate and graduate, achieve academic success and personal growth through quality brief counseling and psychological services, outreach, and consultative services, and the training of mental health professionals.

Students know the Counseling Center for its outstanding counseling services and counseling resources, as well as innovative programs including Stress Free Daze, which provides an opportunity for students to reduce their stress before finals week each semester.

The Counseling Center also offers information on how faculty, staff, parents, and others can support their students, specific information on helping students in crisis, along with further information on self help.

UA offers two full-time psychiatrists, one psychiatric nurse practitioner and seven other physicians who can assist in screening for general mental health issues through the Student Health Center. The UA Counseling Center staff includes 18 licensed clinicians (psychologists, licensed professional counselors, and licensed social workers). The Women and Gender Resource Center employs five staff therapists and two victim advocates.

Come to 3000 South Lawn Office Building (1101 Jackson Avenue) or call 205-348-3863 during operating hours, or call the UA Police Department at 205-348-5454 and ask to speak with the on-call counselor when the center is closed. You may also text BAMA to 741-741 to text with a trained volunteer.
summary: The University of Alabama Counseling Center’s mission is to help UA students achieve academic success and personal growth through quality brief counseling and psychological services.

name: Health Promotion and Wellness
description: The Department of Health Promotion and Wellness (HPW) collaborates with The University of Alabama students, faculty and staff to promote a healthy campus environment, enhance student well-being and foster student empowerment to achieve optimal health and academic success through seven strategic areas. HPW offers individual and group education on a variety of health topics as well as presentations for student organizations and classes, special health awareness events and resources for students with academic projects.
summary: The Department of Health Promotion and Wellness (HPW) collaborates with The University of Alabama students, faculty and staff to promote a healthy campus environment, enhance student well-being and foster student empowerment to achieve optimal health and

name: University Recreation
description: University Recreation’s facilities and programs provide the perfect places to get moving and keep an active lifestyle. Whether you want to work out solo or sweat with friends, UREC has something for everyone. The Student Recreation Center with Outdoor Pool Complex, Robert E. Witt Student Activity Center, and Aquatic Center offer unique spaces for you to either challenge yourself with something new or stick with a consistent routine. University Recreation provides contemporary and relevant opportunities for enhanced health and well-being through the engagement and participation of students, faculty, staff, alumni, and community members. All you need is your Action Card!
summary: University Recreation’s facilities and programs provide the perfect places to get moving and keep an active lifestyle.

name: Career Center
description:
The Career Center partners with students as they explore possibilities, develop skills, and connect with opportunities.

Students can seek guidance selecting a major, planning a career, developing job-search strategies, and more. Our staff welcomes opportunities to speak to classes and student organizations about career preparation, professional school planning, and services we offer, and career consultants can help assess how personal factors influence career choices.

The Career Center hosts several career fairs and utilizes Handshake, a job and internship platform, to connect students, alumni, and employers. The center also facilitates on-campus interviewing and information sessions to recruiters interested in recruiting at the Capstone.
summary: The Career Center partners with students as they explore possibilities, develop skills, and connect with opportunities.

name: Student Care and Well-Being
description:
The Student Care and Well-Being office was established to provide assistance and case management for students in crisis and outreach to parents and families. The staff in this area coordinates a range of services to assist students with issues and concerns that may interfere with their academic success.

The programs and services Student Care and Well-Being offers are detailed throughout this site. If you have questions about services available, please feel free to contact the office via email at bamacares@ua.edu or by phone at 205-348-2461.

In case of emergency, evenings after 5 p.m., weekends, and holidays please contact the University Police Department at 205-348-5454.
summary: The Student Care and Well-Being office was established to provide assistance and case management for students in crisis and outreach to parents and families.

name: Student Conduct
description:
The Office of Student Conduct seeks to maximize each UA student’s learning experience by educating students about the Student Code of Conduct, as well as the Capstone Creed. When necessary, the office also serves as the main campus entity to work with students to resolve disciplinary matters.

Through the conduct process, Student Conduct, part of UA’s Division of Student Life, aims to help students understand the impact their behavior has on the community and to assist them with making decisions that lead to personal and professional success.

Students, faculty and staff who believe there has been a violation can contact the Office of Student Conduct to discuss options available for reporting incidents to the appropriate authorities. OSC staff members are also available for educational programs on a variety of related topics.

The Capstone Creed states “As a member of The University of Alabama community, I will pursue knowledge; act with fairness, integrity and respect; promote equity and inclusion; foster individual and civic responsibility; and strive for excellence in all I do.”
summary: The Office of Student Conduct seeks to maximize each UA student’s learning experience by educating students about the Student Code of Conduct, as well as the Capstone Creed.

name: Veteran and Military Affairs
description:
