Mission: The mission of The Office for Veteran and Military Affairs is to provide, facilitate, or coordinate programs and services to meet the special needs of veterans, service members, dependents, and survivors in order to ease their transition to college life and fulfill their educational goals.

Vision: The vision of the Office for Veteran and Military Affairs is to ensure that The University of Alabama will be the “University of Choice” for all student veterans, service members, dependents, and survivors by being recognized nationally as a “Military and Veteran Friendly University”.
summary: The Office for Veteran and Military Affairs provides, facilitates, and coordinates programs and services to meet the special needs of veterans, service members, dependents, and survivors.

name: Dean of Students
description:
The Dean of Students office provides support and guidance to UA students and families. Emphasizing inclusivity, advocacy, and student empowerment, the Dean of Students office offers a wide variety of specialized intervention, educational, and supportive services to students, parents, and families.

The Dean of Students oversees Blackburn Institute, Center for Service and Leadership, Parent & Family Programs, Student Care and Well-Being, Student Conduct, Veteran and Military Affairs, and the Women and Gender Resource Center. In addition to these functional areas, the Dean of Students oversees the National Student Exchange program.
summary: The Dean of Students office provides support and guidance to UA students and families.

name: Student Life
description:
The Division of Student Life at The University of Alabama provides intentional programs and support services designed to maximize student learning. We know that students are constantly learning beyond the classroom, and our programs complement academic pursuits, support well-being, and promote student development.

To support student learning, we seek to provide transformative learning experiences by collaborating and partnering with academic departments, faculty, the community, and others. From Living-Learning Communities to peer mentoring, from intramural athletics to career counseling, Student Life programs provide a variety of leadership and engagement opportunities that encourage students to explore their passions and develop their skills.
summary: The Division of Student Life empowers and supports students in maximizing their UA learning experiences.

name: Student Media
description:
Student Media trains and educates students through hands-on experiences and informs the campus with a variety of student-run broadcast, digital, online and print media. The skills and proficiencies gained in working in Student Media develop from out-of-classroom experiences that expand upon knowledge gained from professors and textbooks.

The Office of Student Media aspires to provide UA students with the opportunity to develop skills to help them communicate effectively, think critically, work cooperatively and prepare for successful careers across continents and cultures.

The Office of Student Media will train and enable students to produce quality media that deliver relevant news, information, and entertainment, and operate free from prior restraint or censorship in a socially and financially-responsible manner.
summary: Student Media trains and educates students through hands-on experiences and informs the campus with a variety of student-run broadcast, digital, online and print media.

name: Parent and Family Programs
description:
Parents and Families,

As a valued member of The University of Alabama family, we are excited and proud to have you as a partner in your student’s success here at the Capstone. You play a crucial role in your student’s college career and we are happy to work together, with you, to maximize your student’s learning experience.

Within the Division of Student Life, we combine in-class experiences with opportunities outside the classroom to provide each student with a transformative education. Whether serving in a leadership role on campus or participating in intramural sports, you can help promote involvement to your student, helping them feel and be connected to campus.

It is our goal to assist you in supporting the transition and academic success of your student. We serve as the communication link between the University and parents. We recognize you know your student best and ask you to utilize Parent & Family Programs as a resource when you feel your student needs extra support on campus or when you have a question or concern. Keep a close check on this website and complete your free profile in The UA Crimson Connection to stay in the know with campus happenings.

If you ever have additional questions, or if we can help in any way, please contact Parent & Family Programs using the toll-free Parent & Family Helpline at 1-800-392-2777, or e-mail parents@ua.edu.

Best Wishes,

The Parent & Family Programs staff
summary: Parent and Family Programs seeks to serve as a resource for all parents and family members.

name: Student Government Association Executive Council
description: The University of Alabama Student Government Association strives to promote a thriving campus through initiatives, events, and partnerships with many organizations. We believe that the student body comes first and we are here to represent any concerns that come about in each student’s lives. Please come by the SGA office in the Ferguson Center on campus, or feel free to contact any member to address your concerns.
summary: The University of Alabama Student Government Association strives to promote a thriving campus through initiatives, events, and partnerships with many organizations.

name: UA Homecoming
description: Student-led Homecoming festivities are run through the Division of Student Life. To learn more about how to register for and participate in Homecoming events, visit the Homecoming website. Signature events include the Roll Tide Run, the Choreography Competition, the NPHC Step Show, Day of Service, and the annual Homecoming Parade.
summary: Homecoming engages The University of Alabama community in a cohesive celebration of the Capstone’s rich traditions.

name: Theta XI
summary: Fraternity

name: Assessment and Planning
description: The mission of Assessment and Planning is to improve the student experience by strengthening the practice of student life professionals and departments through sound methodologically rigorous evaluation techniques and innovative analytics, thereby creating a data driven culture for informed decision making within the Division of Student Life.
summary: Creating a data driven culture for informed decision making within the Division of Student Life

name: University of Alabama Student Chapter of the Institute of Transportation Engineers
description: The University of Alabama Student Chapter of the Institute of Transportation Engineers aims to promote interest in the transportation planning and engineering profession. The purpose of this organization is to provide a platform for students to showcase their work, improve their knowledge base by inviting internal/external speakers on seminars, and most importantly get connected with the transportation industry for future professional development. Persons interested in becoming members may join after attending two consecutive meetings and paying the required dues. No formal application or interview is required. Students are encouraged to join the international organization of the Institute of Transportation Engineers as student members. Annual dues of $10.00 shall be paid by September 30 of each year, after which a member will be dropped by nonpayment of dues. In order to maintain active membership status, a member must attend 2 meetings per semester. Failure to meet this requirement will result in expulsion from the association. Expelled members must wait a semester (excluding summers) before they may seek active membership status. Membership in the University of Alabama Student Chapter of the Institute of Transportation Engineers shall be open to all students of The University of Alabama, without regard to race, religion, sex, disability, or national origin.
summary: This organization aims to provide a platform for students to showcase their work, improve their knowledge by inviting internal/external speakers on seminars, and most importantly get connected with the transportation industry for professional development

name: Tikkun Olam Makers Medical Engineering Group
description:
UA’s Tikkun Olam Makers is centered around designing low-cost, open-source solutions for those with disabilities in Alabama. We seek to culminate a diverse group of students to solve and support intense engineering challenges with new technologies in the field of rapid prototyping including 3D printing, laser-cutting, and more. We have partnered with the Cube, the University's 3D printing lab, along with several local MakerSpaces to source these traditionally expensive machines.

In 2025, UA participated in the TOM Global Innovation Challenge. Out of 470 participants from 33 campuses across 13 countries, UA was the only US university to win a grand prize and one of only two US universities to win multiple prizes.

The University of Alabama's Chapter of TOM is centered around innovation and social entrepreneurship. This culminated in the first-ever TOM Pitch-a-thon, in collaboration with InnovateAlabama, The Kennemer Center, The Cube, and the Engineering Entrepreneurship Program. Working solo or in up to teams of 5, participants were given 5 different case studies of people with disabilities and had 2.5 hours to ideate a solution to help one of them. Participants presented their ideas to judges with a mix of presentation slides and prototypes. With access to monitors, craft supplies, and whiteboards, groups had a plethora of resources to ideate.

We have also established a diverse group of engineering students, as well as a group of varied medical students, and business students to effectively manage any and all challenges the club may face. 

We are working with the international non-profit group Tikkun Olam Makers to help research cheap alternatives to traditional manufacturing. The devices we develop will be accessible to other Tikkun Olam Maker groups who can recreate the work we complete to help solve their own problems.
summary: TOM Engineering is centered around designing low-cost, open-source solutions for the elderly and people with disabilities. By combining social entrepreneurship with frugal innovation, we utilize engineering to improve lives.

name: Crimson Yogis
description: The Crimson Yogis are dedicated to creating a community where we can practice yoga, destress from college life stresses, maintain physical and mental health, and make new friendships throughout the process.
summary: The Crimson Yogis are dedicated to creating a community where we can practice yoga, destress from college life stresses, maintain physical and mental health, and make new friendships throughout the process.

name: Division of Opportunities, Connections, and Success
description: The Division of Opportunities, Connections and Success will promote access and engagement for everyone in the UA community and offer three primary initiatives to help prepare all students and employees for success on campus and beyond.
summary: The Division of Opportunities, Connections, and Success provides leadership for the University in collaborating with existing and future University efforts to support all students seeking educational access and academic achievement.

name: Japanese Language and Culture Society
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote an interest in the language and culture of Japan while also furthering the educational and academic advancement related to the subject.

name: Student Advocates for Foster Families
summary: The purpose of this association is to educate others on the foster care system and needs of foster families, while providing opportunities for students to serve foster families.

name: Masters of Public Administration Student Association
description: The purpose of this association is to extend the Masters of Public Administration (MPA) program’s mission of preparing MPA graduate and undergraduate Accelerated Master’s Program (AMP) students to lead and manage public service organizations. This extension is accomplished by putting opportunity and responsibility into the hands of the MPA students themselves to add on to what they learn in the academic program. The MPA Student Association is an opportunity for all of those involved in the MPA program to be able to engage in public-service oriented activities such as community service, recruitment to the MPA program, creation of informative newsletters, and more. The MPA Student Association will uplift its members with the guaranteed support of a network of other MPA students in the MPA Student Association, the MPA Student Association advisor, and other MPA faculty.
summary: The purpose of this association is to extend the Masters of Public Administration (MPA) program’s mission of preparing MPA graduate and undergraduate Accelerated Master’s Program (AMP) students to lead and manage public service organizations.

name: Masters in Management Information Systems
description: MMIS is an affinity group serving AMP and traditional Masters students of the MIS program at The University of Alabama. The core of our organization concerns highlighting personal and professional growth, and we aim to facilitate the following three areas of connections: with technology leaders through speaker events, within MMIS through social events, and with undergraduate MIS students through formal mentorship. By highlighting the importance of giving back to the entirety of the MIS community within all facets of our programming, our goal is that all MMIS members will not only benefit personally and professionally but will also contribute to the collective growth and success of the MIS program at UA.
summary: MMIS serves all MIS Masters students to build stronger personal and professional relationships through community events and professional development programming.

name: Society of Art History Students (The Guild)
description:
We are a small group of emerging art historians with a wide range of skills and experience. We love what we do, and we do it with passion.

The Society of Art History Students, or The Guild, is a new campus organization where students can gather to discuss topics of Art History. To join, one does not have to be an Art History major or minor but should have a keen interest in the discipline.
Activities during meetings include discussions of art, faculty spotlights, and items of interest, such as using an Oculus program to view Gothic Cathedrals, or watching films or documentaries related to Art History.
An interest in a specific period, style, or artist is not required. This organization is intended to have an amalgamation of interests coming from each member. With this, fruitful and robust discussions can be held, where students learn from each other.
Resources for undergraduates will be provided, such as opportunities for paper submissions and useful sources regarding graduate school applications. Additionally, if students are interested in help from their peers regarding their Art History classes, this organization can facilitate such needs.
summary: The Society of Art History Students, or The Guild for short, is a group of art-history enthusiasts, regardless of major, who partake in social and developmental events to spread excitement for and knowledge about art history on campus!

name: Student Engagement
description: Student Engagement fosters an environment that supports growth and development of students through active participation in intentional experiential learning opportunities which promote student learning that leads to student success at the Capstone and beyond. As a collaborative unit, we seek to capture and leverage the affinity created among these experiences to build community and further enhance the holistic student experience.
summary: Student Engagement fosters an environment for student growth and high impact practices.

name: Student Life Communications
description: Student Life Communications
summary: Student Life Communications

name: UA Club Fencing
description: Come join us to learn how to fence! No experience is required. We fence with the three Olympic weapons of foil, épée, and saber. We attend competitions across the Southeast, but you don't have to compete to be an active member.
summary: UA Club Fencing is dedicated to the Olympic sport of Fencing

name: Bama Brazillian Jiu-Jitsu
description:
Students can practice and learn Jiu-Jitsu martial arts techniques in a safe, clean, and friendly environment. Learn self-defense, build confidence, make friends, be part of a community, and get in shape. Bama BJJ is open to people of all skill and fitness levels, whether they have no experience, or are veterans of the sport. We work with Cobra Brazillian Jiu-Jitsu, a local Brazillian Jiu-Jitsu gym that provides a space for skills-building and we also use the URec combat room and other campus spaces. We learn from each other and guest teachers! 

Brazilian jiu-jitsu focuses on getting an opponent to the ground in order to neutralize possible strength or size advantages through techniques and submissions. On the ground, physical strength can be offset or enhanced through proper grappling techniques. This makes it great for self-defense. It is also a fun and competitive sport!

UPDATED MEETING LOCATION AND TIME:

Meeting Location: Cobra Brazilian Jiu Jitsu, Tuscaloosa, AL.

 Meeting Times: Sundays @6pm-8pm
summary: This organization gives students of all skill levels opportunities to practice & learn Brazillian Jiu-Jitsu (BJJ ). We work in partnership with a local Jiu-Jitsu gym. All are welcome! All athletic levels are welcome! No equipment needed.

name: The Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description: SASE UA exists to prepare the growing Asian population at the University of Alabama for the professional world, to promote inclusivity and tolerance on campus, and to give back to the local community. SASE UA will provide access to national conferences and career fairs, resume reviews, and interview preparation. SASE UA strives to create a welcoming, diverse community.
summary: SASE UA exists to prepare the growing Asian population at the University of Alabama for the professional world, to promote inclusivity and tolerance on campus, and to give back to the local community.

name: SKY Campus Happiness at UA
summary: SKY Campus Happiness at UA  is a humanitarian student organization at the University of Alabama. SKY@ UA aims to promote wellbeing, happiness, inclusivity and diversity, service, and leadership, creating a stress-free and violence-free campus community.

name: The Federalist Society
description: The purpose of this Federalist Society Chapter is to foster critical thought and debate among Alabama Law students about the application of conservative and libertarian principles to the law. The Chapter embraces the principle that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to the integrity of the American Constitution, and that it is the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is—not what it should be.
summary: The Federalist Society fosters critical thought and debate among Alabama Law students about the application of conservative and libertarian principles to the law.

name: Historical Martial Arts Organization
description: The Historical Martial Arts club focuses on learning the practices, history, and techniques of martial arts from a variety of times and places across history. We focus on teaching and learning technique via meetings and sparring using safe equipment. Prior experience is not required, and any interest in swordsmanship, historical martial arts, or even fantasy and other media that features parts of historical martial arts is eagerly welcomed. We draw from a wide range of abilities, cultures, and teachings in order to share and learn as a group, and have fun dueling and sparring.
summary: The core was to research and study from ancient resources, restore and practice martial arts with safety equipments.

name: End Overdose at the University of Alabama
description:
Our mission is to make resources preventing opioid overdose (i.e. fentanyl test strips and Narcan/naloxone) readily available to the University of Alabama community and beyond. Also, the organization aims to raise public awareness and education of drug-related overdose deaths and how they can be prevented.

https://endoverdose.net/
summary: Our mission is to make resources preventing opioid overdose readily available to the University of Alabama community and beyond. Also, the organization aims to raise public awareness and education of drug-related overdose deaths and how to prevent them.

name: The  Oaks
description: The Oaks is a branch of Church at the Oaks of Tuscaloosa. We meet every Sunday morning at 9:30am and 11am in Moody Music Hall.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to give students a place to belong, a hope to believe in, and opportunities to contribute to the overall well-being of The University of Alabama campus.

name: UA Women's Water Polo Club
description: We are an intercollegiate, women's water polo club at the University of Alabama. We compete in tournaments with other club teams around the country through the Collegiate Water Polo Association.
summary: We are an intercollegiate, women's water polo club at the University of Alabama. We compete in tournaments with other club teams around the country through the Collegiate Water Polo Association.

name: Arts Leadership Association
description: The purpose of ALA shall be to provide a safe environment for arts leadership students at The University of Alabama to learn about the performing arts industry and grow as leaders. In an ever-changing field, ALA shall strive to keep its members informed and maintain a strong leadership network to encourage engagement and development.
summary: The purpose of ALA shall be to provide a safe environment for arts leadership students at The University of Alabama to learn about the performing arts industry and grow as leaders.

name: Bama Bee Club
description: The purpose of this association is to promote native pollinator populations, advocate for issues relevant to pollinators, and increase local conservation efforts. It shall also work towards educating the University of Alabama’s campus and the town of Tuscaloosa about pollinators native and non-native to Alabama through events and keeping our own beehives at the UA arboretum.
summary: The purpose of this association is to promote native pollinator populations, advocate for issues relevant to pollinators, and increase local conservation efforts as well as tend to our own beehives in an educational way.

name: ONE
summary: The ONE College Ministry is a Christian college ministry led by Church of the Highlands that exists to take students on a spiritual journey to Know God, Find Freedom, Discover their Purpose, and Make a Difference.

name: Racquet Club
summary: The Racquet Club seeks to promote the sport of racquetball, health, and wellness to all UA students. At the same time, provide opportunities for members to play and learn the skills and techniques through training and practice sessions.

name: The Culverhouse Academic Honor Council
description:
The purpose of the student-led AHC in the Culverhouse College of Business is to promote honor and integrity in the study and practice of business in accordance with the values of the Capstone Creed, “As a member of the University of Alabama community, I will pursue knowledge; act with fairness, honesty, and respect; foster individual and civic responsibility; and strive for excellence.”

The AHC will strive to:

  • Promote academic integrity and help educate business students about how develop and maintain strong character and ethical habits in their personal and professional lives.
  • Help organize and implement integrity-related events each semester, e.g., “Integrity Week,” the J. Craig Smith Business Ethics Lecture, and an integrity-focused competition.
  • Provide classroom presentations to business classes on academic integrity and its importance.
  • Maintain active, positive internet presence, including in the college website and social media.
  • Interact and partner with the college’s Ethics Advisory Committee to pursue ethics-related objectives.
  • If requested, provide input to the Academic Misconduct Monitor and/or Dean on allegations of academic misconduct.
summary: The purpose of the student-led AHC in the Culverhouse College of Business is to promote honor and integrity in the study and practice of business in accordance with the values of the Capstone Creed

name: Graduate Society of Women Engineers
description:
The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a nationally recognized professional, educational, non-profit, and service organization dedicated to promoting interest in and encouraging women to pursue the fields of science and engineering.

GradSWE is the graduate student faction of The University of Alabama SWE chapter. The focus of this group is to provide networking events, mentorship and outreach opportunities, and career development which cater to graduate women in engineering and science.

Our mission is to provide graduate students with resources they need to excel and graduate with their advanced degrees in order to successfully transition into their desired career.
summary: GradSWE is a part of the larger Society of Women Engineers (SWE) organization. SWE, founded in 1950, is a not-for-profit educational and service organization that empowers women to succeed in STEM (or STEAM) careers.

name: Capstone Collegiate Real Estate Academy
description:
The goal of this organization is formal real estate career development. 

This includes providing on & off-campus industry engagement, exclusive student member resources, and opportunities for real estate students as they prepare for internships and jobs during their undergraduate and graduate Capstone academic experience.

Events and programs within the Academy will include but are not limited to, guest speakers, skill building such as online financial modeling courses, national competitions, a mentorship program, and more.

Membership is open to all juniors and seniors who are Real Estate minors and want to take the extra step to set themselves up for success by earning the Academy’s industry-recognized “Pathway Advantage” Certificate.
summary: The goal of this organization is real estate career development. This includes providing on & off-campus industry engagement, exclusive student member resources, and opportunities for real estate students as they prepare for internships and jobs.

name: Phi Sigma Rho
description: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority for STEM majors. We aim to foster the sorority culture that is prominent on campus, but also bring the grace and understanding that is required for students pursuing STEM majors.
summary: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority for engineering and engineering technology majors. We aim to foster the sorority culture that is prominent on campus, but also bring the grace and understanding that is required for students pursuing STEM majors.

name: Students Demand Action UA
description:
We focus on mitigating the causes and effects of all forms of gun violence that plague our country by organizing in schools and communities to educate students, register voters, and demand common-sense solutions to this national public health crisis.

We work to prevent gun violence because it is the leading cause of death for children and teens in the United States. Every day, more than 100 people are shot and killed and over 200 more are wounded. The daily toll of gun violence in our country doesn’t always make headlines, but this violence affects everyone, everywhere.
summary: Students Demand Action is the largest grassroots youth led gun violence prevention organization. We are working in communities across the country to end the gun violence crisis.

name: Business Law Society
summary: The purposes of BLS are to present educational public discussion groups, forums, panels, or lectures relating to business law in efforts to elicit insights and broaden the perspectives of student members before graduation.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America at the University of Alabama
description:
ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals for America) is a national
nonprofit organization created in 1972. This organization focuses on
providing professional development workshops and career resources to its
members to empower and develop leaders for the nation. ALPFA currently has over 92,000 professional and student members across the country. Learn more at ALPFA.org

 

Click here to join ALPFA UA today!
summary: UA chapter of the national nonprofit ALPFA. We provide professional development workshops and career resources to our members to empower and develop leaders for the nation. Our members access scholarships, career fairs, networking events and mentorship.

name: The OM Collective
summary: The OM Collective is an organization dedicated to professional, personal, and social development in the major of Operations Management. Our main goal is to empower our fellow students who will be entering the operations management industry.

name: Women in Physics and Astronomy
description: Women do physics too! WiPA was established to provide a community for women and other gender minorities in physics and astronomy. We have a mix of career/academic development and social based meetings every three weeks. We are committed to providing you a space where you can be your authentic self in STEM!
summary: We are a club celebrating women and gender minorities in physics and astronomy with equal parts social and career development meetings and events!

name: Phi Alpha Honors Society
description: The purpose of Phi Alpha Honor Society is to provide a closer bond among students of social work and promote humanitarian goals and ideas. Phi Alpha fosters high standards of education for social workers and invites into membership those who have attained excellence in scholarship and achievement in social work.
summary: The purpose of Phi Alpha is to provide a closer bond among students and promote humanitarian goals and ideals. Phi Alpha is dedicated to honoring equality, inclusivity, justice and fostering a community where individuals from all walks of life.

name: Lee J. Styslinger Jr. College of Engineering Ambassadors
description: As official student public relations representatives, ambassadors, tour guides, and student recruitment agents for the Styslinger College of Engineering, it is our purpose that we provide services, promote pride, and communicate an increasing understanding of the College of Engineering and the University of Alabama. It is our responsibility to represent the College in a credible and positive manner with a sense of dedication and responsibility to the students, faculty, administration, alumni, and the community.
summary: The Ambassadors of the Styslinger College of Engineering use their passion and enthusiasm for engineering to serve as the official representatives of the College of Engineering. They function as hosts and hostesses for events and prospective students.

name: The Accounting Masters Association
description: The Accounting Masters Association has existed for 15 years. The purpose of the organization is to organize and lead mentoring events for undergraduate accounting students. It also helps to network with the Young Professionals Board, which is comprised of recent accounting graduates across the country. It lastly provides support for students to prepare for and pass the Certified Public Accounting (CPA) exam
summary: Masters of Accountancy and Tax students.

name: The Higher Education Assembly of Research in Tuscaloosa
description: The Higher Education Assembly of Research in Tuscaloosa (HEART) does hereby dedicate itself to the enhancement of the research experience for African American students and other minority students at the University of Alabama and Stillman College. These efforts extend across the entire spectrum of the undergraduate and graduate research experience. This experience includes but is not limited to: facilitating the transition from undergraduate to graduate school, providing academic support, recruiting, and providing opportunities for fellowship, service, and professional development to students interested in research across all disciplines.
summary: The Higher Education Assembly of Research in Tuscaloosa (HEART) is to serve as a source of social, emotional, and professional support for minority students involved in research and to actively promote the interests of these individuals

name: Tide Talks Wealth
description: Tide Talks Wealth is a finance organization that provides training, resources, and mentorship to help members find their career path through a more holistic understanding of the industry. Through our year-long training program, members will grasp an understanding of the industry landscape, market concepts, personal branding, and financial literacy. In addition to lessons, meetings will consist of guest speakers and employer networking opportunities.
summary: Tide Talks Wealth is an all-women's finance organization that provides training, resources, and mentorship to help members find their career path through a more holistic understanding of the industry.

name: UA Flute Society
description:
The mission of the UA Flute Society is to promote the art of flute playing at the University of Alabama. Our objectives are to:

  1. Provide performance (solo and chamber) and educational opportunities to members;
  2. Engage in community outreach activities to encourage the academic study of the flute;
  3. Socialize with others who share the same mutual interest in flute playing and flute music;
  4. Connect with professional flutists and other collegiate flute groups to learn from them and share our passion for music.

The UA Flute Society is not exclusively for music majors/minors; we will welcome and encourage all students at University of Alabama that have interest in flute playing and/or flute music. We welcome students of any age and any skill level. In addition to providing educational experiences for advanced, pre-professional musicians, this club seeks to harbor opportunities for students who wish to play flute as a hobby or as a secondary instrument. To be granted membership to the UA Flute Society, the prospective member must fit at least one of the categories listed below:

  1. Be enrolled in the MUA course for Flute Studio/flute lessons/Woodwind Methods OR
  2. Be an active member of a large ensemble at UA (such as the Million Dollar Band, Wind Ensemble, Symphonic Band, Concert Band, etc.) OR
  3. Be able to describe their experience as a flute player and how joining the society would benefit them

In the event that a prospective member does not fit any of these categories, their membership may be reviewed by the advisor and council as a special case.
summary: The mission of the UA Flute Society is to promote the art of flute playing at the University of Alabama through performance, education, and fellowship.

name: Will Nolan Film Club
description:
The Will Nolan Film Club hosts bi-weekly viewings and discussions of classic and noteworthy films. Our meetings are completely free; we also provide free snacks and drinks.

Our mission is to to build a community of film lovers at the University of Alabama. We strive to create constructive conversation around the arts and to encourage people to think about the media they consume. We are named after the late professor, Will Nolan, and aim to honor his legacy.
summary: The Will Nolan Film Club hosts viewings and discussions of notable films in order to grow the understanding of cinema and continue on with the late instructor, Will Nolan.

name: The University of Alabama Chapter of the National Sports Media Association
description:
The purpose of this Chapter shall be to encourage the understanding of current theories and procedures in the practice of journalism/sports media, to provide and promote a better understanding of the functions of sports media and its values, to stimulate and encourage sports media professionalism through sports media education, to apply the skills, creativity and energy of sports media to help become better communicators, to develop the individual abilities of its members, to provide students of sports media with the opportunity to become acquainted not only with their peers but with professional practitioners as well, and to encourage students to adhere to the highest ideals and principles of the practice of sports media and to instill in them a professional attitude. 

 

For more information regarding the NSMA, contact Dr. Sean Sadri (srsadri@ua.edu).
summary: The goal of UA NSMA is to develop educational opportunities for those who are interested in pursuing a career in sports media. This student chapter helps aspiring professionals make connections with contacts in the sports media field.

name: The Journal of the Legal Profession
description: Membership is reserved for students actively pursuing a J.D. from The University of Alabama School of Law. All J.D. candidates in good academic standing are eligible to participate in the summer-write on process. The write-on process consists of the Case Note and Bluebook Exam. Following successful completion of the summer write-on process, invitations will be extended for membership in the Journal.
summary: The Journal of the Legal Profession is the nation's oldest periodical exploring the dynamic nature of the legal profession. The Journal is student-run by the Managing Board and Junior Editors.

name: Alabama Club Hockey ACHA M3
description: Alabama Club Hockey ACHA M3 is a club hockey team at the University of Alabama, competing at the Division 2 level within the American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA). As a student-run organization, we are committed to offering a competitive, yet balanced hockey experience while fostering a strong sense of community and school pride. We compete in the College Hockey South Division and have made national appearances while a member of the Amateur Athletic Union (AAU) the last four seasons. This will be our first season as a member of the ACHA.
summary: Alabama Hockey M2 is a club team at the University of Alabama that competes at the Division 2 level in the American Collegiate Hockey Association (ACHA).

name: Alabama Sport Management Association
description: ASMA aspires to promote student involvement and professional development as well as provide networking opportunities and extracurricular learning opportunities for students interested in the sport industry. By inviting guest speakers and offering opportunities to engage with the industry firsthand, we hope to help our members launch their careers in sport.
summary: ASMA aspires to promote student involvement and professional development as well as provide networking opportunities and extracurricular learning opportunities for students interested in the sport industry.

name: Student Life Business Services
summary: This is the departmental MySource page for the Student Life Business Services team.

name: Girls Club Alabama
description: A platform for UA students looking to network and learn about job opportunities in the sports business industry.
summary: A club for students who love sports or want to work in sports to come together, network, build connections in the sports industry.

name: SmartPay Society
summary: SmartPay Society is The University of Alabama’s first Fintech (Financial Technology) Club which’s primary mission is to educate members and the community about financial technology trends, innovations and opportunities.

name: Baby Steps at The University of Alabama Student Organization
description:
Baby Steps is a nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering pregnant and parenting college students facing an unplanned pregnancy to have their education and their baby by providing housing, community, and support. 

The goal of the student organization is to raise awareness about Baby Steps on campus and the resources it provides Student-Moms facing an unplanned pregnancy. Members have various volunteer opportunities to support and empower Student-Moms and their Tiny Trunks each semester. 

Let’s create a culture of empowerment for Student-Moms on our campus!
summary: The Baby Steps Student Organization connects with students to fundraise and build community to further the mission of Baby Steps: empowering students facing an unplanned pregnancy to have their education and their baby.

name: Deeper Life Campus Fellowship
description:
The Deeper Life Campus Fellowship, University of Alabama is a christian campus fellowship.  Its objectives and aims encompass extending the gospel of Jesus Christ to UA students and the broader campus community. This involves creating a robust local community of young individuals, prepared to provide mutual assistance and affection, while also fostering the development and preparation of students for comprehensive success in life.

Membership in this organization shall be open to all students of The University of Alabama, without regard to any federally protected class, except in cases of designated fraternal organizations exempted by federal law from Title IX regulations concerning discrimination on the basis of sex.

Participation in this organization is available to all students enrolled at The University of Alabama. A formal application process is unnecessary for membership. Regular gatherings of the organization are scheduled for Sunday mornings, with supplementary meetings planned for various days and times throughout the week.
summary: The organization  objectives and aims encompass extending the gospel of Jesus Christ to UA students and the broader campus community.

name: The Joy Project
description: The Joy Project is a student-led volunteer organization dedicated to supporting the youth of the Tuscaloosa community. Our mission is to spread joy by hosting events and initiatives that uplift underserved children. From our annual holiday festival, which provides gifts and celebration without financial burden, to our Letters for Joy pen pal program that fosters literacy and mentorship, we strive to make a meaningful impact. Join us to bring smiles, build connections, and create lasting memories for children in need!
summary: The Joy Project is a student-led volunteer group bringing joy to underserved children in Tuscaloosa. Through events like our annual holiday festival and Letters for Joy pen pal program, we provide gifts, mentorship, and meaningful connections.

name: Science of Psychedelics Student Organization
description: In the Science of Psychedelics Student Organization, students are given a space to foster an evidence-based knowledge of psychedelics. In our meetings, student members will lead discussions on various aspects of psychedelics. These topics include but are not limited to the history of psychedelic research, the current state and future of psychedelic therapy and research, the applications of psychedelics in mental health disorders, and the laws surrounding psychedelic research. Our organization does not promote the recreational use of psychedelics, but rather a critical and scientific approach to view these substances.
summary: The purpose of the SOPSO is to explore and promote scientific research, education, and discussion related to the study of psychedelics, their effects on the mind and consciousness, and their potential therapeutic applications.

name: Women in Pre-Law Society
description: WIPLS is to foster a powerful and inclusive environment that empowers students in pre-law track at The University of Alabama, enabling them to confidently pursue their dreams and become influential leaders in the legal profession and beyond. We strive to create a transformative experience by equipping our members with the essential skills, knowledge, and support network necessary to excel not only in law school but throughout their entire professional journey. Through our commitment to cultivating a diverse and inclusive community, we aim to foster a sense of belonging, respect, and collaboration, where every member feels empowered to embrace their unique perspectives, challenge boundaries, and shape the future of justice and equity.
summary: The vision is to prepare, uplift and encourage students in pre-law at The University of Alabama. We plan on setting up our members with the tools to succeed in law school and beyond, while providing a diverse sense of community and be and belonging.

name: Youth Movement Against Alzheimer's
description: The purpose of this organization is to empower and activate college students across the U.S. to change the stigma of Alzheimer’s disease through education, awareness, and collective action. Our aim is to reduce the stereotype associated with Alzheimer’s disease and dementia by promoting disease education, providing opportunities to connect and support those affected by the disease, and inspire a generation of youth to take action to protect their own brain health by learning about and practicing healthy brain habits.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower and activate college students across the U.S. to change the stigma of Alzheimer’s disease through education, awareness, and collective action.

name: The American Civic Republicans
description: The purpose of the American Civic Republicans is to bring together conservative, politically-minded University of Alabama students to jointly advocate for and embody civic republican principles on campus and beyond. The civic republicans notice that many of our public officials and leaders of our private institutions fail to exhibit civic virtue, perhaps in part due to a general lack of understanding of the responsibilities the People are held to by the principles of the American Founding. The ACR will therefore promote American civic virtue and its adoption through the hosting of events, creation and dissemination of promotional materials, and the professional and intellectual development of its members for public service. It will also promote the ideas that ignited the American Founding, ideas that extend therefrom, and conservative policy goals.
summary: The American Civic Republicans is an Alabama Law student organization dedicated to American Founding Principles and the widespread inculcation of civic virtue as envisioned by various Founding Fathers, most notably George Washington.

name: Sigma Delta Pi
description:
Sigma Delta Pi is the only honor society dedicated to students of Spanish at four-year colleges and universities, as well as the largest foreign-language honor society in the world. 

UA houses the Beta Alpha chapter, with Dr. Sarah Moody as faculty advisor. Students can apply for membership in SDP after they have completed a course at the “Civilization” level or equivalent (SP 364 and SP 366). Contact Dr. Moody for more information. Membership applications are reviewed in September for fall induction, and in February for spring induction.
summary: This organization is the Beta Alpha Chapter of Sigma Delta Pi, the National Collegiate Hispanic Honor Society. We honor those who excel in the study of the Spanish language and Hispanic literature and culture.

name: Alabama Adapted Athletics
description: Alabama Adapted Athletics sponsors competitive college sports in wheelchair basketball and wheelchair tennis and emerging sports like wheelchair road racing and wheelchair track. Our mission is to provide our athletes the same elite competitive collegiate experience as every other collegiate athlete on campus.
summary: Alabama Adapted Athletics sponsors competitive college sports in wheelchair basketball and wheelchair tennis and emerging sports like wheelchair road racing and wheelchair track.

name: CrossingPoints Program
description: CrossingPoints is a multi-tiered postsecondary transition program in the College of Education at The University of Alabama and serves college-age students with intellectual disabilities. CrossingPoints is funded in part by a 5-year grant by the Office of Postsecondary Programs of the US Department of Education and supported primarily the University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa City Schools, Tuscaloosa County School System, and the Community of Tuscaloosa. CrossingPoints Tier 1 is a collaboration between The University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa City Schools, and Tuscaloosa County School System. Tier 1 serves students with significant disabilities ages 18-21 years who are still receiving IDEA services. Tier 1 follows the Tuscaloosa City Schools and Tuscaloosa County school systems' Academic year calendar. CrossingPoints Tier 2, also known as the Summer Bridge Program, is a postsecondary pipeline program for college-age students with intellectual disabilities ages 18+ years from anywhere in the State of Alabama and beyond. Tier 3 is a three year, non-degree certificate program for students with intellectual disabilities from anywhere in the state of Alabama or beyond. Students enrolled in the Tier 3 program are full fledged UA students, who live in the dorm, enroll in UA classes, and participate in employment internships on and off campus. For more information about each Tier of the CrossingPoints program, please visit http://crossingpoints.ua.edu/.
summary: CrossingPoints is a multi-tiered postsecondary transition program in the College of Education at The University of Alabama and serves college-age students with intellectual disabilities.

name: Universities Against Cancer
summary: Universities Against Cancer is committed to promoting the health and well-being of those affected by cancer by providing financial assistance for treatment and education. The organization aims to bring awareness to the student body of UA.

name: African American Studies Student Association
summary: AASSA is a non-exclusionary organization that will focus on African American Studies majors and minors, by being a source of support for social and academic desire.

name: Electrical & Computer Engineering Graduate Student Association
description:
• To promote social, professional and academic development of UA ECE graduate students.
• To foster communication and interaction among graduate students and between graduate students and the rest of the department.
• To encourage a sense of common identity among graduate students within the department.
• To facilitate outreach by the ECE Graduate Student Body to the UA community at large.
summary: Association for the graduate students in the ECE department

name: EFLS Doctoral Students
summary: The EFLSDS organization will provide members with opportunities facilitating social engagement and interdisciplinary collaboration.

name: Alabama Barbell Club
description: We are a student run club that encourages a healthy lifestyle through lifting. Alabama barbell is a place to make friends, get stronger, and get passionate about lifting. We compete as a team in usapl compitions (powerlifting) but, the club is open to anyone. All types of lifting and any level of experience is welcome. We have team practice once a week and it is a fun and supportive environment to lift in.
summary:
-Weight lifting club
-Powerlifting compitions
-Bodybuilding, olympic lifting, and general lifting

name: The FarmHouse Fraternity at The University of Alabama
description: The object of our fraternity is to promote good fellowship, to encourage studiousness, and to inspire its members in seeking the best in their chosen lines of studies as well in life. Progress shall mark our every step , the spirit of congeniality shall reign at all times; and every member shall be honest with himself as with his brothers. Men elected to our membership are considered to be of good moral character, to be high in scholarship, to have the capacity for meeting and making friends, and to give promise of service to his fellow man and to the world. To be and become such may at times require a sacrifice of time, pleasures and comforts.
summary: The object of our fraternity is to promote good fellowship, to encourage studiousness, and to inspire our members to seek the best in their studies as well as in life. FarmHouse men are considered to be of good moral character and service-oriented.

name: Yarn Nation
description: Yarn Nation hosts social events that provide opportunities for members to learn and practice needle arts. We also complete various community outreach and service opportunities that highlight needle arts.
summary: Yarn Nation provides a safe place for members to learn and practice crochet and other needle arts.

name: The Lifestyle Medicine Community
description:
The Lifestyle Medicine Community is a Lifestyle Medicine Interest group created to educate students at The University of Alabama about evidence-based Lifestyle Medicine, to live more sustainable lives.

According to The American College, Lifestyle Medicine is defined as, Lifestyle medicine is a medical specialty that uses therapeutic lifestyle interventions as a primary modality to treat chronic conditions including, but not limited to, cardiovascular diseases, type 2 diabetes, and obesity.  Lifestyle medicine certified clinicians are trained to apply evidence-based, whole-person, prescriptive lifestyle change to treat and, when used intensively, often reverse such conditions. Applying the six pillars of lifestyle medicine—a whole-food, plant-predominant eating pattern, physical activity, restorative sleep, stress management, avoidance of risky substances and positive social connections—also provides effective prevention for these conditions.
summary: The Lifestyle Medicine Community is a Lifestyle Medicine Interest group created to educate students at The University of Alabama about evidence-based Lifestyle Medicine, to live more sustainable lives.

name: GLOBE UA
description:
GLOBE UA is an organization dedicated to broadening student perspectives and professional connections with a focus on International Business. The purpose of this association is to offer students opportunities to further develop their global business and cross-cultural knowledge. This is accomplished through professional development seminars, speaker series, and other events designed to accomplish the three primary objectives of our program:

  • Exposing students to international industries and business topics;
  • Providing an opportunity for students to meet others interested in International Business;
  • Developing cross-cultural skills.

This group is primarily targeted towards existing business students with a focus on International Business or management, though is open to all interested students.
summary: GLOBE UA is an organization dedicated to broadening student opportunities to further develop their global business and cross-cultural knowledge. Open to all students, we will focus on seminars, networking and speaker series for various topics.

name: First Gen+ Programs
description:
UA First supports first-year freshman students and first-generation students in their academic and social transition into the campus community through academic support programs, leadership development, and community engagement. With a focus on transition and retention through large welcome activities with all classmates to small group programs for building connections, UA First is ready to support students in building their legacy at UA.

Under the Department of UA First, First Gen+ Programs supports students who are the first in their family to attend college or students who may experience barriers such as financial need. The programs focus is on building community, providing opportunities for mentorship, and placing an emphasis on personal and professional development.

At UA, we define a first-generation student as any undergraduate student whose parents have not earned a bachelor's degree. First-Gen+ Programs provides support, resources, and programs for all first-generation students as well as students experiencing barriers related to financial need as they build the foundation of their Capstone experience.
summary: First Gen+ Programs supports students who are the first in their family to attend college or students who may experience barriers such as financial need through community building, mentorship, and personal and professional development.

name: Economics Student Association
description: We are an organization for economics majors, minors, and any student who is otherwise interested in economics. We host professors and other speakers to hear about work in the field of economics, hold a book club, and more. All students are welcome!
summary: The ESA is an organization designed to provide a source of community for students with an interest in economics.

name: Bama Beach Volleyball Club
summary: Bama Beach Volleyball Club is the official beach volleyball club for The University of Alabama. Bama Beach offers the opportunity for members to continue their beach volleyball training through tournaments and pickup.

name: Alabama Student Mobilization
summary: We exist to help college students grow in their leadership, character, and faith.

name: The Crimson K-9s
description:
Our club exists to bring together UA students who share a love for dogs, building a welcoming and supportive community. We strive to equip members with valuable knowledge about responsible dog care while hosting fun, inclusive events that celebrate the bond between people and their pets. By doing so, we not only enrich the lives of our dogs but also create lasting friendships among fellow dog enthusiasts.

 

STEPS TO JOIN OUR CLUB

It is free to join The Crimson K9 club, just complete these 4 easy steps to become a member!

1. Join our GroupMe! 2025-2026 GroupMe Link

2. Follow our Instagram! The Crimson K-9's on Instagram

3. Complete our Join Form Sign Up/Join Form

4. Complete Shots Form (Only for those bringing your dog to our events) Dog Vaccination Proof
summary: We are committed to fostering an inclusive community of dog owners! Join us for bi-weekly meetings where we come together to build a strong sense of community!

name: Bama Theme Park Engineering & Design
description:
Bama TPED is dedicated to providing University of Alabama students, regardless of their background or major, with project-based opportunities that strengthen both creative and technical skills across all disciplines.
 

Our mission is to transform the themed entertainment industry by creating more ethical, story-driven experiences for audiences everywhere. Through hands-on design projects, professional development, and networking events, we empower students to make a lasting impact.

 

We welcome students from all majors, recognizing that each individual brings unique gifts to the team.

 

Innovate. Design. Thrill. Roll Tide!
summary: We specialize in project-based experiences and education focusing on designing immersive elements for theme parks and all types of themed entertainment experiences.

name: UA Common Ground
summary: Common Ground is a bipartisan political discourse organization with the goal of promoting educated and respectful conversation. We encourage our members to challenge their own beliefs, play devil’s advocate, and learn from those they disagree with.

name: Education Abroad
summary: The Education Abroad office assists students in pursuing educational opportunities all over the globe. Come ask us how you can study, intern, research, teach, or volunteer abroad. It's time to discover the world!

name: BridgeUSA at UA
description: BridgeUSA at UA is a multi-partisan political organization that aims to decrease polarization on campus by bringing together students from various political backgrounds and creating a safe space for them all to share their personal and political views through fun and engaging discussions and activities!
summary: BridgeUSA, a national non-profit, is the largest and fastest-growing student movement improving our political discourse with over 50 college chapters and 20 high school chapters working to end polarization in politics.

name: Alabama Women's Hockey Club
description: The Alabama Women's Hockey Club was founded with the goal of growing the game of ice hockey in the south. The Alabama Women's Hockey club strives to create an empowering and uplifting environment that allows all women at the University of Alabama to have the opportunity to learn and grow in the sport of ice hockey. We aim to work with other women's and girl's hockey clubs around the Southeast to grow the game.
summary: The Alabama Women's Hockey Club was founded with the goal of growing the game of ice hockey in the south. The Alabama Women's Hockey club strives to create an empowering and uplifting environment for all women at the University of Alabama.

name: Capstone Association of Black Psychology Students
description:
The Capstone Association of Black Psychology Students is a student led organization used to establish a community for African American students in the Psychology Department at the University of Alabama while providing students an opportunity to network with professionals within the Psychology field, develop professional skills and help further raise awareness about mental health. Our goals include helping our members obtain vital knowledge that will support their professional career path and further help with decolonizing and diversifying the field of psychology.

To become a member, follow us on Instagram @cabps_ for events and attend general body meetings where you can get more information on how to pay dues. For additional help and questions, please contact us through our email at cabps2023@gmail.com or directly contact our president, Jasmine Wooden, at jjwooden@crimson.ua.edu.
summary: This organization will impact the University of Alabama by providing students an opportunity to network with professionals within the Psychology field, develop professional skills and help further raise awareness about mental health.

name: Black In Medicine
summary: BIM is a hands-on organization. We want our members to gain valuable experience that will have a long-term impact on their careers. Our goal is to ensure that you have experience, knowledge, and an in-depth understanding of the healthcare field.

name: Grow The Game
description: Grow the Game is dedicated to advancing ice sports in Tuscaloosa and the Southeastern Conference. The organization's strategic objectives include supporting the growth of women's teams, assisting existing teams, fostering awareness of ice sports in the South, providing educational and employment opportunities, and advocating for crucial resources through targeted fundraising efforts. Committed to community engagement, equality, and sustainable development, Grow the Game operates with a professional focus on creating a thriving environment for ice sports enthusiasts in the region.
summary: Our organizations mission is to grow the game of hockey in Tuscaloosa and introduce the sport to surrounding communities including schools, youth groups, clubs, and many more.

name: Ghana Diaspora Network
description:
The Ghana Diaspora Network at the University of Alabama is dedicated to enriching the cultural tapestry within the university community while forging connections between Ghanaian students and African Americans with roots in Ghana. Our primary objectives encompass several key areas. Firstly, we aim to celebrate and promote Ghanaian culture and heritage within our academic environment, fostering a vibrant cultural exchange. Secondly, we are committed to providing vital support and mentorship to Ghanaian students, empowering them to excel both academically and personally. Thirdly, we strive to cultivate a profound sense of belonging and unity among individuals with Ghanaian heritage, nurturing an inclusive atmosphere within our university. Lastly, we actively engage in educational initiatives, philanthropic endeavors, health and well-being, advocacy, and collaborative efforts through partnerships such as the Tuscaloosa Sister City program, all aimed at strengthening connections within the Ghanaian diaspora and building bridges with the wider Alabama community. 

 

Mission 

Through our steadfast commitment to these principles, we aspire to empower our members to achieve academic excellence, thrive socially, and make substantial contributions to both our university and the broader global community. Our association serves as a symbol of unity, a source of cultural pride, and a bridge for cross-cultural understanding. We are unwavering in our dedication to upholding the values that encapsulate the spirit of Ghana, while also actively enriching and positively impacting the larger university community.
summary: The Ghana Diaspora Network at the University of Alabama is dedicated to enriching the cultural tapestry within the university community while forging connections between Ghanaian students and African Americans with roots in Ghana.

name: Campus Cupboard
description: At Campus Cupboard, our mission is to nourish the minds and bodies of the University of Alabama community by providing access to wholesome and nutritious food. Committed to fostering a supportive campus community, we strive to alleviate food insecurity among students, ensuring that no member of our university family goes hungry. Through collaboration, compassion, and a dedication to student, faculty, and staff well-being, Campus Cupboard aims to create a hunger-free campus environment, empowering students to focus on their education and achieve their full potential. For more information about how to donate, volunteer, or seek services, visit our website: https://www.campuscupboard.com/
summary: At Campus Cupboard, we aim to create a campus where everyone can focus on learning and reaching their goals without worrying about food insecurity.

name: Radical Athlete aNd Student Oasis Ministry
summary: RANSOM is a diverse, student-led and family-like group of athletes and students spreading the message of Christ throughout the campus and community.

name: Society of Experimental and Computational Mechanics
summary: The purpose of this organization is to give graduate students in the field of Engineering who are involved in experimental and computational research a platform to present their work in front of their peers,

name: Capstone Scholars in Engineering
description: promote real-world opportunities for engineers through professional presentations, networking events, and volunteer work. CSE simultaneously aims to foster the growth of our students through hands-on and collaborative learning, exposing students to experiences that help develop personal and professional skills.
summary: Capstone Scholars in Engineering aims to promote real-world opportunities for engineering majors through professional presentations, networking events, and volunteer work.

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description: Student Affiliates in School Psychology (SASP) aims to support students' professional growth as school psychologists in training. SASP seeks to cultivate community within the school psychology graduate program through social events as well as to advance the scholarship of its members through professional development workshops.
summary: Student Affiliates in School Psychology aims to support graduate students in the school psychology program with activities that promote professional growth and cultivate community among SASP members.

name: Crimson Records
description: The student organization will act as a mock record label. It will provide a place for collaboration among artists, producers, audio engineers, and videographers, while marketing their content. The future goals would be to put on performances at university events and help develop the members into better artists, producers, etc.
summary: Mock record label, facilitating collaboration, performances, and marketing between aspiring musical artists.

name: Slavic Language Arts and Culture Club
summary: SLACC is committed to raising awareness of Slavic, Baltic, and central Asian cultures on campus.

name: Society for Collegiate Leadership and Achivement
description: The Society for Collegiate Leadership & Achievement (SCLA) is a multi-disciplinary honor society with 90,000+ members at 600+ colleges nationwide. We aim to maximize student potential through our powerful skill development platform including training content on hundreds of skills critical to a successful career, a vibrant mentor and peer community, hundreds of career insider interviews, and direct personal access to hiring managers and internship opportunities.
summary: Membership in SCLA is a distinction for undergraduate and graduate students whose academic success demonstrates their commitment and drive. SCLA provides members with powerful preparation resources to launch successful careers.

name: Exploring the Worlds of Fiction Through Literature
description: Explore Book Club will read books of different genres with each book. The organization will have two meetings per month and a group volunteer opportunity once a month. Members will be able to suggest book options and the members will vote on which one we will read for that genre. The organization will prioritize diverse and insightful conversation with each novel. Also, members are not required to have read to attend meetings! The organization will also promote community service on an individual level and on an organizational level.
summary: Explore Book Club is an organization that aims to foster diverse conversation around novels, to provide a sense of community and engagement, and to participate in community service.

name: The Wellness Society
description:
The Wellness Society at The University of Alabama is part of a national student-led organization with chapters at over 80 colleges and cities nationwide. Our mission is to empower students to find balance in their mind, body, and soul through inclusive, accessible wellness programming.

 

At UA, we create a welcoming and uplifting space where students feel supported in prioritizing their well-being while building genuine community. Our programming includes movement classes, mindfulness experiences, educational talks, service opportunities, and social events designed to support students at every stage of their wellness journey.

 

Whether students are looking to move their bodies, slow down, learn something new, or meet like-minded peers, our programming is designed to meet them where they are.

 

Memberships can be purchased through our Linktree or by visiting our Instagram.
summary: The Wellness Society at Alabama promotes balance of mind, body, and soul through inclusive events, meaningful connections, and accessible wellness resources for all students looking to prioritize their well-being and find community.

name: The Network of Older Undergrads
description: According to UA's Fall 2023 Enrollment at a Glance document, only 2011 of the university's 33,000+ students are older than 25. Life changes a lot from age 18 to 25, and older students might struggle to connect with their younger classmates. Without community, it's hard to be successful in college. The Network of Older Undergrads seeks to provide a space where older undergraduates can meet other students their age, build community, and find support for problems unique to being an older undergrad. Our membership eligibility is simple: Do you identify as an older undergrad? If so, you are welcome in our club.
summary: We are a collective of older undergraduates who seek to provide community and resources to each other.

name: Chemical and Biological Engineering Graduate Student Council
description:
The Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering Graduate Student Council was initiated in Spring 2019, which corresponds to its first meeting.  The original formation of the council was motivated by an unmet need within the department to facilitate communication among the graduate students, provide opportunities for students to develop non-technical skills for future career success, such as personal, presentation, communication and leadership skills, foster a stimulating and supportive academic community, and continuously improve the operation, educational mission, and overall quality of the department.

The GSC shall act as a voice for the graduate student body in meetings and other communications with the faculty and staff of the ChBE department, and the university graduate student association.  Further, the GSC shall reflect the interests of the ChBE student body consistent with the purpose and stated scope of the GSC.  Likewise, the GSC shall act as a voice to the ChBE body, in order to broaden student awareness of various opportunities, including educational, social, and cultural experiences not otherwise provided in the curriculum.  On occasion, the GSC or its delegate may represent the ChBE department to other constituencies, such as the College of Engineering or other campus organizations and administrative units.
summary: ChBE-GSC serves to represent all graduate students of ChBE to promote social, professional and academic development.

name: Pickleball Club at UA
description: The UA Pickleball Club is a student-run organization that welcomes all students to participate in practices, tournaments, and social events. Additionally, the UAPC offers the most experienced players the chance to play against other Universities and intense competitive play through our UA Pickleball Team.
summary: The Pickleball Club at The University of Alabama welcomes players of all levels to join us for practices, tournaments, and social events. Additionally, It invites those interested in competitive play to try out for the UA Pickleball Team.

name: Flag Football Club
summary: An organization dedicated to providing a resource for people interested in flag football to get together and play.

name: Red Rook Press
description:
Red Rook Press is a blossoming publishing house for early-career novelists and poets. Our goal is to foster and premiere the creative minds of undergraduate and graduate students still establishing themselves in the writing world by giving them the opportunity to bring their voice to life. We crave any form and genre, from high fantasy to literary fiction, from sonnets to freeform. And you don’t have to be an English student either– sometimes the best voices are those we don’t expect. We’d love to publish writers and poets from any academic background. 

 

With our staff made up of undergraduates dedicated to every process of publication and a stellar collection of professional advisors, we hope to bring two new books of prose and poetry into the world every academic year. Hit the bookshelves next April to find the newest books from Red Rook Press!
summary: Red Rook Press is a student-run publishing house operating out of UA that publishes novel-length work from undergraduate and graduate students across the country. 50+ members work all year to help publish 2-3 books at the end of every spring semester.

name: Fold Christian Bible Study
description:
The Fold is an organization that holds a weekly on-campus Bible study on Thursday evenings.

 

Our goal is to further the knowledge of the truth revealed in the Bible and to learn how to better live as Christians in the world. We use scripture alone to shape our understanding of our relationship with God and Jesus, his Son. With the Bible as our guide, we hope to build each other up and bring others to a relationship with God. 

 

Each semester has an overarching theme around which each study is based. There are no requirements/prerequisites to join our organization or attend a study, as they are open to all students. The weekly studies will be guided by students which volunteer and are selected at the officers' discretion. Membership is obtained by simply expressing interest and joining the GroupMe.

 

Many of our students attend Northwood Church of Christ. More information about this church can be found here: https://northwoodchurchofchrist.org. Interested students may also reach out to Drew Miller (dmiller29@crimson.ua.edu) for more information about the organization.
summary: The Fold is an organization that holds a weekly on-campus Bible study on Thursday evenings. We use the Bible alone as our source material for information about God, Jesus, and our relationship with them. All are free to come study with us!

name: La Escuelita de Inglés
description: La Escuelita de Inglés is a volunteer organization which provides free English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction to Spanish-speaking adults in the Tuscaloosa Area. For information about volunteering, please reach out to our organization's email.
summary: We are a volunteer group which provides free English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction to Spanish-speaking adults in the Tuscaloosa Area.

name: The University of Alabama Museums
description: UA Museums as we currently know it — a collection of multiple museums under a single administrative umbrella — stems from a reorganization in 1990. Prior to that all the museums were under the Alabama Museum of Natural History at UA. Until 1961 the museum(s) were part of the Geological Survey of Alabama, reflecting the museums’ roots in the work of pioneering geologist E.A. Smith.
summary: A combined account for all of The University of Alabama's museums.

name: Crimson Wishes
summary: Elementary students write letters to Santa, which are received by university student/organization donors who write personalized letters back and supply the child with their gift(s) at an assembly.

name: Wish Makers at Bama
description:
Wish Makers at Bama is entering its third year as an organization on campus. We are a direct branch of Make-A-Wish Alabama. Our goal is to raise $10,000 to grant the wish of a critically ill child each academic year. We aspire to bring UA students, faculty, staff, and the Tuscaloosa community together to make this possible!

We host an array of events from $5 to $30 fundraisers, percentage nights at local businesses, and free events geared towards our community. This organization has a diverse group of members. We have students who are Wish Kids, non-Wish Kids, many different majors, many different states, and a few different countries as well! Wish Makers at Bama welcomes everyone to join our mission. We strive for our members to meet new people and make friends along the way. 

If you would like to join our organization or have any questions, please contact us at wishmakersatbama@gmail.com with your interests.
summary: Wish Makers at Bama has been on campus since Spring 2024. We are a direct branch of Make-A-Wish AL. Our goal is to raise $10,000 to grant the wish of a critically ill child in our area. Join us in our mission, to change kids lives and maybe your own too!

name: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine
summary:
The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to
