increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already e

name: REACH for Success
summary: The purpose of RFS is to foster a community atmosphere and create a sense of belonging for students who have experiences in foster care, legal orphanhood, and kinship/guardianship care.

name: American Library Association at UA
summary: The American Library Association at UA works to promote scholarship and professional ethics, provide professional contacts within the field, and increase awareness in the American Library Association.

name: National Kitchen and Bath Association Student Chapter
description: The intent of creating the National Kitchen and Bath (NKBA) Student Chapter at The University of Alabama’s campus is to better connect interior design students with the resources and opportunities that the NKBA so generously offers. In creating this chapter, it provides an active student community that will be involved with the local NKBA chapter, where students can build connections and further nurture their design visions that can add to their future in design. As a student community, we look to enhance and develop our knowledge in kitchen and bath design together as an organization. You can become a member of the National Kitchen & Bath Association Student Chapter for free because UA is an affiliated program.
summary: Connected with the National Kitchen and Bath Association, this organization looks to deepen interior students connections in the design world and to help them further explore the specialty of kitchen and bath design.

name: Cave Appreciation Club
description:
The purpose of this club is to promote an interest in caving and to support safe caving practices.

In pursuit of this general purpose, the Cave Appreciation Club will work to:

A) Host social events that provide opportunities to build comradery amongst students.

B) Provide opportunities to engage with cave environments to foster stewardship and conservation.

C) Promote the value of cave environments to the University of Alabama and Tuscaloosa communities through service and outreach.

D) Partner with other student and caving organizations to further the goals above.
summary: The purpose of this club is to promote an interest in caving and to support safe caving practices.

name: Remote Area Medical at UA
description: Remote Area Medical at UA will help the national RAM organization expand its outreach to further it's ability to provide free, quality healthcare to underserved communities. RAM USA is a major non-profit provider of free pop-up clinics delivering medical, dental, and vision care. Since it was founded in 1985, 196,000 volunteers have treated nearly 1 million individuals. Our chapter goal is to help RAM deliver their services to underserved Alabama communities and at the same time, help UA students discover their passion and commitment in service to others.
summary: Remote Area Medical at UA will help the national RAM USA organization expand it's outreach to further its ability to provide free, quality healthcare to underserved communities.

name: Whitfield Patient Experience Program
summary: The Whitfield Patient Experience Program aims to provides students both with exposure to the challenges of rural healthcare and a setting to share their experiences while learning about the Demopolis community.

name: In Her Bag Student Organization
summary: Entrepreneurial organization that provides students with the opportunity to learn more about entrepreneurship in the cosmetology industry concerning skills and general business aspects.

name: Student Basic Needs Coalition
description: The Student Basic Needs Coalition is committed to ensuring students' basic needs access and fighting the rising cost of college. We work to promote policies on campus that support student access to the four pillars of basic needs: Food, Housing, Safety, and Healthcare. By ensuring access to those basic needs and lowering the cost of higher education, receiving a degree will be more accessible to all Americans.
summary: Student Basic Needs Coalition is committed to ensuring students' basic needs access & advocating for policies supporting them, by ensuring access to those basic needs & and enhancing degree accessibility for all UA students.

name: Serving Bama
description:
As a student-led program, Serving Bama connects students to weekly service opportunities that appeal to a wide range of students and meet a variety of needs in the Tuscaloosa community.

The Serving Bama team organizes around 10 service opportunities every week with local non-profits that address causes such as outdoor and environmental efforts, housing security, community development, and animal welfare. More information about the ways students can get involved with Serving Bama can be found on BamaPulse.
summary: As a student-led program, Serving Bama connects students to weekly service opportunities that appeal to a wide range of students and meet a variety of needs in the Tuscaloosa community.

name: Association for Information Systems
summary: AIS exists to promote the opportunities and knowledge of career opinions within the IT field through professional development, social networking, and community development.

name: Big Days of Service
description: Throughout the year, the Center for Service and Leadership hosts Big Days of Service for the campus community. These service days offer students, faculty, and staff the chance to serve all across town.
summary: Throughout the year, the Center for Service and Leadership hosts Big Days of Service for the campus community. These service days offer students, faculty, and staff the chance to serve all across town.

name: Alabama Leadership Association
description: At the Alabama Leadership Association, we are dedicated to cultivating the leaders of tomorrow through a commitment to leadership development and impactful community service. Our mission is to empower students to excel in both their academic and professional endeavors while instilling a profound sense of responsibility towards serving and uplifting the communities in which we live and work.
summary: At the Alabama Leadership Association, we are dedicated to cultivating the leaders of tomorrow through a commitment to leadership development and impactful community service.

name: Alabama Men's Club Basketball
description:
The Alabama Men’s Club Basketball team offers passionate and skilled players the opportunity to continue playing competitive basketball at a high level while representing The University of Alabama. Open to students of all majors, the club fosters a sense of camaraderie, teamwork, and athletic excellence. Members engage in regular practices, compete in regional and national tournaments, and work together to improve their skills both on and off the court.

As a student-led club sport, Alabama Men’s Club Basketball provides participants with the chance to develop leadership skills in roles such as team captains, event organizers, and administrative positions within the club. The team competes against other universities and colleges, offering a platform to showcase talent and pursue athletic goals beyond intramurals.

In addition to competition, the club emphasizes personal development, sportsmanship, and academic success. We strive to maintain a balance between athletics and academics, ensuring that all members can excel both on the court and in their studies. Joining the Alabama Men’s Club Basketball team means becoming part of a tight-knit community where lifelong friendships and valuable networks are formed.
summary: The Alabama Men’s Club Basketball team, in its first year, aims to bring competitive basketball to campus. We strive to offer students a platform to hone their skills, compete at high levels, and foster a strong basketball community.

name: Building Our Successful Sisters
summary: Building Our Successful Sisters is a professional development organization dedicated to the advancement of minority collegiate women in professional spaces while also emphasizing the importance of networking, community service, and academic success.

name: Poker Society at Alabama
description: Whether it’s breaking down what happened at the most recent high-stakes poker tournament on TV or just starting to learn about how to play, the Poker Society at Alabama aims to bring together players at every level to grow conceptual experience and learn the nuances of the classic card game. We want to provide a friendly, zero-stakes environment in which one can simply learn about the game with no risk, but also educate people on the warning signs of gambling addiction and provide resources to those in need.
summary: Educational poker club at The University of Alabama.

name: Girls Staters at UA
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote the American Legion Auxiliary’s Girls State conferences and provide a forum for alumnae and friends of Girls State programs to continue to develop friendships, leadership skills, and civic responsibility.

name: Pack-A-Sack
summary: Pack A Sack's purpose is to increase outreach within the local community, serve as a catalyst for help and hope to individuals and groups impacted by homelessness, and raise awareness of the hunger crisis in America.

name: Crimson Cheer Club
summary: Welcome to the Crimson Cheer Club! Learn how to stunt, tumble, and perform in this super fun club! Meet lifelong friends and learn new skills!

name: Broadcast Education Association at UA
description:
The Broadcast Education Association at The University of Alabama aims to educate and inform students of topics relating to media production. Through hands-on workshops, panels, and critique sessions, students will improve their media production skills. Its association with the national Broadcast Education Association organization affords its members scholarship, networking, and competition opportunities. 

 

Please contact us through Instagram if interested in attending a meeting!
summary: The Broadcast Education Association at The University of Alabama aims to educate and inform students of topics relating to media production. Through hands-on workshops, panels, and critique sessions, students will improve their media production skills.

name: Roll Tide® For Reading
description:
Roll Tide® For Reading is an organization whose mission is to champion childhood literacy and foster a love for reading in young minds. As a member, you'll engage directly with children, caregivers, and educators in a variety of settings, both educational and non-educational.

Through interactive events, workshops, and volunteer opportunities, you’ll help create enriching experiences that inspire a passion for reading. Whether you’re sharing stories, organizing literacy initiatives, or supporting local schools, your involvement makes a meaningful difference in the lives of children and their families.

Together, we can build a brighter future by promoting literacy and lifelong learning—one story at a time. Join us in making an impact today! Roll Tide!
summary: The mission of Roll Tide® For Reading is to promote childhood literacy and give group members direct experience with children, caregivers, and educators in both educational and non-educational settings.

name: Alabama Crimson Cubing
summary: A club for Rubik's cube enthusiasts to compete and learn about new methods and puzzles.

name: Latinx Law Student Association
description: LLSA focuses on addressing legal issues impacting the Latin American community, advocating for diversity and inclusion, and creating opportunities for its members through social events and collaborations with other organizations. Additionally, the association is affiliated with the National Latina/o Law Student Association (NLLSA), ensuring a broader network and support system for its members​.
summary: The Latinx Law Students Association ("LLSA") at the University of Alabama School of Law is dedicated to advancing the voices of Latin American law students, promoting academic success, and fostering a commitment to our community.

name: Latter-Day Saints Student Association
description: The Latter-Day Saints Student Association's purpose is to encourage and support members in achieving their academic goals and maintaining a balanced life while on campus, to encourage them to become an influence for good by participating in activities and service on campus and in the community, and to provide students with meaningful service opportunities and social and religious activities consistent with LDSSA standards, to promote traditional moral values.
summary: The Institute of Religion established the Latter-Day Saint Student Association (LDSSA) to help members have a balanced secular and spiritual educational experience during their years of formal education.

name: Crimson Combat Robotics
description: CCR is a student organization that focuses on the design, fabrication, and competition of a 30lb combat robot. This organization will work to advance the skills and knowledge of its members in their respective fields while offering a chance to compete at a high level. The organization will also seek to mentor local high school teams through outreach programs, as well as host smaller events for students.
summary: CCR, Crimson Combat Robotics, Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, mechanical, electrical engineering, computer science, competition, team

name: The Hands of McCollough
summary: We are a service-oriented organization affiliated with the McCollough Institute for Pre-Medical Scholars that focuses on improving the well-being of our community by providing medical and non-medical volunteering opportunities.

name: Friends of the Union
description: Friends of The Union will serve as an on-campus alliance between the United Campus Workers and UA students to advocate and engage with UCW's actions on campus. This is meant to build a coalition of support and advocacy on-campus.
summary: Friends of The Union will serve as an on-campus alliance between the United Campus Workers and UA students to advocate and engage with UCW's actions on campus.

name: Undergraduate African Student Organization
description: The Undergraduate African Student Organization at the University of Alabama is dedicated to serving undergraduate students, including first-generation and continuing students, as well as the African diaspora. The organization aims to foster a sense of community by emphasizing the intersection between African Americans, Africans, and the diaspora. It seeks to create a welcoming space that highlights shared experiences and cultural ties, differentiating itself by focusing on the needs and connections of undergraduate students at a primarily white institution.
summary: The Undergraduate African Student Organization at the University of Alabama fosters community among undergraduates, first-generation, and African diaspora students, emphasizing and teaching shared cultural ties between all cultures.

name: Association for Computing Machinery - Women
description: ACM-W is an organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for women pursuing studies and careers in computer science and related fields. We provide opportunities for women to connect, collaborate, and support each other in their endeavors. Anyone who supports this mission is welcome!
summary: ACM-W is an organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for women pursuing studies and careers in computer science and related fields. We provide opportunities for women to connect, collaborate, and support each other in their endeavors.

name: Zeta Psi
summary: The Rho Tau Chapter of Zeta Psi International Fraternity

name: College of Arts and Sciences Dean's Office
summary: Events, workshops, and conferences for students hosted by The College of Arts and Sciences.

name: Engineering Leadership Development Society
description: The proposed organization, Engineering Leadership Development Society (ELDS), aims to empower engineering students at the University of Alabama to become effective leaders within College of Engineering (COE) organizations. This organization will build upon the structure of ME 380 (Engineering Leadership I) and ME 480 (Engineering Leadership II) courses, offering a comprehensive platform for students to gain practical leadership experience through structured discussions, workshops, and project analysis. Purpose The primary purpose of ELDS is to provide engineering students with the opportunity to develop and refine leadership skills necessary for managing COE organizations effectively. These skills include recruiting, motivating team members, managing resources and time, sponsor outreach, fundraising, scheduling, presentations, and budgeting. By participating in ELDS, students will gain hands-on experience in these areas, enhancing their ability to lead design teams, clubs, and professional organizations within the College of Engineering. Goals - Leadership Development: To foster the leadership potential of engineering students through structured discussions, workshops, and project analysis. - Workshop Facilitation: To organize and execute three workshops per semester, providing attendees with practical skills and strategies for leading engineering teams. - Community Building: To create a network of engineering leaders who collaborate and support one another in their leadership journeys. - Sponsorship and Outreach: To build and maintain relationships with sponsors and partners to support the growth and activities of the organization. - Sustainable Growth: To establish a long-term framework for the organization that aligns with university requirements and ensures continuous improvement and expansion. Membership Eligibility Membership in ELDS is open to all engineering students enrolled at the University of Alabama, with a preference for those actively participating in COE organizations or design teams. Enrollment in ME 380 or ME 480 is encouraged but not required, as the organization aims to be inclusive and provide leadership development opportunities to all interested students. Members will have the opportunity to participate in workshops, lead discussion sessions, and contribute to the planning and execution of events. By formalizing the organization under the university’s structure, ELDS will have access to university resources, facilities, and funding opportunities, enabling it to operate effectively and expand its reach across the College of Engineering.
summary: The Engineering Leadership Development Society (ELDS) empowers UA engineering students with leadership skills to manage College of Engineering organizations.

name: Det 010 Cadre
description: Our program affords students a sense of community and belonging that is forged through shared experiences with military training. Detachment 010 has a rich history of developing top-notch leaders for the Department of the Air Force. With 80 cadets, we provide a challenging experience designed to mold you into a leader of tomorrow, while also ensuring a personalized experience for each cadet. Our Cadet Wing is run by cadets with guidance and oversight from the Cadre (active duty personnel assigned to the detachment). The active duty personnel teach the cadets about the Air Force, help to develop leadership skills and supervise the cadet training environment during all training events (physical training, required class time, leadership training at Leadership Laboratory and so on).
summary: Air Force ROTC presents many opportunities for students. It is a great way for college students to pursue higher education with a degree of their choosing at a civilian institution while developing leadership skills as they prepare to serve their country

name: The Undergraduate Student Council of Chemical and Biological Engineering
description: The Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering Undergraduate Student Council was initiated on September 24, 2021, which corresponds to its first meeting.  The original formation of the council was motivated by an unmet need within the department to facilitate communication between the students and the faculty/staff in order to continuously improve the operation, educational mission, and overall quality of the department. The USC is a voice for the student body in meetings and other communications with the faculty and staff of the ChBE department and will reflect the interests of the ChBE student body consistent with the purpose and stated scope of the USC.  Likewise, the USC shall act as a voice to the ChBE body in order to broaden student awareness of various opportunities, including educational, social, and cultural experiences not otherwise provided in the curriculum.  On occasion, delegates from the USC may represent the ChBE department to other constituencies, such as the College of Engineering or other campus organizations and administrative units.
summary: The USC is a voice for the student body in meetings and other communications with the faculty and staff of the ChBE department and will reflect the interests of the ChBE student body consistent with the purpose and stated scope of the USC.

name: The Psychology Research Think Tank
description: An undergraduate focused research club working with Dr. Darren George, with the goal to brainstorm, conduct and publish the highest quality psychology research with student authors and co-authors, as well as to connect students together through research. Each semester we run 2-3 different studies while constantly discussing new ideas throughout the entire year!
summary: An undergraduate focused research club working with Dr. Darren George, with the goal to brainstorm, conduct and publish the highest quality psychology research with student authors and co-authors, as well as to connect students together through research.

name: Minervites
summary: An organization dedicated to supporting students in Minerva, the Creative Advertising Program within the College of Communication and Information Sciences at The University of Alabama.

name: Pre-Health Engineering Network
description: The purpose of the Pre-Health Engineering Network (PEN) is to support primarily engineering students who are pursuing pre-med or pre-health pathways by providing resources, guidance, and community. The club aims to prepare students to successfully apply to pre-health schools by offering opportunities for professional development, mentorship, and networking. PEN helps members navigate all aspects of pre-health applications, including securing shadowing opportunities, gaining clinical experience, volunteering, and preparing for exams like the MCAT or DAT. It strives to foster collaboration, academic growth, and a deeper understanding of how engineering principles can contribute to the medical field. Through workshops, guest speakers, and hands-on experiences, the club equips members with the tools and knowledge to excel in medical, dental, or other healthcare-related graduate programs.
summary: The Pre-Health Engineering Network (PEN) is a community for engineering students pursuing pre-health pathways. We provide resources, mentorship, and opportunities to prepare students to successfully apply to pre-health programs.

name: ASCE - Steel Bridge Team
description: ASCE Steel Bridge is a student competition comprised of primarily students pursuing a degree in Civil Engineering, however, any and all students interested are welcome, regardless of major. Our primary objective is to design and construct a steel bridge to compete in the ASCE Gulf Coast Student Symposium. This process incorporates several aspects of the typical process in an engineer's career such as product design, testing, production, communication, and teamwork.
summary: ASCE Steel Bridge is a student competition with the objective of designing and constructing a steel bridge in order to compete in the ASCE Gulf Coast Student Symposium.

name: Biotech and Bioethics Association
description:
The Biotech and Bioethics Association (BaBA) aims to provide students with a platform to discuss topics in biotechnology and their bioethical implications, as well as inform members of opportunities for hands-on involvement in biotech and bioethics.

We host regular social events and meetings where students can openly explore new developments in biotechnology and talk through the ethical questions that come with them. Whether it’s a new gene editing tool or an emerging health technology, our goal is to create space for thoughtful conversations and collaboration across disciplines.

BaBA also aims to inform students about various opportunities in research, shadowing, internships, and other ways to get directly involved in the fields of biotech and bioethics. At the same time, we encourage the academic study of these topics - sharing relevant articles, organizing discussions, and supporting students interested in diving deeper into the science and ethics behind innovation.

Ultimately, we’re here to build a community of students who care about where biotechnology is going - and what it means for the people it affects.
summary: The Biotech and Bioethics Association (BaBA) aims to provide students with a platform to discuss topics in biotechnology and their bioethical implications, as well as inform members of opportunities for hands-on involvement in biotech and bioethics.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha Political Science Honor Society
description: The purpose of the organization is to recognize excellence in academic achievement in the fields of political science, to stimulate scholarship and interest and promote worthwhile curricular and extracurricular activities related to political science.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to recognize excellence in academic achievement in the fields of political science, to stimulate scholarship and interest and promote worthwhile curricular and extracurricular activities related to political science.

name: Capstone Asset Management Society
summary: The Capstone Asset Management Society (CAMS) is a student-led organization dedicated to helping members grow their knowledge of investing and prepare for successful careers in finance.

name: Mergers and Acquisitions Group
description:
The University of Alabama Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A) Group offers an immersive, hands-on introduction to the world of mergers and acquisitions. Through semester-long deal simulations, industry guest speakers, and a structured curriculum, the group equips students with the skills and knowledge necessary to excel in finance careers. We focus on developing students' technical abilities, networking skills, and practical experience to prepare them for success in competitive finance roles.

The group hosts interactive sessions, networking events, and trips with industry professionals to foster connections and enhance learning. We also emphasize active participation through hands-on simulations and a comprehensive new member education program, ensuring that students gain meaningful, real-world experience. By providing high-quality internal resources, mentorship, and opportunities for personal development, we aim to prepare members for success in corporate finance, valuation, and M&A, setting them up for future success in the finance industry.
summary: The UA Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A) Group provides a hands-on introduction to M&A through semester-long deal simulations, industry guest speakers, and a structured curriculum designed to prepare students for careers in finance.

name: Army ROTC
description: Army ROTC offers elective Military Science courses alongside your core curriculum, providing leadership training for success in any career. Enroll as a freshman or sophomore without obligation and learn essential leadership skills to succeed as an Army Officer. Available at over 1,100 colleges, ROTC offers merit-based scholarships covering up to full tuition. Whether you're in high school, college or already in the Army, ROTC can help you become an officer and turn your passion into a career.
summary: Army ROTC offers elective Military Science courses alongside your core curriculum, providing leadership training for success in any career. Whether you're in high school, college or already in the Army, ROTC can help you become an officer.

name: Graduate Association of Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering
summary: GRACCEE is a graduate student organization which aims to connect students within the Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering Department. Various professional development opportunities as well as social gatherings will be held.

name: Thomistic Institute
description: The Thomistic Institute exists to promote Catholic truth in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of Christians at universities, in the Church, and in the wider public square. The thought of St. Thomas Aquinas, the Universal Doctor of the Church, is our touchstone.
summary: The Thomistic Institute hosts guest lecturers and book clubs on campus that cover topics such as Christianity, Catholic thought, religion, philosophy, Thomism, and more.

name: Portuguese Club
description: The Portuguese Club at UA aims to celebrate and promote Portuguese-speaking cultures, languages, and traditions. Our mission is to engage students by improving language proficiency and cultural understanding and provide a welcoming community for all interested members.
summary: The Portuguese Club helps language learning and culture through events, conversation tables, and community-building activities centered on Lusophone cultures.

name: Prepared At All Times
description: Our club is dedicated to improving health literacy and emergency preparedness by empowering the community through education and resource connection. We host workshops, training sessions, and outreach events that equip individuals with the knowledge and skills needed to respond confidently to health emergencies. By partnering with local healthcare providers, emergency services, and advocacy groups, we aim to bridge gaps in access to vital resources and foster a more informed, resilient community.
summary: Our club is dedicated to improving health literacy and emergency preparedness by empowering the community through education and resource connection

name: College of Education FIRE: First-Generation Initiatives for Research and Education
description:
In order to achieve its purpose, College of Education FIRE will:

Host resource centered events annually to help empower first-generation graduate students in navigating graduate school;

Help first-generation graduate students thrive in their programs by facilitating annual professional and/or personal events; distribute an annual survey to understand the needs of first-generation graduate students within the COE; and communicate the survey feedback to designated COE administration to inform college and departmental programming, practices, and understandings of first-generation graduate students;

Create opportunities for first-generation graduate students to build community across peers by hosting annual events that allow students to meet, engage, and collaborate with other first-generation graduate students across COE departments and fields; and

Continuously work to increase organization membership and engagement of first-generation graduate students through email announcements and designated social media platforms to communicate events/activities and request opportunities to present at new student orientations.
summary: The purpose of College of Education FIRE is to empower first-generation graduate students to navigate graduate school, thrive in their program, and build community across peers.

name: The Alpha Chapter of The Nikolas Ritschel Foundation
summary: Nik's Wish, UA supports the Nikolas Ritschel Foundation's mission to provide hope, strength and joy to 18–24-year-olds battling cancer by granting their most cherished wishes.

name: The Verb Kind in Tuscaloosa
description: This organization works with the Verb Kind, a nonprofit organization that supports and mentors incarcerated youth. The Tuscaloosa branch for The Verb Kind operates in conjunction with, and at the direction of, The Verb Kind officers and directors to provide mentorship to incarcerated youth at the Tuscaloosa Juvenile Detention Center. The purpose of this association is to promote an interest in and raise awareness of the needs of and opportunities within the Tuscaloosa branch for The Verb Kind, in accordance with the overall mission of The Verb Kind, Inc., a Florida not-for-profit corporation (“The Verb Kind”).
summary: We are an organization geared towards raising awareness and providing opportunities for incarcerated youth in Tuscaloosa, AL., in accordance with The Verb Kind Inc.'s Nonprofit's national mission.

name: Black Aspiring Scholars in Education & Exercise Science
description: The purpose of B.A.S.E. is to advocate for a fair and inclusive educational experience with an emphasis on African American students' growth and development through the Capstone. B.A.S.E. seeks to reach all students and build community within the College of Education while focusing on bringing together Black and minority students in the college. We seek to do so by creating networking opportunities between individuals and groups in order to express common viewpoints, creating opportunities for mentoring and the sharing of collegiate resources, and allow students to build peer groups among like-minded individuals. Membership in this organization shall consist of currently enrolled students in or affiliated with the College of Education at the University of Alabama.
summary: The purpose of B.A.S.E. is to advocate for a fair and inclusive educational experience with an emphasis on African American and minority students' growth and development through the Capstone.

name: Weeks of Welcome
description:
Coordinated by University Programs within the Division of Student Life, Weeks of Welcome is the official welcome back to campus for new and returning students. Eight weeks of programs, events, and initiatives aimed at helping all UA students build community, find resources, and get connected at the Capstone. With more than 100 events annually, there is something for everyone. 

 

To keep students engaged and support their long-term success, we are expanding our Weeks of Welcome (WOW) programming to eight full weeks. While our signature events will continue to take place during the first two weeks, this extended format provides increased visibility, sustained opportunities for connection, and a broader range of experiences tailored to the diverse interests of our student body. Our retention data consistently shows that students who participate in at least one WOW event are significantly more likely to persist beyond their first semester. By creating touchpoints throughout the early weeks of the academic year, we aim to build community, foster a strong sense of belonging, and enhance student engagement—critical factors in student success and retention.
summary: UA’s Weeks of Welcome (WOW) is a large series of programs coordinated by University Programs and the Division of Student Life to help all students find resources, build community and have fun.

name: OPTICA at The University of Alabama
description: We will be conducting and discussing optical research on behalf of the University of Alabama department of physics and astronomy and OPTICA.
summary: Optica, Advancing Optics and Photonics Worldwide, is the society dedicated to promoting the generation, application, archiving and dissemination of knowledge in the field.

name: Nach Basanti
description:
Nach Basanti is an inclusive Indian fusion dance group open to everyone, regardless of prior dance
experience. It promotes popular dance forms from the Indian subcontinent and fosters a sense of community among people of Indian descent, as dance is an integral part of Indian culture and a widely shared form of artistic expression and enjoyment.

Nach Basanti offers a platform for individuals to learn and explore diverse dance forms from India and perform at various events on and off campus, including celebrations like Diwali and holi.
summary: Nach Basanti is an inclusive Indian fusion dance group that promotes popular dance forms from the Indian subcontinent, as dance is an integral part of Indian culture.

name: Blue Beacon Foundation
summary: The Blue Beacon Foundation is a student-led organization dedicated to combating human trafficking through awareness, advocacy, and action. Our mission is to educate students, support survivors, and push for stronger legal measures to fight trafficking.

name: The Crafting Corner
description: The Crafting Corner welcomes all students who have a craft that they enjoy and would like to share with others. We have members who do pottery, yarn crafts, needlework, and a variety of other crafts.
summary: The Crafting Corner is a crafting club welcome to everyone with a passion for a craft from crocheting to glasswork.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law & Government Fraternity - Alpha Tau Chapter
description: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Gender Inclusive Professional Fraternity exists nationally to foster a gender-inclusive brotherhood for undergraduate students interested in law and government. Our brotherhood is open to all undergraduate focuses of study. We are devoted to developing students professionally and academically to be leaders in the world that follows graduation.
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi exists for undergraduate students interested in law and government and is open to all undergraduate focuses of study. We are devoted to developing students professionally and academically to be leaders in the world in law and government.

name: Girls Who Code College Loop
description:
Girls Who Code GWC is an international nonprofit organization working to close the gender gap in technology, and leading the movement to inspire, educate and equip young women with the computing skills needed to pursue 21st-century opportunities. Since launching in 2012, Girls Who Code has reached 500 million people through its work and 300,000 girls through its in-person programming. College-aged alumni of Girls Who Code are declaring majors in computer science and related fields at 15 times the U.S. average. In 2019, the organization was named the #1 Most Innovative Non-Profit on Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies list.


College Loops are about more than programming, they're on-campus organizations offering monthly challenges focusing on honing computer science skills and engaging women to take action, collaborate, and create with one another. The GWC Bama Loop focuses on engaging with the community to put on coding workshops throughout the school year.

Today, our cohort of college-aged alumni is made up of 80,000 young women-double the number of those who graduated with computer science degrees in 2017. The sisterhood is strong. Our alumni support one another and other women in computer science to succeed in the field. Girls Who Code college-aged alumni are choosing to major in computer science or related fields at 15x this national average.
summary: The Girls Who Code College Loop at The University of Alabama hosts coding events for young students in the area and supports members with workshops and career-building opportunities to grow as confident, creative leaders in technology.

name: Tide and Tables
description: Tide & Tables is a new UA organization that explores all of the many restaurants Tuscaloosa has to offer. Every two weeks, members visit a new spot and write detailed reviews on the food, drinks, and overall experience. These reviews contribute to a student-made restaurant encyclopedia for the benefit of UA students, residents, athletes, and visitors. Our mission is to promote Tuscaloosa businesses, to establish a dynamic and engaging network, and to create a platform for leadership and service in the Tuscaloosa area.
summary: Tide & Tables is a new UA org exploring Tuscaloosa’s restaurant scene. Every two weeks, members review local spots, covering food, drinks & experience. Their reviews build a student-made restaurant encyclopedia for the UA and Tuscaloosa community.

name: Pakistani Student Association
description:
The Pakistani Student Association (PSA) at The University of Alabama is a student-led organization committed to celebrating and sharing the rich culture, traditions, and values of Pakistan with the campus community. Our mission is to provide a welcoming and inclusive space for Pakistani students while fostering cross-cultural understanding and engagement among students of all backgrounds. PSA serves as a supportive home away from home, where members can connect, collaborate, and take pride in their heritage.

Through a wide range of cultural events, educational workshops, and social gatherings, PSA introduces the vibrant diversity of Pakistan to the broader UA community. From traditional festivals and music nights to guest speakers and movie screenings, our events aim to foster dialogue, friendship, and mutual respect. We also support the academic and social transition of new Pakistani students through mentorship and community-building activities.

Membership in PSA is open to all students, faculty, and staff at UA - regardless of cultural background - who are interested in Pakistani culture or want to support the organization’s mission. By building bridges across communities, PSA hopes to enrich the multicultural environment of the university and create lasting connections that go beyond borders. Whether you're looking to celebrate your roots or explore a new culture, PSA welcomes you to be part of a dynamic and inclusive community.
summary: The Pakistani Student Association at the UA provides a welcoming space for Pakistani students, sharing cultural traditions and promoting unity. Through events and gatherings, PSA fosters support, friendship, and cultural exchange within the UA community.

name: Capstone Project Management Society
description:
The Capstone Project Management Society (CPMS) at The University of Alabama is a student-led organization dedicated to equipping students with real-world skills, industry knowledge, and professional connections in the field of project management. Open to all undergraduate students, CPMS provides a collaborative platform for members to build foundational project management competencies, pursue industry-recognized certifications, and engage in hands-on workshops, professional networking events, and leadership development.

CPMS fosters career readiness by preparing members for internships and full-time roles in project-based industries, while also supporting personal growth in leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Through a structured leadership team and regular programming—including certification workshops and professional development events—CPMS creates a vibrant community focused on developing future project leaders.
summary: The Capstone Project Management Society (CPMS) at The University of Alabama is a student-led organization dedicated to equipping students with real-world skills, industry knowledge, and professional connections in the field of project management.

name: Pre-CAA Student Association
description:
The UA Pre-CAA Student Association is a student-run club at UA for students who are pursuing careers as certified anesthesiologist assistants (Pre-CAA). Anesthesiologist assistants are medical professionals who work alongside anesthesiologists to administer anesthesia and monitor patients during surgery. Think of a CAA as a more specialized type of PA (physician assistant). The field is relatively new - only about twenty graduate programs exist in the US - but rapidly growing. CAA programs are attractive to many pre-health students because they allow a high level of specialization and can be completed in 2-3 years (a much shorter time frame than medical school and residency). 

Our club’s mission is to promote community and professional development for students interested in the CAA field. Our goal is to prepare pre-CAA students to be successful in their applications to CAA programs and beyond. We offer monthly meetings where we share information about the CAA profession, graduate programs, and the application process. We plan to include professional development opportunities (resume and CV workshops) by partnering with Pre-Health advising. It is also our goal to develop connections with local anesthesia providers to assist student members in getting the shadowing hours required to apply to graduate programs. 

To be eligible for membership, students must be undergraduate or graduate students interested in pursuing a career in the anesthesiologist assistant field. To maintain member status, they must attend at least three organization events per semester (including at least two monthly meetings) and demonstrate a continued interest in anesthesiology or the CAA field. 

Follow us on Instagram (@ua_pre_caa) for the most current updates!
summary: Promoting community and professional development for UA students interested in pursuing a career in the Certified Anesthesiologist Assistant (CAA) field.

name: Women in Criminal Justice and Criminology
description: Women in Criminal Justice and Criminology (WCJC) is a student-led organization at the University of Alabama dedicated to supporting students pursuing academic and professional careers in criminal justice, criminology, and related fields. WCJC fosters an inclusive and empowering environment where members can build meaningful connections, grow their leadership skills, and engage with key issues in the justice system. Through networking events, guest speakers, workshops, mentorship opportunities, and community service initiatives, WCJC equips members with the tools and experiences needed to thrive in both academic and professional spaces. Whether you’re planning a future in law enforcement, legal advocacy, forensic science, or public policy, WCJC offers a platform to learn, lead, and make an impact.
summary: Women in Criminal Justice and Criminology (WCJC) at the University of Alabama is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering, educating, and supporting individuals pursuing careers in the fields of criminal justice and criminology.

name: Crimson Trading Group
description:
Crimson Trading Group is a student-led organization at The University of Alabama focused on helping students develop real-world trading skills in a collaborative environment. Open to all experience levels, we explore stocks, forex, options, futures, and commodities with a strong emphasis on technical analysis, a key skill for anyone from different academic backgrounds and pursuing degree at UA

We meet at least once monthly to discuss market trends, economic policy, and trading strategies, while also staying active online to share insights, news, and resources. With guest speakers, networking opportunities, and a supportive community, this is the perfect space to grow your knowledge, connect with like-minded peers, and gain a competitive edge in the financial world. To learn more about us and join us, please click the link below, which will directly connect to our website and Instagram.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to educate, guide, and build a responsible trading community. Our club is dedicated to financial markets trading, including stocks, forex, commodities, and indices.

name: St. Philip's Anglican Student Fellowship
description: The purpose of St Philip’s Anglican Student Fellowship shall be to call many to true and lively faith in the gospel of Jesus Christ through the practice of the Anglican way. In pursuit of this purpose, St Philip’s Anglican Student Fellowship will host prayer meetings and/or other worship gatherings consistent with the Christian Faith and offer educational opportunities to learn about the Christian faith and the Anglican Tradition.
summary: The purpose of St Philip’s Anglican Student Fellowship shall be to call many to true and lively faith in the gospel of Jesus Christ through the practice of the Anglican way.

name: Admissions Ambassadors
description: The Admissions Ambassadors (AA) serve as a student recruitment organization within The University of Alabama’s Office of Undergraduate Admissions & Scholarships. AA offers insight and provides guidance to prospective students, their parents, and/or family and friends about information regarding campus life, student involvement, and the enrollment process. They provide this insight by hosting touring families for lunch in a dining hall and by assisting on-campus admissions events.
summary: The Admissions Ambassadors (AA) serve as a student recruitment organization within The University of Alabama’s Office of Undergraduate Admissions. AA offers insight and provides guidance to prospective students & families about UA.

name: Association for Women Geoscientists at the University of Alabama
description:
Our Chapter works to:

- host social events that provide a supportive environment where members can discuss the challenges women face in geoscience careers.

- raise awareness and advocate for the resolution of systemic inequalities that negatively impact women and girls in geosciences.

- gather a membership with diverse geoscience experiences and interests to provide networking and mentoring opportunities.

- communicate academic opportunities and career resources to women currently pursuing geosciences and support the advancement of women and girls who are considering a geoscience career.

- partner with other student organizations to further the goals above.
summary: AWG at UA aims to enhance the quality and level of participation of women in geosciences and to encourage girls and young women (including cis-gender, transgender, gender fluid and non-binary persons) to explore geoscience careers.

name: Students for Life at UA
description:
Students for Life at UA engages in the following:

  1. Advocate for the rights of unborn children and vulnerable individuals by promoting life-affirming alternatives to abortion, euthanasia, and other life-ending practices.
  2. Educate the community on issues related to the sanctity of life, fostering an understanding of the moral, legal, and social implications of life-based ethics.
  3. Provide resources and support to individuals, families, and communities facing life-related decisions, particularly around unplanned pregnancies, prenatal care, and end-of-life care.
  4. Inspire and mobilize individuals to join in peaceful, lawful efforts to build a culture that respects and protects every human life.
summary: Students for Life promotes life-affirming alternatives to abortion and other life-ending practices, educates on life ethics, supports those facing life decisions, and inspires peaceful action to protect the dignity and rights of all human life.

name: Students for Justice in Palestine at the University of Alabama
description: The purpose of this organization is to engage in advocacy and educational efforts on behalf of Palestinian human rights, as well as the principle of anti-militarism. Its goal is to organize events related to Palestinian sovereignty and inform the student body about the consequences of U.S. involvement in foreign conflicts.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to engage in advocacy and educational efforts on behalf of Palestinian human rights, as well as the principles of anti-militarism.

name: The Immunology Club
summary: A club dedicated to providing students at the University of Alabama with faculty and graduate student led research presentations about topics in immunology.

name: Alabama SCUBA Club
summary: This organization is for people who are SCUBA certified. It is all recreational. Members of the club can plan fun dive trips and meet new people with similar interests.

name: Central Asian Student Association
description:
The Central Asian Student Association (CASA) is dedicated to representing the people, values, and cultures of Central Asian countries at the University of Alabama. Our mission is to share the rich diversity of Central Asian cultures—each with its own unique heritage yet connected by common traditions—with the broader UA community. Through cultural events, educational initiatives, and community gatherings, we aim to:

  • Promote awareness of Central Asian food, history, traditions, and values

  • Enrich the multicultural landscape of the University of Alabama

  • Foster meaningful dialogue around the distinct historical and cultural contributions of the region

  • Build a sense of community and shared purpose among students that are interested in the region

Membership is open to all students—whether you’re from Central Asia or simply curious to learn more.
summary: An organization with the purpose to promote an interest in the culture and history of Central Asian countries and ethnicities.

name: Alabama Diplomacy Club
description: The Alabama Diplomacy Club will be dedicated to playing the board game Diplomacy, its many variants, and other similar games. We plan to have approximately 8-hour monthly meetings where we will run games of Diplomacy for the members of the club and potentially host tournaments. We may also host online longform games through the websites Backstabbr, WebDiplomacy, or VDiplomacy that would update every few days. Our group chat will be hosted on Discord, though we may have some temporary interest group chats on GroupMe or other similar programs.
summary: The Alabama Diplomacy Club is club all about the board game Diplomacy! We host in-person and online games and play variants and other similar games. We hope to grow the Diplomacy hobby community and spread a love of the game to UA and Tuscaloosa.

name: Farmhand
summary: Farmhand is dedicated to supporting small farms and farmers within Alabama and the surrounding region. We aim to provide educational experiences for students to learn about our agri-food systems and promote local food consumption.

name: Outgoing Hearts
summary: Non-profit service focused on communities and youth.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description: Students Supporting Israel is an independent, nonpartisan, grassroots campus movement that was created BY students, FOR students, in order to organize a strong and united pro-Israel front on college campuses. We are made up of students from different political, religious, and ethnic backgrounds, all working together as Students Supporting Israel. We believe in grassroots action, and want to empower students who support Israel on campus to lead their fellow students and the campus community. ​​
summary: Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro -Israel voice on collage campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy.

name: Venture Capital Hub
description: The Venture Capital Hub is an environment where students learn about venture capital practices, develop professional networks, and gain practical experience. Members will gain a comprehensive understanding of venture capital from founding and funding a startup to how to evaluate and invest in opportunities.
summary: The Venture Capital Hub is an environment that gives students practical experience when it comes to all things venture capital, including investor decision-making and startup dynamics.

name: BLissful GLare
description: We are a club of BL (boys love) and GL (girls love) fans who love to gather and chat about all things related to these genres (like BL/GL dramas, comics, and anime). We meet on a biweekly basis at Barnes and Noble to discuss BL/GL as a group.
summary: We are a club of BL  (boys love) and GL (girls love) fans who love to gather and chat about all things related to these genres (like BL/GL dramas, comics, and anime). We meet on a biweekly basis at Barnes and Noble to discuss BL/GL as a group.

name: Alabama Club Dodgeball
description:
Alabama Club Dodgeball is a traveling, intercollegiate dodgeball team which plays under the National Collegiate Dodgeball Association (NCDA). ACD is co-ed and aims to grow the sport of dodgeball at the collegiate level.

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/108509743/rDosLaPY
summary: Alabama Club Dodgeball is a traveling, intercollegiate dodgeball team.

name: Letters of Love UA
summary: Create and Distribute support cards for pediatric patients in hospitals across the country

name: Institute for Social Science Research
description:
MISSION

ISSR is committed to conducting, supporting, and facilitating ambitious, practical, and data-driven social science research that informs policy, drives evidence-based decisions, and advances scientific knowledge, particularly in addressing critical societal challenges.

VISION

ISSR aspires to be a premier center for high-impact social science research, serving as both a research hub and a trusted collaborative partner for interdisciplinary innovation. Our purpose is to create healthier, safer, and more accessible communities.

VALUES

ISSR is driven by curiosity, rigor, and the pursuit of knowledge. We set high standards and strive for transformative impact. We prioritize research over personal recognition and foster teamwork. We integrate multiple disciplines to drive discovery. We recognize the human impact of our research.
summary: The Institute for Social Science Research (ISSR) at The University of Alabama is a hub for interdisciplinary research, collaboration, and innovation in the social sciences.

name: Alabama Engineering Initiative
description: The Alabama Engineering Initiative (AEI) is a student-led organization at The University of Alabama that partners with universities and humanitarian groups to design sustainable engineering solutions and develop community-focused engineers.
summary: The Alabama Engineering Initiative (AEI) is a student-led organization at The University of Alabama that partners with universities and humanitarian groups to design sustainable engineering solutions and develop community-focused engineers.

name: Women in Public Health Society
description: We are a sisterhood for women in public health, dedicated to service, advocacy, and connection. Through mentorship, networking, and community outreach, we empower women to grow as leaders and prepare for success in school, careers, and beyond.
summary: Our mission is to build a strong sisterhood for women in public health. Through service, advocacy, mentorship, and networking, we empower women to grow, lead, and make an impact while gaining the skills and confidence needed for school and careers.

name: Holle Center for Communication Arts
description: The Holle Center for Communication Arts is an incubator and a creative collaboratory designed to shape the future of 21st-century nonfiction storytelling. The center convenes researchers, practitioners, community partners and student interns to foster innovative, arts-informed research and narrative projects that are crucial to advancing the notion that everyone has a story to tell and a story worth telling. The center’s work is to tell meaningful stories that reflect the world as it is, and as it can be.
summary: The Holle Center for Communication Arts is an incubator and a creative collaboratory designed to shape the future of 21st-century nonfiction storytelling.

name: AV Dance Collective
summary: Dance organization that focuses on driven dancers on a professional path

name: Office of Disability Services
description: In collaboration with the University community, the Office of Disability Services (ODS) is committed to ensuring educational access that enhances the lives of students with disabilities. Our work enriches the campus environment by supporting the institution in its collective responsibility to provide an accessible education.
summary: In collaboration with the University community, the Office of Disability Services (ODS) is committed to ensuring educational access that enhances the lives of students with disabilities.

name: Girls Who Lead (UA Chapter)
summary: Girls Who Lead (UA Chapter) is an organization dedicated to pushing college women into policy, advocacy, and entrepreneurship, all while closing the opportunity gap to help girls rise into positions of leadership at the University of Alabama and beyond!

name: 57 Miles
description: 57 Miles exists to cultivate reciprocal relationships between students at The University of Alabama and the communities of Alabama’s Black Belt through intentional service-learning, participatory action, and long-term partnership. Through semesterly Days of Service and continual community development projects in the areas of healthcare, education, economic development, and history and culture, we create opportunities for students to engage directly with communities in the Black Belt of Alabama.
summary: 57 Miles aims to build sustainable relationships between The University of Alabama and the community of Marion, Alabama, through service, education, and engagement.

name: Canine Companions Collar Scholars at UA
summary: Canine Companions Collar Scholars focuses on advocating for and educating about the importance of service dogs through fundraising, tabling, and raising service dogs.

name: Ripping Tide Drone Racing Team
description: The purpose of Ripping Tide is to give students a chance to gain hands-on experience with engineering, piloting, and drone racing. All students regardless of experience with a desire to learn or practice drone racing will be allowed. Drone racing is an emerging field which tests the limits of aerial technology and pilot skill. This organization will give members a dedicated community to gain valuable experience in this sector. We will practice efficient drone racing techniques and compete as a members in the national Collegiate Drone Racing Championship (CDRC), the nation's premier annual college drone racing competition.
summary: The purpose of Ripping Tide is to give students a chance to gain hands-on experience with engineering, piloting, and drone racing. We compete as a members in the national Collegiate Drone Racing Championship to test our skills again the best pilots.

name: Unite for Reproductive & Gender Equity
summary: URGE envisions a liberated world where we can live with justice, love freely, express our gender and sexuality, and define and create families of our choosing.

name: Crimson Sword Fighting
description: University of Alabama's premier Historical European Martial Arts club (HEMA), our purpose is to give students the opportunity to study historical martial techniques developed by European sword masters in the Middle Ages and Renaissance.
summary: Our purpose is to give students the opportunity to study historical martial techniques developed by European sword masters in the Middle Ages.

name: Redeemer's Campus Fellowship
description:
As a Christian student organization, Redeemer’s Campus Fellowship (RCF) at The University of Alabama is committed to preaching the gospel of Jesus Christ, nurturing students' spiritual growth, and equipping them to become excellent leaders in their fields.

RCF is deeply invested in the academic success of its members (both domestic and international students), encouraging diligence, excellence, and integrity in their studies. We are equally committed to the holistic well-being of our members, supporting their spiritual, emotional, and personal development.

Through both the teaching and lived experience of the gospel, we are committed to building an inclusive community of believers who grow together in faith, pursue academic excellence, and support one another in their personal, spiritual, and professional journeys.
summary: The official name of this organization shall be "The Redeemer’s Campus Fellowship (RCF).

name: Black Health Awakening
summary: Teaching the community to be more self-aware about our health and wellness through education, events, and preventive care.

name: Fueling Opportunities, Resources, Growth, and Exploration
summary: FORGE is a student-led organization committed to empowering individuals by providing the resources, guidance, and opportunities needed to thrive academically, grow personally, become leaders in the community, and explore meaningful career paths.

name: Blossoms at Bama
summary: Blossoms at Bama's mission is to foster a defined sense of community and belonging amongst student members and adolescents by building their confidence in leadership skills through meaningful mentorship. We aim to further develop leadership

name: Reformed University Fellowship - International
summary: This organization is a ministry of the Presbyterian Church in America (PCA) to the international community at the University of Alabama, to create a multicultural community that seeks to live out the implications of the Christian faith on the campus.

name: Bitcoin Alabama
description: The purpose of the Alabama Bitcoin Club is to contribute to fostering a global network of Bitcoin students, promoting education and collaboration and connecting students with opportunities and resources within the Bitcoin ecosystem. We hold bi-weekly educational meetings, learn from industry experienced guest speakers, and provide access to learning resources and Bitcoin industry opportunities.
summary: Educational and Social club discussing bitcoin.

name: MEDLIFE
description: The purpose of the Students for MEDLIFE at The University of Alabama is to support and extend MEDLIFE's mission and all MEDLIFE’s divisions (including Smiles Movement, Safe Homes Movement, and Engaged Education) within The University of Alabama community by focusing on providing healthcare, education, and safe housing to low-income families. The Chapter aims to transform volunteering and community engagement through a collaborative and empowering approach, emphasizing listening, analyzing root causes, and educational exchange. The organization is dedicated to raising awareness, fundraising, and mobilizing students for service, fostering an inclusive, action-oriented campus culture, and contributing to a vibrant, engaged, and socially responsible student body.
summary: The purpose of the Students for MEDLIFE at The University of Alabama is to support and extend MEDLIFE's mission within The University of Alabama community by focusing on providing healthcare, education, and safe housing to low-income families.

name: Built by Bama General Education Ambassadors
description: The purpose of this organization is to inform current and prospective students of the academic initiatives offered in the undergraduate general education curriculum. Members serve in capacity to represent the Office of Academic Affairs as student ambassadors.
summary: The Built by Bama General Education Ambassadors are a student collective founded in the Fall of 2025 in coalition with the Office of Academic Affairs. They serve to inform current and prospective students about the new General Education curriculum.

name: Office of Undergraduate Research
summary: The Office for Undergraduate Research is dedicated to enriching the undergraduate experience through hands-on research opportunities.

name: Student Tide Against D.U.I
summary: My organization is dedicated to raising awareness about the dangers of drunk driving and promoting safer ways for students to get home. We aim to create a designated driver program at UA events, support MADD through fundraising, and work with campus orgs

name: American Constitution Society at the University of Alabama
summary: UA Law ACS is Alabama Law's chapter of the nation's leading progressive legal organization, dedicated to training and cultivating the next generation of progressive lawyers.

name: Data Science Club
description: The purpose of this association is to develop career readiness in data science for students in the data science major and related majors, illuminating career paths while enhancing educational opportunities for our members. We meet every other week to share updates and introduce new opportunities, and hope to bring in employers and develop projects to help members improve their data science skills while helping the local community.
summary: The purpose of this association is to develop career readiness in data science for students in the data science major and related majors, illuminating career paths while enhancing educational opportunities for our members.

name: Crimson Saxophone Organization
summary: The mission of the Crimson Saxophone Organization is to promote the art of saxophone playing at the University of Alabama.

name: Dream on 3
description: Dream on 3’s Collegiate Dream Team at the University of Alabama is one of 38 collegiate programs whose mission is to help those with life-altering conditions live out their sports dreams. In collaboration with Alabama Athletics, the mission of Alabama Dream on 3 is to bring to life a member of the Alabama community’s sports dream. Working closely with the Exceptional Foundation, located in Birmingham, Alabama, to select an individual ages 5-21 with life-altering conditions, this child is treated as an MVP during a 3-day eventful weekend, along with various events spread out the year, including the Dream Reveal, which we inform the selected child that they will become our “Dream Kid,” along with events to get to know more about them. Working closely with Dream on 3 Nationals and with our selected team, we will fundraise a minimum of $10,000 to provide an all-inclusive weekend for the “Dream Kid” and their family, hosting events centered around their interests and selected sport. Depending on what Alabama Athletics can provide per sport, the “Dream Kid” will be provided a memorable experience with their favorite team. Additional local and/or corporate sponsorships are involved in the Dream Weekend through the donation of meals, events, or various gifts to celebrate the “Dream Kid” while they stay in Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
summary: Dream on 3 is a non-profit dedicated to granting sports dreams to children and teens with life altering conditions. We work closely with the exceptional foundation where our dream kid is selected and given a 3 day vip experience in Tuscaloosa.

name: GlamourGals
description: GlamourGals is funded by the national organization, and offers a multitude of awards and scholarships to their members. The goal is to create intergenerational relationships and learn from one another in a positive way.
summary: GlamourGals works within the community to attempt to end senior isolation. This is done through painting nails, doing makeovers, games, crafts, and more!

name: Black with a Small Business
summary: Foster relationships with students at the University of Alabama to build a community of present and future entrepreneurs.

name: The Open-source Projects and Security Lab
description: The goal of The OPS Lab is to encourage students to pursue projects in open-source software for developing skills in cybersecurity and networking. Free and Open-Source Software (FOSS) is pivotal to the computer science field and holds up the industry. This organization will introduce students to FOSS, why they should use FOSS, and how it can benefit their careers. To do so, club projects will focus on many different concepts such as Linux/Unix system management, app development, and homelabbing. The mission is to give students a place where they can gain hands-on, marketable experience in cybersecurity and networking as well as provide a place for community and learning among Computer Science and Cybersecurity students.
summary: The goal of The OPS Lab is to encourage students to pursue collaborative projects in Computer Science and Cyber Security to develop real-world, marketable skills.

name: LEAD Ambassadors
description:
The purpose of the UA Business LEAD Ambassadors is to promote professional development, student engagement, and career readiness among students in the Culverhouse College of Business. The organization serves as a representative body for the UA Business LEAD program, fostering leadership, inclusivity, and professionalism.

In pursuit of its purpose, the organization shall:

1. Host professional development events, workshops, and networking opportunities.

2. Mentor students and provide guidance on career readiness, academic affairs, and other professional areas.

3. Represent the LEAD Program at university and community events.

4. Foster a culture of leadership, teamwork, and ethical reasoning among members.

 

https://culverhouse.ua.edu/lead/ambassadors/
summary: The purpose of the UA Business LEAD Ambassadors is to promote professional development, student engagement, and career readiness among students in the Culverhouse College of Business.

name: Hispanic Latino Pre Health Organization
summary: The purpose of the Hispanic Latino Pre-Health Organization (HLPHO) is to empower, support, and equip Hispanic and Latino students pursuing careers in health-related fields.

name: Girls Talk:Sisters in Christ
summary: An sisterhood that an safe space for any girl on campus that wants to have a community filled with love, support, and joy.

name: Brother2Brother
summary: Brother2Brother is an org thats uplifts black men through mental and physical health.

name: Tide Sports Analytics
description:
Tide Sports Analytics is a student-led organization at The University of Alabama dedicated to exploring the intersection of data science, statistics, and athletics. Our mission is to give students hands-on experience applying analytical methods to real sports data while deepening their understanding of how data shapes modern sports decisions.

 

Members collaborate on projects that analyze team and player performance, build predictive models, and create interactive visualizations across collegiate and professional sports. We also host workshops, speaker events, and competitions to help students develop technical skills in programming, data analysis, and sports research.

 

Whether you’re a data enthusiast, an athlete curious about analytics, or simply a sports fan eager to learn how numbers drive results, Tide Sports Analytics offers a space to learn, collaborate, and turn passion into real-world insights.
summary: Using data-driven analysis to explore the strategies, trends, and performances that define collegiate and professional athletics.

name: Architectural Engineering Institute
summary: The Architectural Engineering Institute (AEI) is the official organization for students studying Architectural Engineering, who plan to gain experience with working with both architects and civil engineers in their respective career fields.

name: Once Upon A Room
summary: Once Upon A Room at The University of Alabama is a student organization that decorates hospital rooms and donates toys to pediatric patients, bringing comfort, joy, and hope to children and families while promoting compassion and community service.

name: The Marathon Club
